title: Miso Chilli Roasted Tofu, Broccoli & Cashews (No Onion/Garlic)
description: Easy sheet-pan dinner: tofu, broccoli and cashews roasted in an umami-rich sesame, ginger and miso marinade. Adapted egg-free vegan style without onion or garlic; hing stands in for allium depth. Serve over noodles, steamed vegetables or a crunchy cabbage salad.
ingredients: 400 g firm tofu, pressed and cubed | 1 large head broccoli, cut into florets | 75 g raw cashews | 2 tablespoons white or chickpea miso paste | 2 tablespoons tamari or soy sauce | 1 tablespoon toasted sesame oil | 1 tablespoon neutral oil | 1 tablespoon maple syrup | 1 tablespoon rice vinegar | 1 tablespoon grated fresh ginger | 1-2 teaspoons chilli flakes or chilli paste, to taste | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 tablespoon sesame seeds (optional) | Fresh mint or coriander leaves, to finish (optional)
method: 1. Heat the oven to 200°C / 400°F. Line a large baking tray. | 2. Warm the neutral oil briefly, bloom the hing in it for a few seconds, then whisk with miso, tamari, sesame oil, maple syrup, rice vinegar, ginger and chilli until smooth. | 3. Toss the tofu cubes in half the marinade and spread on the tray. | 4. Toss broccoli and cashews with the remaining marinade and arrange around the tofu in a single layer. | 5. Roast 25-30 minutes, turning once, until the tofu is golden at the edges and the broccoli is tender with charred tips. | 6. Scatter sesame seeds and herbs if using, and serve hot.
adaptations: Reconstructed from Botanical Kitchen public recipe description (member-only full amounts were not publicly readable). Kept vegan and egg-free; no onion or garlic; hing (asafoetida) added for allium-free savoury depth.

title: Aam Papad Challi
description: A zesty fusion of tangy aam papad and crunchy nuts, delivering a burst of sweet and sour flavors in every delightful bite.
ingredients: Few pieces regular aam papad Few pieces kala aam papad | 1 corn on cob Oil for drizzling Butter for brushing | 1 teaspoon chaat masala + for sprinkling | 2 tablespoons lemon juice
method: 1. Peel corn on cob and roast directly on high flame. | 2. Heat a non-stick grill pan. | 3. Cut roasted corn on cob into 3 portions, halve and place on the hot pan. | 4. Drizzle some oil and toss well. | 5. Brush the corn pieces with some butter, add red chilli powder, black salt, chaat masala and lemon juice. | 6. Top with regular and kala aam papad pieces and cook till soft. | 7. Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle chaat masala and serve immediately.

title: Almond Milk Smoothie
description: Enriched with almonds and garnished with sabza and blueberries, this smoothie is a power house of essential nutrients.
ingredients: 1 cup almonds, soaked overnight and drained | 2 medium ripe banana | 1 cup fresh blueberries + for garnish | 1 teaspoon vanilla essence | 3 tablespoons honey Fresh mint sprigs for garnish | 2 tbsps basil seeds (sabza), soaked
method: 1. Put soaked almonds in a grinder jar, add 2 cups warm water and grind till mixture is smooth. | 2. Line a strainer kept on a bowl with muslin cloth, pour the ground almond mixture into it and strain the almond milk into the bowl. | 3. Grind the residue again with 1 cup warm water to get more almond milk and strain this into the same bowl. | 4. Peel the bananas, break into half and put in the grinder jar. | 5. Add blueberries, vanilla essence, honey, almond milk and grind to a smooth mixture. | 6. Skew blueberries and a mint sprig on each skewer. | 7. Put some basil seeds in each serving glass and pour the smoothie over them. | 8. Place one skewer on each glass and serve immediately.

title: Almond Phirni
description: This recipe is specially for those who are intolerant to lactose, so almond milk is used instead of milk
ingredients: 3 cups Almond milk | 4 tablespoons Rice, soaked for | 0 minutes and drained a large pi to garnish Saffron 1/ | 2 teaspoon Green cardamom powder 3/ | 4 cup Jaggery, chopped 1/ | 2 tablespoon Rose water to garnish Blanched pistachios, slivered
method: 1. Heat almond milk in a deep non-stick pan and let it come to a boil. | 2. Reduce heat and let it simmer for 8-10 minutes. | 3. Put rice in a blender jar, add ¼ cup water and blend to a coarse paste. | 4. Add this paste to almond milk and mix well. | 5. Add a large pinch of saffron and mix well and cook till the rice is cooked and the mixture thickens. | 6. Add green cardamom powder and mix well. | 7. Cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly. | 8. Add jaggery and mix well. | 9. Cook till the jaggery is completely dissolved. | 10. Add rose water and mix well. | 11. Take the pan off the heat. | 12. Pour phirni into individual earthen bowls, sprinkle pistachio slivers on top and garnish with a few strands of saffron and serve chilled.

title: Aloo Muri
description: Satisfy your snack cravings with 'Aloo Muri', a beloved street-side delight from Meghalaya. This irresistible recipe features crispy puffed rice, spiced mashed potatoes, a special homemade masala...
ingredients: 3 medium potatoes (aloo), boiled and peeled | 4 cups puffed rice (muri) | 2 tablespoons coriander seeds | 1 tablespoon cumin seeds | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 green chillies, chopped | 3 tablespoons mustard oil 2- | 3 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves + to sprinkle ¼ cup aloo bhujiya + to sprinkle ½ lemon
method: 1. Heat a nonstick pan. | 2. Add coriander seeds, cumin seeds and sauté for 2-3 minutes, then add a pinch of hing. | 3. Transfer in a mortar and crush to fine powder with a mortar. | 4. Take a bowl, cut the potatoes into medium sized pieces and add to the bowl. | 5. Add green chillies, puffed rice, 2-3 tbsps ground spice mix, tamarind water, mustard oil and mix well. | 6. Add coriander and aloo bhujiya and mix well. | 7. Squeeze lemon and mix. | 8. Transfer in serving bowls, sprinkle coriander, aloo bhujiya and serve immediately.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Anjeer and Boondi Pancakes
description: A delectable fusion of traditional Indian flavors, featuring sweet figs and crunchy boondi, wrapped in a fluffy pancake for a delightful breakfast treat.
ingredients: 0 dried figs, soaked and chopped ¼ cup sweet boondi | 2 cups refined flour (maida) ½ teaspoon baking soda ¼ teaspoon vanilla essence 1¾ cups milk | 2 tablespoons chopped jaggery 15- | 0 walnuts Butter for shallow frying | 8 whole dried figs, soaked Rabri for garnish
method: 1. Take refined flour in a bowl, add baking soda and vanilla essence. | 2. Heat 1½ cups milk in a deep non-stick pan stirring continuously till the milk comes to a boil. | 3. Add jaggery, switch off heat and mix well till jaggery melts. | 4. Pour the milk mixture into the refined flour mixture. | 5. Add chopped figs, boondi and remaining milk. | 6. Add little water and mix well to make a medium thick and smooth batter. | 7. Chop and add walnuts and mix well. | 8. Set aside to rest for 10 minutes. | 9. Heat butter in a non-stick tawa, pour a ladle of batter and spread into a medium sized pancake. | 10. Place a whole fig in centre of and cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes, turnover and cook on other side till golden brown on both sides. | 11. Transfer onto a serving plate and garnish with rabri. | 12. Serve immediately.

title: Anjeer aur Khajur Milkshake
description: Healthy and delicious Milkshake made using Anjeer aur Khajur is absolutely easy to make. Especially during hot summer, a glass full of this milkshake gives an instant boost of energy.
ingredients: 10- | 5 dried figs 10- | 5 dates | 2 cups milk 2- | 3 tablespoons honey Fusion Omega Boost Trail Mix to sprinkle
method: 1. Take Zahidi Whole Natural Dates in a bowl and deseed. | 2. Add sufficient water and soak for 6-8 hours. | 3. Take Select Figs in a bowl and deseed. | 4. Add sufficient water and soak for 6-8 hours. | 5. Drain the water from dates and figs and transfer in a blender jar. | 6. Add milk, honey and blend to fine mixture. | 7. Transfer in individual glasses and sprinkle Fusion Omega Boost Trail Mix on top.

title: Anjeer Toffee
description: A healthy sweet made without the addition of sugar – ideal for growing children
ingredients: 5 Dried figs (anjeer), soaked for 2- | 3 hours and drained | 1 tbsp + for greasing Ghee | 1 cup Seedless dates (khajur), chopped 4- | 5 Walnuts | 2 tablespoons Cashewnuts, chopped | 2 tablespoons Pistachios, chopped | 2 tablespoons Almonds, chopped 1/ | 2 cup Gum resin (gond), fried
method: 1. Put the figs in a grinder jar, add a little water and grind to fine paste. | 2. Heat ghee in a deep non-stick pan and add fig paste and sauté on medium heat for 3-4 minutes. | 3. Add dates, mix well and cook for 10-15 minutes or till excess moisture dries up. | 4. Chop walnuts and add to the pan along with cashewnuts, pistachios and almonds. | 5. Mix well and cook till mixture begins to leave the sides of the pan. | 6. Add gond and mix well. | 7. Cook for 3-4 minutes on medium heat. | 8. Set aside in a bowl and allow to cool. | 9. Refrigerate for 30 minutes. | 10. Grease your palms with ghee and shape the mixture into oblong or any other desired shape. | 11. Take strips of parchment paper and place a toffee on each paper and roll them. | 12. Take coloured toffee sheets and roll the toffees again. | 13. Store the toffees in a glass jar or serve.

title: Arugula and Apple Salad
description: A peppery, distinctive-tasting green combined with apple slices and dressed with some sauces for a vibrant blend of flavors and textures — arugula and Apple Salad.
ingredients: 0 grams arugula leaves | 1 red apple | 1 green apple | 1 English cucumber 1½ teaspoons mustard paste | 1 lemon | 1 ½ tablespoons honey
method: 1. Cut red and green apple into quarters. | 2. Deseed and slice. | 3. Halve and slice English cucumber. | 4. Take ice cold water in a bowl. | 5. Add arugula leaves and set aside for 10-15 minutes. | 6. Transfer in a salad rinser and drain excess water. | 7. Take mustard paste in a glass jar. | 8. Squeeze lemon, add honey, extra virgin olive oil, crushed black peppercorns and salt. | 9. Close the lid and shake well. | 10. Take cucumber in a bowl. | 11. Add green apple and red apple. | 12. Reserve some dressing and drizzle prepared dressing and mix well. | 13. Arrange some arugula leaves on a serving plate. | 14. Add some apple mixture on top. | 15. Sprinkle pecans and dried cranberries on top. | 16. Arrange remaining arugula leaves and apple mixture on top. | 17. Sprinkle some more pecans and dried cranberries on top. | 18. Drizzle remaining dressing and serve.

title: Avocado Toast
description: Toasted sourdough topped with avocado, cream cheese, and mixed seeds.
ingredients: 2 avocados ½ sourdough bread loaf | 4 tablespoons mixed seeds Micro greens for garnish Extra virgin olive oil for drizzling | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida)
method: 1. Cut the sourdough bread loaf into thick slices. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Heat a cast iron pan. | 4. Add butter and let it melt. | 5. Place the sourdough slices on the pan and toast on both sides on low heat till golden and crisp. | 6. Take slices off the heat and arrange on a worktop. | 7. Cut the avocados in half, remove the seed, peel, and cut each half of the avocados into thin slices. | 8. Apply some cream cheese over one side of the toasted bread slices. | 9. Arrange the avocado slices over them and sprinkle crushed black peppercorns and salt. | 10. Garnish with pickled rings. | 11. Sprinkle mixed seeds, garnish with micro greens, and drizzle extra virgin olive oil. | 12. Serve immediately.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Babycorn Paneer Drumsticks
description: A savory symphony of textures and flavors, marrying the tender crunch of babycorn wrapped with creamy paneer and potato mixture, all rolled into one delicious bite.
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 0 baby corns | 1 cup grated cottage cheese (paneer) | 2 medium potatoes, boiled, peeled and grated | 1 small carrot, finely chopped | 1 tablespoon chopped ginger | 3 green chillies, chopped | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder | 1 teaspoon garam masala powder | 1 tablespoon refined flour (maida) | 1 tablespoon +
method: 1. Take paneer in a bowl, add potatoes, carrot, ginger, green chillies, red chilli powder, garam masala powder, red chilli sauce and soy sauce and mix well. | 2. Add refined flour, 1 tablespoon cornflour and salt and mix well. | 3. Cover ¾ of each baby corn with the mixture to form drumsticks. | 4. Transfer in plate and add ½ cup cornflour and coat well. | 5. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai, slide in the baby corn drumsticks and deep fry on low heat till golden and crisp, then add a pinch of hing. | 6. Drain on absorbent paper. | 7. Sprinkle chaat masala. | 8. Arrange them on a serving platter, sprinkle some more chaat masala, garnish with red cherry tomatoes and flower. | 9. Serve hot with red chilli sauce.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Bajra Risotto
description: Healthy version of risotto – bajra is a good replacement for Arborio rice since it has a similar bite
ingredients: 1 1/ | 2 cups pearl millet (bajra), soaked for | 2 hours, drained and cooked with v | 1 tablespoon olive oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 tablespoons butter 1/ | 1 cup white wine 3- | 0 grams parmesan cheese | 2 1/ | 2 tablespoons fresh cream Red chilli flakes to sprinkle Fresh parsley leaves to garnish
method: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan, add butter and let it melt, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Finely chop and add to the pan, sauté till translucent. | 3. Add and sauté well for 2 minutes. | 4. Add white wine, mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes. | 5. Add cooked bajra and mix well. | 6. Cook for 2-3 minutes. | 7. Add thyme sprigs and crushed black peppercorns and mix well. | 8. Grate parmesan cheese into the risotto and add fresh cream and mix well. | 9. Cook for 1-2 minutes. | 10. Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle chilli flakes and crushed black peppercorns. | 11. Garnish with parsley and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Baked Chocolate Apple
description: Apples hollowed out, stuffed with fresh coconut and jaggery mixture, baked and served sprinkled with chocolate chips
ingredients: 4 medium Red apples to garnish Chocolate chips to sprinkle Sea salt
method: 1. Preheat oven to 160º C. | 2. Slice the top and bottom of the apples and scoop out the flesh. | 3. Sprinkle sea salt and rub it uniformly inside the hollowed apples and place them on a baking tray. | 4. Chop the flesh. | 5. Heat a non-stick pan, add the chopped apple flesh and sauté till lightly cooked. | 6. Add coconut, mixed nuts and jaggery and mix well. | 7. Cook on low heat till jaggery melts. | 8. Switch off heat. | 9. Add green cardamom powder and cinnamon powder and mix well. | 10. Wipe the apples from inside with a tissue if they are moist from inside. | 11. Fill the nut mixture into each cored apple. | 12. Break half the caramel bars into small pieces and place on top, drizzle some rabdi. | 13. Repeat the process once more. | 14. Place the tray in the preheated oven and bake for 12-15 minutes. | 15. Spread remaining stuffing in deep serving plate and place the baked apples over it. | 16. Garnish with rabdi, chocolate chips and mixed nuts and serve immediately.

title: Banana Walnut Chocolate Ice Cream
description: The combination of ripe bananas, walnuts and chocolate make for a super delicious ice cream that will win many a heart
ingredients: 3 medium Ripe bananas, peeled and frozen 3/ | 4 cup Walnuts | 4 tablespoons Unsweetened cocoa powder 1/ | 2 cup Fresh cream | 3 tablespoons Honey | 1 teaspoon Vanilla essence to garnish Ripe banana, peeled and coated with melted dark chocolate clove Sugar fondant to garnish Walnuts, crushed
method: 1. Cut each banana into 3 pieces and put in a grinder jar. | 2. Add cocoa powder, cream, honey, walnuts and vanilla essence and grind into a smooth mixture. | 3. Pour this mixture into an air tight container and put the lid on. | 4. Keep it in the freezer for 6-8 hours or overnight. | 5. Once set take the ice cream out of the freezer. | 6. Dip ice cream scooper in warm water and then scoop out 5 scoops of ice cream into a serving bowl. | 7. Skew the banana coated with melted dark chocolate onto a skewer and place it on the rim of the bowl. | 8. Use sugar fondant to create eyes and nose of a penguin. | 9. Garnish with crushed walnuts and serve immediately.

title: Barley Risotto
description: Barley replaces Arborio rice in this risotto and the result is very tasty. Barley cooked with garlic , assorted mushrooms and served.
ingredients: 1 1/ | 2 cups barley (jau), soaked for | 8 hours and cooked | 1 tablespoon olive oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 tablespoon butter 6- | 5 button mushrooms | 1 cup white wine 7- | 2 cup fresh cream
method: 1. Heat olive oil and butter in a deep non-stick till butter melts, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add and sauté well. | 3. Add mix well and saute till translucent. | 4. Quarter button mushrooms and add to the mixture. | 5. Add white wine, mix well and cook for 1-2 minutes. | 6. Add shitake mushrooms and mix well. | 7. Cover and cook for 4-6 minutes. | 8. Add cooked barley and mix well. | 9. Add salt. | 10. Grate processed cheese directly into the barley mixture and mix well. | 11. Add crushed black peppercorns, mix well and cook for 1-2 minutes. | 12. Add fresh cream and mix well. | 13. Cook for 1 minute and take the pan off the heat. | 14. Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle crushed black peppercorns and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Batata Puri
description: Call them batata puri or sabudana vada, they are just as delicious
ingredients: 4 Medium potatoes medium, boiled and peeled | 1 cup Sago (sabudana) soaked 1/ | 2 cup Roasted peanuts crushed | 2 tablespoons Fresh coriander leaves chopped | 1 tablespoon Ginger-green chilli paste 1/ | 2 tablespoon Lemon juice | 2 Banana leaves cut into medium squares to deep-fry Oil to serve Coconut chutney
method: 1. Take sago in a bowl add sago, add peanuts, coriander leaves, sugar, rock salt, ginger green chilli paste and lemon juice. | 2. Mash and add potatoes and mix well. | 3. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 4. Divide the mixture into equal portions. | 5. Place a banana leaf square on the worktop, apply little oil on it, place a portion of potato mixture, gently press and flatten slightly. | 6. Slide into hot oil and deep-fry, turning sides, till golden brown on both sides. | 7. Drain on absorbent paper. | 8. Arrange the puris on a serving plate and serve hot with coconut chutney.

title: Bathue ka Parantha
description: Bathua leaves kneaded with whole wheat flour, rolled into paranthas and cooked
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 medium bunches Bathua leaves, cleaned | 1 1/ | 2 cups for dusting Whole wheat flour | 1 teaspoon Carom seeds (ajwain) 2- | 3 Green chillies, finely chopped | 1 tablespoon Ginger, finely chopped 1/ | 2 tsp + to sprinkle Red chilli powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon Turmeric powder
method: 1. Roughly chop bathua leaves and transfer into a bowl. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Add carom seeds, green chillies, ginger, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, wheat flour and and mix well. | 4. Add water as required and knead to a soft dough. | 5. Divide the dough into equal portions and roll out into large rotis on a dusted worktop. | 6. Apply some ghee on the rotis, dust some flour and sprinkle chilli powder and chaat masala on top. | 7. Fold into a square and roll out into paranthas. | 8. Heat a non-stick tawa. | 9. Place the parathas on it, one at a time, and cook, basting with ghee, till evenly golden brown on both the sides. | 10. Place them on individual serving plates and serve hot with white butter, yogurt and pickle of your choice.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Beetroot Browines
description: These extremely healthy and dark pink brownies are simply irresistible
ingredients: 1 cup Boiled beetroot puree for greasing Butter | 3 tablespoons Flax seed (alsi) powder | 1 Ripe banana, roasted 3/ | 4 cup Honey 3/ | 4 cup Coconut oil | 1 cup Rice flour 3/ | 4 cup Cocoa powder | 1 pinch Salt | 1 teaspoon Baking powder 1/ | 2 cup Walnuts, chopped
method: 1. Preheat oven to 200º C. | 2. Grease a square baking tin with butter and line it with butter paper. | 3. Take flax seed powder in a large bowl, add 9 tablespoons water and mix well, set aside for 5 minutes. | 4. Peel the roasted banana and mash it with a fork. | 5. Add the banana to the flax seed mixture. | 6. Add beetroot puree and mix well. | 7. Add honey and coconut oil and whisk till well combined. | 8. Sift rice flour into the beetroot mixture and fold in gently. | 9. Sift cocoa powder into the beetroot mixture and fold in again. | 10. Add salt, baking powder and walnuts and mix well. | 11. Pour the batter into the prepared cake tin, keep it in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes. | 12. Remove the tin from the oven and let it cool. | 13. Cut it into squares and pack it in the tiffin.

title: Beetroot Coconut Barfi
description: These beautiful ruby red barfis are so tasty that they will get consumed in a jiffy
ingredients: 1 medium Beetroot, peeled and grated | 2 cups Fresh coconut, scraped | 1 tbsp _ for greasing Ghee | 1 cup Sugar 1/ | 2 cup Fresh cream to garnish Walnuts | 3 tablespoons Powdered sugar 1/ | 2 teaspoon Green cardamom powder
method: 1. Grease a barfi tray with some ghee. | 2. Heat ghee in a deep non-stick pan, add sugar, coconut and cream and mix well. | 3. Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously, for 15-20 minutes. | 4. Meanwhile roughly chop walnuts. | 5. When mixture begins to leave the pan, add the beetroot and mix well. | 6. Add powdered sugar and green cardamom powder, mix well and cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. | 7. Transfer the barfi mixture in the greased tray and spread it evenly. | 8. Sprinkle walnuts on top. | 9. Set aside till set and cooled down to room temperature. | 10. Cut into rectangular barfis. | 11. Insert a coloured ice cream stick in each barfi, arrange them on a serving platter and serve immediately.

title: Beetroot Quinoa Pachadi
description: Popular South Indian accompaniment made with all super foods. Watch this video to find out how to make this recipe. This is
ingredients: 4 tablespoons beetroot puree ¼ cup quinoa | 1 teaspoons oil ¼ cup scraped fresh coconut | 1 green chillies, finely chopped ½ inch ginger, finely chopped | 2 dried red chillies ½ teaspoon mustard seeds 4- | 5 curry leaves
method: 1. Heat a small deep non-stick pan. | 2. Add quinoa, 1 cup water and salt and mix. | 3. Add 1 teaspoon oil, mix and bring the mixture to boil. | 4. Cover and cook till quinoa is done. | 5. Transfer in a bowl and refrigerate it. | 6. Coarsely grind together coconut, green chillies and ginger. | 7. Take yogurt in another bowl and whisk. | 8. Add ground coconut mixture and whisk. | 9. Add beetroot puree and whisk well. | 10. Add salt to yogurt mixture and whisk. | 11. Add cooked quinoa and whisk well. | 12. Transfer on a shallow serving plate. | 13. To prepare tempering, heat remaining oil in a tempering pan. | 14. Roughly cut dried red chillies using a pair of scissors. | 15. Add mustard seeds to hot oil and let it sputter. | 16. Add curry leaves and let it crackle. | 17. Add dried red chillies and swirl the pan for few seconds. | 18. Pour the tempering over the beetroot yogurt mixture and serve.

title: Bhakri Pizza
description: A creative fusion dish that combines the rustic charm of traditional Indian bhakri with the flavorful toppings of a classic pizza, offering a unique culinary experience.
ingredients: Readymade pearl millet (bajra) dough Pearl millet (bajra) flour for dusting | 1 teaspoon oil ½ teaspoon cumin seeds | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 medium red capsicum, cut into triangles | 1 medium green capsicum, cut into triangles ½ cup boiled corn kernels Cheese sauce as required Halved black olives for garnish Black olive slices for garnish
method: 1. To prepare pizza sauce, heat oil in a non-stick pan, add cumin seeds and and mix well, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add mix and sauté till translucent. | 3. Add tomato puree, turmeric powder, red chilli powder and salt and mix well. | 4. Sauté till oil separates. | 5. Sprinkle bajra flour on worktop, keep the dough on it and roll into a thick disc. | 6. Heat a non-stick tawa and place the disc on it and cook for 3-4 minutes. | 7. Flip and cook on other side till golden brown specs appear. | 8. While the bhakri is still on the pan, spread a spoonful of pizza sauce on it, sprinkle mozzarella cheese in the centre, sprinkle red and green capsicum triangles, boiled corn kernels on top. | 9. Cover and cook on low heat till cheese melts completely. | 10. Transfer the pizza on a serving plate. | 11. Put cheese sauce in a piping bag and pipe out cheese sauce on top of pizza in the shape of spider web. | 12. Place a halved black olive in centre. | 13. Place 6 halved olives around. | 14. Now place black olive slices as legs of the spider. | 15. Serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Bhakri with Thecha
description: Typical rural meal of Maharashtra – it is so very tasty that you will want to eat it again and again
ingredients: 3 tablespoons Oil 10- | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 Dark green chillies 10- | 4 cup Roasted peanuts
method: 1. To make bhakri, heat sufficient water in an earthen pot, and let it come to a boil, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Take ragi flour in a large bowl, add salt and mix well. | 3. Pour the hot water little at a time, mix well till it forms soft dough. | 4. Divide the dough into equal portions and shape each into a ball. | 5. Dust each ball with ragi flour, flatten it and roll into a thin disc. | 6. Heat an earthen tawa, place the discs on it, one at a time, and cook, turning sides, till small brown specks appear on both sides. | 7. Take it off the heat and keep under a muslin cloth. | 8. To make thecha, heat oil in another earthen pot, add dark green chillies, light green chillies and sea salt and sauté well. | 9. Cover and cook for 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally. | 10. Remove the lid and add roasted peanuts, adjust salt and sauté well. | 11. Cook for 2-3 minutes and take it off the heat. | 12. Cool the mixture, transfer into a mortar pestle and pound it coarsely. | 13. Arrange the bhakris on a serving platter and transfer thecha into a bowl and serve the two together.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Bhindi Hara Masala
description: Bursting with flavour this bhindi dish is so very irresistible. Ladyfingers cooked with a green masala and Indian spices.
ingredients: 0 grams ladyfingers (bhindi), ends trimmed and cut into | 1 inch pieces | 1 tbsp oil + to deep fry | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 inch ginger, cut into strips + to garnish | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds 2- | 3 green chillies, finely chopped | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder 1/ | 4 teaspoon turmeric powder 1/ | 2 medium tomatoes | 1 green chilli, slit Yellow capsicum strips to garnish Red capsicum strips to garnish Hara masala | 1 cup fresh coriander leaves 1/ | 2 cup fresh mint leaves 1/ | 4 cup scraped fresh coconut | 2 green chillies | 1 inch ginger
method: 1. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai and deep-fry the ladyfinger pieces till crisp, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Drain on absorbent paper. | 3. To make hara masala, put coriander leaves, mint leaves, coconut, green chillies and ginger in a grinder jar and grind to a coarse paste. | 4. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a non-stick pan, add ginger, cumin seeds and green chillies and sauté till fragrant. | 5. Add mix and sauté till it turns brown. | 6. Add mix and sauté till translucent. | 7. Add the fried ladyfingers, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, dried mango powder and salt, mix and cook for 2-3 minutes. | 8. Add hara masala and mix well. | 9. Roughly chop tomatoes, add to the pan and cook till they turn soft and pulpy. | 10. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with ginger strips, slit green chilli, yellow and red capsicum strips and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Bhindi Methi Bhurjee
description: Bhindi and methi make odd couple but in this dish they are simply very compatible. Ladyfingers and fenugreek leaves cooked with Indian spices. Best served with hot phulkas.
ingredients: 0 grams ladyfinges (bhindi), finely chopped | 1 large bunch fresh fenugreek leaves (methi), chopped | 3 tablespoons oil 2- | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 3 green chillies, finely chopped | 1 tablespoon ginger, finely chopped | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon turmeric powder | 1 tablespoon coriander powder | 1 teaspoon garam masala powder | 1 1/ | 1 medium tomato, finely chopped Fresh coriander sprigs to garnish
method: 1. Heat rice bran oil in a non-stick pan, add green chillies, ginger and and sauté till fragrant, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add mix and sauté till translucent. | 3. Add ladyfingers, mix and cook for 8-10 minutes. | 4. Add red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder, dried mango powder and salt. | 5. Mix well and cook for 4-5 minutes. | 6. Add fenugreek leaves and tomato, mix and cook till the tomatoes turn soft and pulpy. | 7. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprigs and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Biscuit Ice Cream Chocolate Bars
description: Icecream bar covered with glucose biscuits, frozen and served drizzled with melted chocolate and nuts Icecream bar covered with glucose biscuits, frozen and served drizzled with melted chocolate and...
ingredients: 0 Glucose biscuits | 1 litres Vanilla ice cream bar | 1 cup Dark chocolate, melted to garnish Pistachios, finely chopped to garnish Vermicelli sprinklers to garnish Almonds, chopped
method: 1. Stick half the glucose biscuits on the edge of one aide of the ice cream bar. | 2. Trim the exposed portion of the bar and turn it sideways so that biscuit portion is on one side. | 3. Now halve the bar from middle lengthwise and stick the remaining biscuits on the cut side. | 4. Transfer the bar on an aluminium sheet and cover it completely. | 5. Freeze for 1-2 hours. | 6. Further cut into smaller bars. | 7. Insert an ice cream stick in centre of each bar. | 8. Take melted dark chocolate in a bowl. | 9. Pour melted dark chocolate on each bar with a spoon. | 10. Drip off extra chocolate. | 11. Sprinkle pistachio, vermicelli sprinkles and almonds on each bar. | 12. Place them on a silicon mat and refrigerate for 5 minutes. | 13. Remove from refrigerator and transfer onto a serving platter and serve immediately.

title: Black Grapes Shikanji
description: A refreshing and tangy black grape shikanji with a burst of spices, perfect for a chilled, revitalizing drink! This is
ingredients: 25- | 0 black grapes + for garnish ¼ cup sugar ¼ teaspoon salt A pinch of red chilli powder ¼ teaspoon roasted cumin powder A pinch of chaat masala Lemon wedges as required Pink salt as required Ice cubes as required Lemon slices as required Grape slices as required Mint sprigs as required Drinking soda as required
method: 1. Transfer black grapes in a blender jar. | 2. Add sugar and blend to a fine paste. | 3. Heat a nonstick pan and strain the puree through a fine sieve. | 4. Cook till the mixture thickens. | 5. Add salt, red chilli powder, roasted cumin powder, chaat masala and mix well. | 6. Cook for 2 minutes. | 7. Allow to cool. | 8. Apply lemon on rim the glass and press pink salt on it. | 9. Add ice cubes, lemon slices, grape slices and some more ice. | 10. Add mint sprigs and pour a portion of prepared syrup. | 11. Top up with soda, garnish with grapes and mint and serve chilled.

title: Broccoli & Cheese Soup
description: This velvety concoction combines the wholesome goodness of tender broccoli florets with the rich, creamy allure of melted cheese, creating a symphony of flavors. This soup gets done easily and...
ingredients: 2 medium broccoli 8- | 2 small pieces of bocconcini | 3 tablespoons butter | 1 cup milk | 2 teaspoons olive oil Slices of bread to serve Parsley sprig for garnish | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida)
method: 1. Separate small florets from broccoli and discard their stems. | 2. Put butter in a soup maker. | 3. Roughly chop and add. | 4. Add broccoli florets, vegetable stock, salt and crushed black peppercorns and cover with the lid. | 5. Select the desired option and let it cook for 4-6 minutes. | 6. Remove the lid and add milk, cover with lid, select the option and cook till the soup is done. | 7. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, keep the bread slices in it and cook, turning sides, till crisp on both sides, then add a pinch of hing. | 8. Keep them on a plate. | 9. Place 2-3 bocconcini pieces at the bottom of each serving bowl and pour the soup over. | 10. Garnish each bowl with a fresh parsley sprig and serve hot with toasted bread slices.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Broccoli Mac and Cheese
description: Blanched broccoli florets tossed in a healthy cheesy white sauce . Macaroni is replaced with broccoli in this recipe.
ingredients: 4 tablespoons butter | 1 tablespoon whole wheat flour | 2 cups milk
method: 1. Boil sufficient water in a deep non-stick deep pan. | 2. Cut small florets of broccoli. | 3. Add salt and broccoli florets to the boiling water and blanch for 1-2 minutes. | 4. Meanwhile, melt butter in another non-stick pan, add whole wheat flour and mix well. | 5. Add milk and mix well. | 6. Grate processed cheese directly into the pan, mix well and cook till the cheese melts. | 7. Add salt and crushed black peppercorns and mix well. | 8. A pinch of dried mixed herbs and mix well. | 9. Strain the broccoli and add to the sauce, mix well and take the pan off the heat. | 10. Transfer into a serving plate, sprinkle red chilli flakes and serve hot.

title: Burnt Garlic Vegetable Fried Rice
description: Rice tossed with fresh cut vegetables and burnt garlic
ingredients: 1 1/ | 2 cups Basmati rice,soaked for 15- | 0 minutes 10- | 6 French beans 1/ | 4 medium Cabbage 1/ | 2 medium Carrot 2- | 1 tablespoon Oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 teaspoon Soy sauce | 1 teaspoon Vinegar
method: 1. Finely chop string the French beans and chop them finely. | 2. Finely shred cabbage, peel and grate carrot. | 3. Slice and finely chop the stalk. | 4. Boil sufficient water in a deep pan and add salt. | 5. Drain and add the rice cover and cook till the rice is ¾ done. | 6. Drain and spread the rice on a plate to cool down. | 7. Heat oil in a non-stick wok, add and sauté on high heat till golden brown, then add a pinch of hing. | 8. Add mix and sauté for 30 seconds. | 9. Add French beans, carrot and cabbage, mix and sauté for 1 minute. | 10. Add salt and soy sauce and mix well. | 11. Add rice and adjust salt, toss and cook for 1 minute. | 12. Cover and cook on low heat for 2 minutes. | 13. Switch off heat, add vinegar and mix well. | 14. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Caramel Walnut Fudge
description: This fudge will be highly appreciated by both children and adults alike
ingredients: 4 tbsps + for drizzling Caramel syrup 1/ | 4 cup Walnuts, chopped | 1 tbsp + to grease Ghee | 2 tablespoons Butter 1/ | 2 cup Khoya, grated | 1 cup Condensed milk | 0 grams Dark chocolate, chopped
method: 1. Grease a shallow bowl with some ghee. | 2. Heat ghee in a non-stick pan, add butter and walnuts, mix and sauté till the walnuts are roasted. | 3. Reduce heat, add khoya and condensed milk, mix and cook till the mixture thickens, stirring continuously. | 4. Remove from heat, add caramel syrup and mix well. | 5. Place the pan back on heat and mix till well combined. | 6. Add chocolate and mix till it melts. | 7. Transfer the mixture onto a greased bowl and spread evenly. | 8. Drizzle some caramel syrup on top and refrigerate till set. | 9. Cut into pieces, arrange them on a serving platter and serve.

title: Cashew Pesto Pasta
description: Get ready to fall in love with 'Cashew Pesto Pasta' all over again, which features the premium taste of #Prov, making it a flavorful recipe perfect for every occasion!
ingredients: 10- | 5 cashews 1½ cups fresh basil leaves 8- | 0 black peppercorns 10- | 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 5 button mushrooms, quartered ¼ cup boiled corn kernels 5- | 2 cups boiled penne pasta Grated parmesan cheese to sprinkle Fresh basil sprig for garnish
method: 1. Take basil leaves in a grinder jar. | 2. Add black peppercorns, parmesan cheese powder, ½ cup extra virgin olive oil and cashews and grind to a coarse paste. | 3. Heat remaining extra virgin olive oil in a nonstick wok, then add a pinch of hing. | 4. Add button mushrooms and sauté for 2 minutes. | 5. Add corn kernels, cherry tomatoes and sauté for a minute. | 6. Add prepared pesto, salt and mix well. | 7. Add pasta and toss well. | 8. Transfer in a pasta plate, sprinkle parmesan cheese, garnish with basil sprig and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Chaat Quiche
description: A fusion delight combining the flaky goodness of quiche with the tangy, spicy flavors of Indian chaat, perfect for a unique and satisfying snack or appetizer.
ingredients: Tart 2½ cups soya bean flour Salt to taste | 4 tablespoons ghee | 1 teaspoon carom seeds (ajwain) | 1 cup black eyed beans (lobia) Filling | 2 tablespoons powdered sugar ¼ cup roasted peanuts, peeled | 1 cup dried white peas (matara), soaked overnight, boiled with salt and turmeric powder | 2 medium sweet potatoes, boiled, peeled and cut into ½ inch cubes | 1 medium carrot, peeled and grated | 1 medium beetroot, peeled and grated Chaat masala to sprinkle Tamarind chutney to drizzle Green chutney to drizzle Nylon sev for garnish Blanched carrot, cut into star shaped pieces for garnish Blanched beetroot, cut into star shaped pieces for garnish
method: 1. Preheat the oven to 180˚C. | 2. To prepare short crust dough, take soyabean flour in a bowl. | 3. Add salt, ghee and mix well. | 4. Add carom seeds. | 5. Add little water and knead to semi soft dough. | 6. Divide the dough into equal portions, place each on the worktop and roll into a thick disc. | 7. Place it in a quiche mould and press lightly on all sides. | 8. Trim the edges which are bulging out of the mould. | 9. Spread a parchment paper on it and spread black eyed beans over the paper. | 10. Place the mould in the preheated oven and blind bake for 12-15 minutes. | 11. Take yogurt, salt, black salt, chaat masala and powdered sugar in a bowl. | 12. Mix well and set aside. | 13. Lightly crush roasted peanuts and set aside in another bowl. | 14. Remove quiche mould from the oven and allow it to cool down completely. | 15. Unmould the quiche base from the mould. | 16. Spread boiled matara evenly over it. | 17. Add sweet potato cubes, grated carrot and beetroot. | 18. Sprinkle chaat masala and crushed peanuts. | 19. Pour the yogurt mixture on top and cover the filling completely. | 20. Drizzle tamarind chutney and green chutney. | 21. Sprinkle nylon sev, garnish with carrot and beetroot stars and serve.

title: Chakna Platter
description: An assortment of spicy and crunchy snacks commonly enjoyed with drinks in India, featuring items like peanuts, fried lentils, chickpea flour noodles (sev), and seasoned nuts or seeds.
ingredients: Masala Peanuts | 1 teaspoon chaat masala Oil for deep-frying Wasabi Makhana | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 tablespoon oil | 2 cups puffed lotus seeds (makhana) | 2 teaspoons wasabi powder Masala Papad Cones | 1 papad | 2 cups chana, boiled | 1 tomato, finely chopped | 1 green chilli, finely chopped ½ teaspoon black salt | 1 tablespoon chopped coriander Juice of half lemon Sev for garnish Coriander leaves for garnish Cheesy Nuts | 1 cup cashews, whole | 1 teaspoon chaat masala Cheeseling Bhel | 2 cups cheeseling | 1 medium tomato, finely chopped | 1 ½ teaspoon chaat masala | 1 tablespoon chopped coriander ¼ cup sev Schezwan Chakli | 1 cup rice flour ¼ cup split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal), ground
method: 1. Masala Peanuts Heat sufficient oil in a kadai and deep-fry the peanuts till golden brown, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Transfer to a bowl. | 3. In a separate bowl add chilli powder, black salt, salt to taste, chaat masala and prepare a masala for the peanuts. | 4. Mix the prepared masala in the peanuts well, transfer to a cutting glass filled till top and serve. | 5. Wasabi Makhana Heat oil in a non-stick pan and add the makhana, toss for 2-3 minutes. | 6. Add wasabi powder and toss again. | 7. Transfer to a cutting glass, filled till top and serve. | 8. Masala Papad Heat a non-stick tawa. | 9. Cut the papad into half and roast on the heated tawa until cooked. | 10. Fold into a cone, once cooked. | 11. Add boiled chana, tomato, green chilli, black salt, chaat masala, salt to taste, chopped coriander and lemon juice in a bowl and mix well. | 12. Fill this mixture in the prepared papad cones. | 13. Garnish with sev and coriander leaves, place the cones in a cutting glass and serve. | 14. Cheesy Nuts Heat sufficient oil in a kadai and deep-fry the cashews and almonds till golden. | 15. Drain on an absorbent paper and transfer to a bowl. | 16. Add cheese powder, black salt, salt to taste and chaat masala and mix well. | 17. Transfer to a cutting glass filled till top and serve. | 18. Cheeseling Bhel Add cheeselings, tomatoes, raw mango, black salt, salt, lemon juice and chaat masala in a bowl and mix well together. | 19. Add chopped coriander and sev. | 20. Transfer to a cutting glass filled till top and serve. | 21. Schezwan Chakli Add rice flour, ground split skinless black gram (urad dal aata), schezwan sauce, salt to taste, cumin seeds, chilli powder and some water in a bowl. | 22. Knead it to make soft dough. | 23. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 24. Grease the chakli press with some oil. | 25. Put some dough, at a time, in a chakli press and press out chaklis in strips directly in the oil. | 26. Deep-fry on medium heat till crisp. | 27. Drain on absorbent paper and cool down to room temperature. | 28. Serve in a cutting glass.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Chana Cheese Roti Turnover
description: A delightful fusion of Indian and Western cuisines, featuring a savory turnover made with roti crust filled with a hearty mixture of spiced chickpeas and cheese.
ingredients: 1½ cups chickpeas (kabuli chane), soaked overnight, drained and boiled with salt and drained 2- | 3 tablespoons oil + to drizzle | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 tablespoon grated ginger | 4 whole wheat tortillas or rotis Chana jor garam as required Cheese cubes as required Ginger strips for garnish Fresh mint sprigs for garnish
method: 1. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add ginger and sauté till raw flavour goes away, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add and sauté till it turns golden brown. | 3. Add capsicum and sauté for 1 minute. | 4. Add chickpeas, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder and salt. | 5. Mix well. | 6. Crush the chickpeas slightly with a masher. | 7. Transfer the mixture into a bowl. | 8. Chop coriander leaves and add to the chickpea mixture and mix well. | 9. Allow the mixture to cool down. | 10. Keep each roti on the worktop, place some mixture on one side, place chana jor garam and cheese cubes on top. | 11. Fold the roti into half. | 12. Heat a non-stick tawa, place the folded rotis on it, drizzle some oil all around and cook on medium heat till the underside becomes crisp. | 13. Turnover and cook till the other side becomes evenly golden and the cheese melts. | 14. Cut into half and transfer on a serving plate. | 15. Sprinkle some red chilli powder, crush some chana jor garam and sprinkle on top. | 16. Serve hot, garnished with ginger strips and mint sprigs.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Cheese Kurkure
description: Got guests coming over for Diwali? This recipe will come in handy. Mozzarella and cheddar cheese stuffed rolls deep-fried till the outside is crisp and the cheese inside has melted. These are a...
ingredients: 3 tablespoons grated mozzarella cheese | 0 grams processed cheese, cut into ½ cm small cubes | 1 cup + | 2 tablespoons yogurt | 1 tablespoon oil + for deep-frying
method: 1. Take 1 cup flour in a bowl. | 2. Add salt, baking soda, yogurt and some water and knead into a soft dough. | 3. Add oil and knead well. | 4. Set aside for 5-10 minutes. | 5. Take mozzarella cheese in a bowl. | 6. Add processed cheese, dried oregano, chilli flakes, crushed peppercorns and salt and lightly mix. | 7. Heat sufficient oil in a pan. | 8. Divide the dough into equal portions, coat with some flour and roll them out to make thin discs. | 9. Cut each disc into rectangles. | 10. Take 1 tablespoon flour in another bowl. | 11. Add 2-3 tablespoons water to make a thick slurry. | 12. Apply some slurry on 2 sides of each rectangle. | 13. Put some cheese mixture in the center, and fold, applying some slurry on the edges to make thick rolls. | 14. Deep-fry the prepared rolls in hot oil till golden and crisp. | 15. Drain on absorbent paper. | 16. Line a serving platter with individual shot glasses and put some tomato ketchup in each glass. | 17. Place one fried roll in each glass and serve hot.

title: Cheesy Roesti
description: A crispy Swiss delight, marrying golden-brown potatoes with gooey melted cheese for a comforting and indulgent treat.
ingredients: ½ cup grated mixed cheese (processed, cheddar and mozzarella) | 3 large potatoes, peeled | 1 teaspoon crushed black peppercorns ⅓ cup refined flour | 2 teaspoons butter Fresh parsley sprig for garnish
method: 1. Grate potatoes on a plate and soak in a bowl of ice cold water for 15-20 minutes. | 2. Drain the potatoes thoroughly, put them on a kitchen towel and dab excess water. | 3. Transfer into another bowl, add mixed herbs, red chilli flakes, salt, crushed black peppercorns and refined flour and mix well. | 4. Divide the potato mixture into two portions. | 5. Heat a non-stick pan and spread one portion of the potato mixture on it. | 6. Sprinkle cheese evenly on top and spread the remaining potato mixture over the cheese. | 7. Add butter and let it cook until crisp and golden in colour. | 8. Turn and cook similarly on the other side as well. | 9. Turn it out onto a serving dish, garnish it with parsley sprig and serve hot.

title: Chickoo Walnut Kulfi with Salted Caramel Sauce
description: Chickoo and walnuts add a wonderful flavour and texture to this ice cream and the salted caramel sauce is an added attraction
ingredients: 6 Sapodilla (chickoo), quartered, seeded and pureed with skin 1/ | 4 cup Walnuts, chopped | 2 litres Whole fat milk 2- | 3 tablespoons Milk powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon Green cardamom powder | 1 cup Khoya, grated 3/ | 4 cup Sweet condensed milk Salted caramel sauce | 1 pinch Salt 1/ | 4 cup Sugar 3/ | 4 cup Fresh cream | 1 tablespoon Butter
method: 1. Heat milk in a deep non-stick pan. | 2. Once it comes to a boil, reduce heat and simmer, stirring continuously, till it reduces to half its original volume. | 3. Scrape the sides of pan and put it back into the pan. | 4. Grind walnuts to a fine powder. | 5. Take milk powder in a bowl, add 4 tbsps of hot milk and mix well. | 6. Add this mixture to the simmering milk and mix. | 7. Add green cardamom powder and khoya and mix well. | 8. Continue to simmer till the mixture thickens. | 9. Switch the heat off, transfer the mixture into a bowl and set aside to cool down to room temperature. | 10. Add condensed milk and walnut powder to the milk mixture and mix well. | 11. Add sapodilla puree and mix well. | 12. Transfer into a tall jug. | 13. Pour the mixture into metal kulfi moulds till they are ¾ full. | 14. Cover them with their lids and keep the moulds in the freezer for 2 hours or till semi set. | 15. Now insert sticks into kulfi mixture and keep them in the freezer again till completely set. | 16. To prepare salted caramel sauce, heat a non-stick pan, add sugar and caramelize it on low heat. | 17. Gradually add cream and whisk continuously so that no lumps are formed. | 18. Add salt and mix well. | 19. Remove from heat. | 20. Add butter and whisk again. | 21. Demould the kulfis, place them in individual serving bowls and serve with salted caramel sauce.

title: Chilli Chana Aloo
description: This tangy preparation will satiate even the most discerning palate. Indo - Chinese preparation made with boiled brown Bengal gram and potatoes.
ingredients: 3 tablespoons red chilli sauce | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder | 2 tbsps schezwan sauce + to garnish | 1 cup brown Bengal gram (kala chana), soaked overnight and boiled | 2 medium potatoes (aloo), boiled and peeled | 1 tablespoon oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds | 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger | 1 tablespoon soy sauce | 1 tablespoon vinegar
method: 1. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add cumin seeds, and ginger, mix well and sauté for 2 minutes, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add mix and sauté till translucent. | 3. Cut potatoes into small cubes, add and cook for 1-2 minutes. | 4. Add red chilli sauce, soy sauce, vinegar, red chilli powder, Schezwan sauce and brown Bengal gram, mix well and cook for 1-2 minutes. | 5. Add and toss. | 6. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with Schezwan sauce and fried noodles and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Chocolate and Cheese Sandwich
description: A decadent marriage of sweet and savory, melting creamy cheese with rich chocolate between two slices of buttery bread for a truly indulgent bite.
ingredients: Chocolate syrup for drizzling | 1 cup grated processed cheese | 8 white bread slices Butter as required Chocolate vermicelli for sprinkling Colourful vermicelli for sprinkling
method: 1. Heat a non-stick tawa. | 2. Apply some butter on half the bread slices, sprinkle some cheese on top and drizzle some chocolate syrup over it. | 3. Cover with the remaining bread slices and apply some more butter on top. | 4. Transfer the sandwiches, one at a time, onto the hot tawa and toast, turning sides, till evenly golden and crisp on both sides. | 5. Cut each sandwich into half and place them on a serving platter. | 6. Drizzle some more chocolate syrup, sprinkle chocolate and colourful vermicelli and serve hot.

title: Chocolate and Coconut Dim sums
description: You will definitely relish these dimsums in their sweet avatar
ingredients: 4 tbsps + for drizzling Chocolate syrup 1/ | 2 cup Fresh coconut, scraped | 0 grams Readymade dim sum dough for brushi tablespoons Ghee 1/ | 4 cup Mixed dried nuts, coarsely powdered 1/ | 2 cup Khoya, grated
method: 1. Place a banana leaf in a steamer, pour sufficient water in the lower compartment and heat. | 2. Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a non-stick pan, add powdered nuts and saute for a minute. | 3. Add coconut, mix and saute till light brown. | 4. Remove from heat and transfer the mixture into a bowl. | 5. Add khoya and 2 tbsps chocolate syrup and mix well. | 6. Divide the dim sum dough into medium equal portions and roll out each into a disc. | 7. Place a spoonful of coconut mixture in the centre of each disc, brush a little water on the edges, seal and shape into dim sums. | 8. Brush some ghee on the banana leaf, place the dim sums on it, cover the steamer and steam for 5-6 minutes. | 9. Heat 1 tbsp ghee in another non-stick pan, add steamed dim sums and pan-fry till lightly browned on all sides. | 10. Add 2 tbsps chocolate syrup and toss. | 11. Transfer into a serving platter, drizzle some Hershey"s Chocolate Syrup on top and serve immediately.

title: Chocolate Bread Bomb
description: Chocolate shavings and cheese stuffed between bread roundels and crumb fried till crisp
ingredients: 0 grams Dark chocolate shavings | 6 White bread slices | 0 grams Processed cheese, grated as required Refined flour slurry | 2 cups Dried breadcrumbs to deep fry Oil for dusting Icing sugar
method: 1. Lightly roll the bread slices with a rolling pin. | 2. Then cut the bread slices in roundels with a round cutter. | 3. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 4. On each bread roundel, place some chocolate shavings in the center, top it up with some cheese, further place some more chocolate shavings on top. | 5. Apply water on the edges of the bread roundel and cover with another bread roundel. | 6. Press the edges to seal. | 7. Dip this bread bomb in refined flour slurry and then coat with breadcrumbs. | 8. Slide these bombs gently into hot oil and deep fry until golden brown and crisp. | 9. Drain on absorbent paper. | 10. Dust with icing sugar, arrange them on a serving platter and serve.

title: Chocolate Diya
description: This Diwali you can make edible diyas which the kids will simply love
ingredients: Khoya/mawa ( | 5 grams), Sugar ( | 2 cups + | 1 tablespoon Khoya/mawa ( | 5 grams), grated 1/ | 2 cup Sugar ( | 5 grams) | 2 tablespoons Cocoa powder 1/ | 8 teaspoon Liquid chocolate food colour 7- | 8 Almonds, slivered and soaked in saffron water
method: 1. Heat a non-stick pan, add khoya and saute till it melts. | 2. Add sugar and mix well and cook, on medium heat, stirring continuously, till sugar dissolves and mixture starts leaving the sides of pan. | 3. Transfer a small portion of cooked mixture into a small bowl and the remaining mixture into another bowl. | 4. Add cocoa powder to the mixture in the second bowl and mix well. | 5. Add liquid chocolate food colour and mix well. | 6. Refrigerate both mixtures for 10-15 minutes or till they are set. | 7. Divide cocoa-khoya mixture into medium equal portions and shape each into a ball. | 8. Flatten each ball slightly, raise the edges and shape into a diya. | 9. Take small portions of the plain khoya mixture, roll and shape into vaatis. | 10. Place them on the diyas. | 11. Stick soaked almond slivers on vaatis to form jyot (flame). | 12. Transfer the prepared diyas on a serving plate and serve.

title: Chocolate Gulab Jamun
description: Chocolate ganache balls stuffed into gulab jamuns give them a delicious make over
ingredients: 1 1/ | 2 cups Hariyali khoya, grated | 2 cups Cottage cheese (paneer), grated | 2 cups Sugar 1/ | 2 teaspoon Cornflour | 2 tablespoons Refined flour (maida) 1/ | 4 teaspoon Baking soda 1/ | 4 teaspoon Green cardamom powder to deep fry oIL | 1 tablespoon Milk to garnish Pistachio slivers
method: 1. To make sugar syrup cook 2 cups sugar and 2 cups water in a deep kadai on low heat. | 2. Meanwhile take mawa and cottage cheese in a bowl. | 3. Sift together cornflour, refined flour, baking soda and green cardamom powder into this bowl and knead lightly to make smooth dough. | 4. Shape 10 small portions of the dough into balls. | 5. Cover the rest of the dough with a damp muslin cloth and also cover balls with damp muslin cloth. | 6. Add milk to the sugar syrup and let it boil. | 7. Discard the scum that may rise to the top. | 8. Switch off heat just before the syrup reaches one string consistency. | 9. Keep the syrup warm. | 10. Heat sufficient oil in a pan. | 11. Make a dent in the centre of each ball, keep a chocolate ganache ball in it, gather the edges, seal and shape into a ball again. | 12. To remove cracks, moisten palms with water and roll the balls between them. | 13. Set aside covered with muslin cloth. | 14. Place a muslin cloth over a strainer kept over a bowl and strain the sugar syrup. | 15. Slide the balls into hot oil and deep-fry, on low heat, stirring constantly till golden brown. | 16. Drain the fried jamuns and soak them in the lukewarm sugar syrup for 1 hour. | 17. Drain and transfer the gulab jamuns into serving bowls. | 18. Pour a few spoons of sugar syrup over the jamuns. | 19. Garnish with pistachio slivers and serve immediately.

title: Chocolate Nut Bar
description: Whenever you want to bite into something healthy, this bar is just for you
ingredients: 4 cup Chocolate chips 1/ | 2 cup Mixed nuts, chopped | 1 tbsp + for greasing Butter | 1 cup Brown sugar | 2 cups Rolled oats | 1 tablespoon Isabgol | 2 1/ | 2 tablespoons Honey
method: 1. Heat butter in a non-stick pan, add brown sugar, mix well and add rolled oats. | 2. Toss the mixture well and transfer into a large bowl. | 3. Add mixed nuts to the same pan and toast for a few minutes. | 4. Add to the same bowl. | 5. Add chocolate chips, isabgol and honey and mix well. | 6. Transfer this mixture into a greased baking tray and spread evenly. | 7. Press to even out the top and refrigerate till set. | 8. Cut into bars and serve.

title: Chocolate Overnight Muesli
description: Soy milk is not only rich in proteins but it can be tolerated by those who are allergic to lactose
ingredients: 2 tablespoons cocoa powder 1¾ cups muesli + for garnish | 2 ripe bananas | 2 cups soy milk 6- | 7 seedless dates, chopped | 1 apple 8- | 0 orange segments | 2 tablespoons chia seeds | 2 tablespoons honey Chopped mixed nuts for garnish 10- | 2 black berries for garnish 2- | 3 strawberries for garnish 8- | 0 blueberries for garnish
method: 1. Peel and break bananas in half and put into a blender jar, add soy milk, dates and cocoa powder and blend to a find paste. | 2. Grate apple into a large bowl, add orange segments, muesli and the blended mixture. | 3. Add chia seeds and honey and mix well. | 4. Cover and refrigerate overnight. | 5. Transfer into serving bowls, sprinkle chopped mixed nuts and some muesli on top, garnish with a few black berries. | 6. Slice the strawberries and place on top, arrange a few blueberries and serve.

title: Chocolate Pakode
description: A decadent twist on the classic Indian snack, pakode. Chocolate bars batter fried and served garnished with rabdi and chocolate sauce.
ingredients: 1 cup refined flour (maida) ½ cup cornflour ½ teaspoon cinnamon powder + to sprinkle A pinch of baking soda | 1 tablespoon powdered sugar + for garnish ¼ teaspoon vanilla essence | 1 tablespoon oil + to deep fry | 8 frozen chocolate bars Cocoa powder for garnish Rabdi for garnish Chocolate sauce to drizzle
method: 1. Take refined flour, cornflour, cinnamon powder, baking soda, powdered sugar and vanilla essence in a bowl. | 2. Add sufficient water and mix well to make a thick batter. | 3. Add oil, mix well and set aside to rest for 10 minutes. | 4. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 5. Coat the chocolate bars with batter and deep fry in hot oil till golden and crisp. | 6. Drain on an absorbent paper. | 7. Arrange them on a serving plate. | 8. Sift cocoa powder and powdered sugar over them. | 9. Sprinkle cinnamon powder, place a few spoonsful of rabdi in the centre and drizzle chocolate sauce. | 10. Serve immediately.

title: Chocolate Peanut Chikki
description: Indulge in the perfect fusion of chocolate, peanuts, and jaggery with this chikki- a delightful treat that brings joy to both children and adults alike!
ingredients: 2 cup cocoa powder | 1 cup roasted peanut powder | 1 cup chopped jaggery | 1 tbsp ghee + for greasing
method: 1. Line a baking tray with parchment paper and grease a rolling pin with ghee. | 2. Heat 1 tablespoon ghee in a non-stick pan, add jaggery and mix well. | 3. Let the jaggery melt completely. | 4. To test whether the syrup is ready, put a drop of it in cold water. | 5. If it forms a firm ball then the syrup is ready. | 6. Add cocoa powder and mix well, add roasted peanuts and mix well. | 7. Pour the mixture into the lined tray and spread it evenly with the greased rolling pin. | 8. Cut into squares while it is still hot. | 9. Allow to cool and then break the squares. | 10. Serve or store in an air-tight container.

title: Chocolate Sandwich Biscuits
description: The children will simply enjoy these biscuits sandwiched with chocolate icing and so will the adults
ingredients: 4 tablespoons Cocoa powder 1/ | 3 cup Butter 3/ | 4 cup Powdered sugar | 1 1/ | 2 cups Refined flour (maida) | 4 teaspoons Baking powder a pinch Salt 1/ | 4 teaspoon Vanilla essence | 3 tablespoons Milk to sprinkle Granulated sugar For the icing 1/ | 4 cup Butter | 1 cup Icing sugar | 3 tablespoons Cocoa powder
method: 1. To make the dough, take butter and powdered sugar in a bowl and beat till light and fluffy. | 2. Sift refined flour, baking powder, cocoa powder and salt directly in the same bowl and mix well. | 3. Add vanilla essence and milk and mix well to form a dough. | 4. Place a butter paper on the worktop, transfer the dough on it, cover with another piece of butter paper and roll out thinly. | 5. Cut into 1 inch × 3 inch rectangles and arrange them on a greased baking tray keeping a little distance between each. | 6. With the back of satay stick, make small holes at equal distance on each rectangle. | 7. Refrigerate for 10-15 minutes. | 8. Preheat the oven at 180° C. | 9. Place the tray in the preheated oven, sprinkle granulated sugar over the rectangles and bake for 7 minutes. | 10. Transfer the biscuits onto a wire rack and allow to cool down completely. | 11. Meanwhile, for the icing, take butter and icing sugar in another bowl and beat till light and fluffy. | 12. Add cocoa powder and mix till well combined. | 13. Apply the prepared icing evenly on half the biscuits, cover each of them with one of the remaining biscuits. | 14. Arrange the biscuits on a serving platter and serve. | 15. Or store in an airtight container to serve whenever needed.

title: Coconut Moong Soup
description: Our 'Coconut Moong Soup' is a delicious and nutritious option that brings together the best of both worlds. This high protein recipe, made with protein-rich moong dal and creamy coconut, results in a...
ingredients: 3 cups thin coconut milk ¾ cup soaked whole green gram (sabut moong) | 1 tablespoon ghee | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 medium carrot, peeled and cut into small cubes ¼ cup sweetcorn kernels | 1 fresh red chilli, diagonally sliced
method: 1. Heat ghee in a nonstick deep pan, add ginger and and sauté till translucent. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Add bay leaf and cinnamon and sauté for 1-2 minutes. | 4. Add the carrot, sweetcorn kernels and salt, mix and cook for 1 minute. | 5. Add the soaked green gram and mix well. | 6. Stir in the thin coconut milk and 1 cup water and mix well. | 7. Add crushed black peppercorns and red chilli and mix well. | 8. Bring the mixture to a boil, reduce the heat to low and cook for 10-12 minutes. | 9. Add and mix well. | 10. Transfer the soup in a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprigs and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Corn Cheese Balls
description: They are delightful snacks made from a mixture of sweet corn kernels, cheese, herbs, and spices, shaped into small balls, coated with breadcrumbs, and then deep-fried until golden and crispy.
ingredients: 1 cup corn kernels, boiled | 1 cup grated processed cheese | 1 teaspoon carom seeds (ajwain) | 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger | 2 green chillies, finely chopped ½ teaspoon turmeric powder ¼ teaspoon baking soda | 1 medium ½ cup gram flour (besan) | 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh coriander leaves Oil for deep-frying and greasing Yellow capsicum strips for garnishing Green capsicum strips for garnishing Fresh coriander sprigs for garnishing Green chutney for serving | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida)
method: 1. Crush corn kernels slightly and put into a bowl. | 2. Add cheese, carom seeds, ginger, green chillies, turmeric powder and baking soda and mix well. | 3. Add gram flour and red chilli paste and mix well. | 4. Add salt and coriander leaves and mix well. | 5. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai, then add a pinch of hing. | 6. Grease your palms with some Fortune Rice Bran Oil, divide the mixture into equal portions and shape them into small balls. | 7. Deep-fry the balls in hot oil till half done. | 8. Remove from oil and fry once again on high heat till golden brown. | 9. Drain on absorbent paper. | 10. Garnish with yellow and green capsicum strips, coriander sprigs and serve hot with green chutney.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Corn Halwa
description: This combination of corn kernels and khoya will result in a delicious halwa that will satiate the most discerning palate
ingredients: 1 cup Corn kernels, boiled | 4 tablespoons Ghee 1/ | 4 cup + to garnish Mixed nuts, chopped | 2 tablespoons Semolina (suji/rawa) | 1 1/ | 2 cups Khoyta (mawa) a pinch Saffron strands | 1 teaspoon Green cardamom powder 1/ | 2 cup Jaggery, grated | 1 cup Milk
method: 1. Put corn kernels into a blender jar and blend into a fine paste. | 2. Heat ghee in a non-stick pan, add nuts and sauté till lightly browned and crisp. | 3. Add semolina, mix and sauté till fragrant. | 4. Add corn paste and cook for 4-5 minutes, stirring continuously. | 5. Add khoya and mix till it melts. | 6. Add saffron strands and green cardamom powder and mix well. | 7. Add jaggery and mix till it melts. | 8. Add milk, mix and cook till the mixture thickens. | 9. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with nuts and serve hot.

title: Corn Sprouts Bhel
description: There is no kurmura in this bhel. It is made with corn kernels and mixed sprouts making it super healthy
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 cup sweet corn kernels, boiled ½ cup mixed sprouts | 1 large potato, boiled and peeled | 1 small beetroot, boiled, peeled and cut into small cubes | 2 tablespoons date and tamarind chutney | 2 tablespoons green chutney ½ small raw mango, chopped | 2 tablespoons lemon juice | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves | 3 tablespoons sev 6- | 8 crisp papdi
method: 1. Boil water in a deep non-stick pan, add mixed sprouts and cook until the sprouts are cooked. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Cut potato in to small cubes. | 4. Drain sprouts and set aside to cool. | 5. Mix corn, potato and sprouts in a bowl. | 6. Add beetroot, date and tamarind chutney and green chutney and mix well. | 7. Add raw mango, lemon juice, coriander leaves and sev and mix well. | 8. Break the papdi and add to the bhel and mix. | 9. Pack the bhel in tiffin box.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Couscous and Mixed Vegetable Salad with Orange Vinaigrette
description: Combine couscous, colorful veggies, and a sweet orange vinaigrette into a vibrant salad, topped with fresh microgreens and edible flowers for a refreshing and elegant dish.
ingredients: 1 cup couscous | 1 orange segments ½ medium red capsicum, finely chopped ½ medium green capsicum, finely chopped ½ medium tomato, finely chopped | 2 cups orange juice 2- | 3 sunflower microgreens 2- | 2 tablespoons olive oil | 1 tablespoon sugar | 1 teaspoon lemon juice | 1 tablespoon chopped coriander Edible flowers for garnishing
method: 1. Put water in a bowl, add microgreens and iceberg lettuce leaves. | 2. Keep in the refrigerator for 15-20 minutes. | 3. Boil some water in a non-stick pan. | 4. Put couscous in a large bowl, add salt pepper powder and 1 tablespoon olive oil and mix well. | 5. Add boiling water and mix well. | 6. Cover with cling film. | 7. Set aside for 15 minutes. | 8. Heat orange juice in a 2nd non-stick pan, add sugar and cook till thickens. | 9. Transfer into a bowl. | 10. Drain water from the salad leaves and microgreens. | 11. Put couscous in another large bowl, add green and red capsicum, tomato, coriander, orange segments reserving some for garnishing and mix well. | 12. Add orange vinaigrette and mix well. | 13. Place a ring mould on a serving plate, put the prepared couscous salad at the bottom of the ring, put microgreens and iceberg lettuce leaves over it. | 14. Gently remove the ring mould. | 15. Arrange reserved orange segments and edible flowers on the sides and serve immediately.

title: Cranberry Shikanji
description: Lemon, fresh mint leaves and healthy dried cranberries muddled and topped up with cranberry juice. This is
ingredients: 4 tsps dried cranberries | 2 cups Cranberry Juice | 2 lemons 25- | 0 fresh mint leaves | 2 lemons, halved | 4 pinches of black salt | 1 tsp roasted cumin powder 16- | 0 ice cubes Fresh mint sprigs for garnish
method: 1. Cut lemons into wedges and remove the seeds. | 2. Make one serving glass of cranberry shikanji at a time. | 3. Add a few lemon wedges into a serving glass, tear 8-10 mint leaves and add. | 4. Add 1 tsp dried cranberries and squeeze the juice of ½ lemon into it. | 5. Add a pinch of black salt and ¼ teaspoon roasted cumin powder and lightly muddle. | 6. Add 3-4 ice cubes and ½ cup Cranberry Juice. | 7. Garnish with a lemon slice and a fresh mint sprig. | 8. Serve immediately.

title: Creole Mushroom Rice
description: Rice cooked with mushrooms and some other ingredients in the Creole style.
ingredients: 1 cup Button mushrooms | 2 cups Rice, cooked | 1 tablespoon Oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 teaspoon Roasted cumin powder | 1 teaspoon Paprika powder ½ teaspoon Sugar | 2 medium Tomatoes, blanched and chopped | 1 teaspoon Lemon juice | 4 tablespoons Saffron (kesar) milk | 1 tablespoon Butter | 3 tablespoons Processed cheese, grated | 2 tablespoons Fresh parsley, chopped
method: 1. Preheat oven to 180º C. | 2. To make Creole sauce, heat oil in a non-stick pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 3. Add and sauté till it turns translucent Add capsicum, mix and sauté. | 4. Add mushrooms, mix and sauté on lower heat for a minute. | 5. Add salt, cumin powder, paprika powder and sugar and mix well. | 6. Add tomatoes and mix well. | 7. Add ½ cup water, mix and cook on low heat for 2 minutes. | 8. Add 1 tablespoon chopped olives and lemon juice. | 9. Mix well and remove from heat. | 10. To make buttered rice, heat butter in another non-stick pan. | 11. Add rice and toss gently. | 12. Add saffron milk and salt. | 13. Mix well and let it get heated through and switch off the heat. | 14. Spread the buttered rice in a glass baking dish. | 15. Top with a layer of Creole sauce, cheese, remaining olives and chopped parsley. | 16. Put the dish in the preheated oven and bake for 5-8 minutes. | 17. Serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Crispy Crunchy Baby Corn
description: Bite-sized bursts of flavor, perfectly fried to golden perfection, offering a delightful crunch with every munch.
ingredients: 10- | 2 baby corns, blanched 1¼ teaspoon red chilli powder + to sprinkle ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder | 1 teaspoon garam masala powder ½ teaspoon crushed black peppercorns | 1 tablespoon oil + for deep frying | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 cup refined flour (maida) ½ cup cornflour A pinch of baking soda | 1 cup cornflakes Chaat masala to sprinkle Green chutney to serve
method: 1. Take baby corns in a bowl, add 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, turmeric powder, garam masala powder, crushed black peppercorns, and salt and mix well. | 2. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai Take refined flour, cornflour, baking soda, remaining red chilli powder and a pinch of salt in another bowl, then add a pinch of hing. | 3. Add ½ cup water and 1 tablespoon oil and mix well to make a thick batter. | 4. Crush the cornflakes and add to the batter and mix well. | 5. Dip baby corns in this batter and slide into hot oil and deep fry on medium heat till golden brown. | 6. Drain on absorbent paper. | 7. Sprinkle red chilli powder and chaat masala over them. | 8. Transfer in a serving basket and serve hot with green chutney.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Crispy Spinach and Coriander Bites
description: Kothimbir wadi is a well-loved Maharashtrian snack and here we have made it healthier with the addition of spinach
ingredients: 1 cup chopped spinach leaves | 2 cups chopped fresh coriander leaves | 2 tablespoons yogurt | 2 teaspoons oil + to grease ½ teaspoon mustard seeds ¼ teaspoon asafoetida | 1 teaspoon white sesame seeds (safed til) | 1 tablespoon scraped fresh coconut + for garnish
method: 1. Heat 4 cups water in a steamer. | 2. Mix coriander leaves, spinach and besan in a bowl. | 3. Add turmeric powder, ginger green chilli paste, salt, dried mango powder and yogurt and mix well. | 4. Add little water and make a dropping consistency mixture. | 5. Grease a thali with some oil. | 6. Transfer the mixture in it. | 7. Tap the thali to level the mixture. | 8. Place the thali in the steamer and steam for 12-15 minutes on medium heat. | 9. Release the steamed mixture from sides of the thali with a palette knife and invert the thali on the worktop. | 10. Trim the edges of the wadi. | 11. Cut into squares. | 12. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in a non-stick pan, add mustard seeds and let them splutter. | 13. Add asafoetida, white sesame seeds and wadis and cook on low heat for 2-3 minutes. | 14. Flip and cook till golden brown on both the sides. | 15. Add coconut and cook for few seconds. | 16. Switch off heat. | 17. Transfer the vadis onto a serving platter, garnish with scraped coconut and serve hot.

title: Dal Amboli
description: Discover the delightful flavors of Dal Amboli, a classic South Indian comfort food. This dish combines fluffy, fermented rice pancakes with a flavorful dal made from lentils, tomatoes, and aromatic...
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 cup short grain rice ½ cup pressed rice (poha) ¼ cup split pigeon peas (toovar dal) ¼ cup split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal) ¼ cup split green gram with skin (chilkewali moong dal) ¼ cup split Bengal gram (chana dal) ¼ cup split red lentils (masoor dal) | 1 tablespoon flax seeds | 2 teaspoons fenugreek seeds
method: 1. Take rice in a bowl. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Add pressed rice, pigeon peas, green gram, green gram with skin, Bengal gram, red lentils, flax seeds, and fenugreek seeds.Wash for 2-3 times. | 4. Add 3 cups buttermilk and soak for 6-8 hours. | 5. Transfer the mixture in a grinder and grind coarsely. | 6. Transfer in a bowl, add remaining buttermilk and mix well. | 7. Set aside to ferment for 8-10 hours. | 8. Add salt and mix well. | 9. Heat a cast iron tawa. | 10. Dip half in oil and grease tawa. | 11. Pour a ladleful of batter, drizzle oil, cover and cook for 3-4 minutes on medium heat. | 12. Flip and cook for 2-3 minutes more. | 13. Serve hot with coconut chutney.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Dal Baati Churma
description: This trio of sweet and savoury dishes on a platter is among the finest and most popular offerings from Rajasthan. Served with generous amounts of ghee is metaphorically a way of showing love to the...
ingredients: Baatis | 2 cups whole wheat flour (atta) | 4 tablespoons semolina ½ cup pure ghee + to deep fry + to serve | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 cup milk ¾ cup powdered sugar ¼ teaspoon green cardamom powder | 0 cashewnuts, chopped | 0 almonds, chopped | 0 raisins Dal | 2 cups cooked split red lentils (masoor dal) 2- | 3 tablespoons oil | 2 teaspoons cumin seeds | 3 green chillies, chopped 6- | 1 tablespoon red chilli powder | 2 teaspoons garam masala powder
method: 1. Preheat oven to 180° C. | 2. To make baatis, take whole wheat flour in a parat. | 3. Add semolina, salt, ½ cup ghee and mix well. | 4. Add 1¼ cups water knead into stiff dough. | 5. Divide the dough into small equal portions and roll into balls and arrange them on a baking tray. | 6. Bake them in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes. | 7. Dip the baked baatis in melted ghee and let them soak for 5-10 minutes. | 8. To make churma, mix whole wheat flour and semolina in a parat. | 9. Add ½ cup melted ghee and mix well. | 10. Add milk and knead into a stiff dough. | 11. Divide the dough into equal portions and shape into cylinders. | 12. Heat sufficient ghee in a kadai and deep-fry the balls on medium heat, till they are golden brown and crisp. | 13. Drain on absorbent paper and cool. | 14. Transfer in a grinder jar and grind coarsely Add powdered sugar, cardamom powder, cashew nuts, almonds and raisins and mix. | 15. The churma is ready. | 16. To make dal, heat oil in deep non-stick pan and add cumin seeds, then add a pinch of hing. | 17. Once they start to change the colour, add green chillies, and sauté for 30 seconds, Add red chilli powder, garam masala powder, tomato and sauté till tomatoes turn soft and pulpy. | 18. Add boiled dal, salt, 2 cups water and mix well. | 19. Cook on low heat for 5 minutes. | 20. Drizzle ghee on dal, baati, churma and serve hot with and green chillies.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Dal Dhokli
description: Dal Dhokli is an authentic Guajarati dish which is luscious in flavour and tastes best when served hot. A family favourite, it appeases all age groups
ingredients: 1 1/ | 2 cups split pigeon peas (tuvar dal) 1/ | 1 tablespoon raw peanuts | 2 tablespoons ghee | 1 teaspoon mustard seeds 1/ | 4 teaspoon fenugreek seeds (methi dana) 1/ | 4 teaspoon asafoetida | 2 dried red chillies, broken 16- | 8 curry leaves | 1 teaspoon ginger-green chilli paste | 1 teaspoon grated jaggery | 1 kokam petal, soaked in water for | 5 minutes | 1 tsp lemon juice+ for drizzling | 4 cup whole wheat flour (atta) 1/ | 4 teaspoon turmeric powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon red chilli powder A pinch of asafoetida 1/ | 2 teaspoon ginger-green chilli paste to apply + teaspoons Oil
method: 1. Pressure cook pigeon peas in some water with ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder, salt and peanuts till 3-4 whistles are given out. | 2. Open the lid when the pressure is completely released. | 3. To prepare dhokli, combine wheat flour and gram flour in a bowl. | 4. Add salt, ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder, chilli powder, asafoetida, and sufficient water and knead into a semi-soft dough. | 5. Add 2 teaspoons oil and knead again. | 6. Heat ghee in a deep non-stick pan, add mustard seeds and let them splutter. | 7. Add fenugreek seeds, asafoetida, broken dried red chillies, curry leaves and cooked pigeon peas and mix well. | 8. Add 1½ cups water and cook till the mixture comes to a boil. | 9. Cover and cook for 2-3 minutes. | 10. Divide the dough into equal portions, apply some oil on each portion and roll out into large thin discs. | 11. Transfer onto the worktop and cut each disc into diamonds. | 12. These are dhoklis. | 13. Add ginger-green chilli paste, and salt to pigeon peas mixture and mix well. | 14. Add dhokli pieces to the pan and mix. | 15. Add jaggery and kokum petal, mix, cover and cook for 10-15 minutes or till dhokli pieces are done. | 16. Switch off heat, add lemon juice and mix well. | 17. Transfer into a serving bowl, top with some and coriander leaves, drizzle some lemon juice and serve hot.

title: Dal Soya Parantha
