description: Paranthas stuffed with a highly flavoured and nutritious mixture of chana dal and soya chunks will leave you asking for more and more and…
ingredients: 4 cup Split Bengal gram (chana dal), soaked and boiled | 1 cup Soya chunks, soaked in hot water | 0 grams Readymade whole wheat flour dough | 1 tbsp + for basting Ghee | 1 teaspoon Fennel seeds (saunf) | 1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (jeera) | 1 teaspoon Carom seeds (ajwain) | 1 tablespoon Ginger, finely chopped 2- | 3 Green chillies, finely chopped | 1 teaspoon Red chilli powder | 1 tablespoon Coriander powder | 1 teaspoon Cumin powder | 1 teaspoon Garam masala powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon Black salt | 1 Dried mango powder (amchur) | 1 teaspoon Black peppercorns, crushed 10-
method: 1. Heat ghee in a non-stick pan, add fennel seeds, cumin seeds and carom seeds, mix and sauté for 1-2 minutes. | 2. Add ginger and green chillies, mix and sauté for 2-3 minutes. | 3. Add Bengal gram and soya chunks and mix. | 4. Add red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala powder, black salt, dried mango powder and crushed peppercorns, mix and cook for 3-4 minutes. | 5. Transfer the mixture into a blender jar and blend to a coarse paste. | 6. Transfer the paste into a bowl. | 7. Chop coriander leaves, add to the bowl and mix well. | 8. Add salt and mix again. | 9. Heat a non-stick tawa. | 10. Divide the dough into equal portions and roll each portion into a ball. | 11. Roll out each portion to a disc, place a portion of dal-soya mixture in the centre, bring edges together, press to seal and roll into a ball. | 12. Dust each stuffed ball with dry flour and roll out into a parantha. | 13. Place the paranthas on the hot tawa, one at a time, and cook, basting with ghee, till evenly cooked and golden on both sides. | 14. Transfer onto individual serving plates and serve hot with your choice of pickle.

title: Date and Coconut Ice Cream
description: Treat yourself to a delightful experience with Dates and Coconut Ice Cream.
ingredients: 10- | 2 dates ¼ cup cocoa powder | 2 teaspoons vanilla essence ½ cup tender coconut water | 0 grams tender coconut water
method: 1. Transfer dates in a bowl. | 2. Add sufficient water and soak for 2-3 hours. | 3. Deseed and transfer in a grinder jar. | 4. Add cocoa powder, vanilla essence, tender coconut water, tender coconut flesh and grind to a fine mixture. | 5. Transfer in an air tight container, close the lid and freeze for 6-8 hours. | 6. Scoop and serve chilled.

title: Deconstructed Vada Pav
description: A modern twist on the iconic Indian street food, featuring separated elements like spiced potato patties, bread, and spicy chutney for a unique culinary experience. This recipe is from Food Food TV...
ingredients: 8 slices of French baguette, toasted with butter till golden | 1 teaspoon oil ½ teaspoon mustard seeds | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 cup salted boondi Chives, halved for garnish
method: 1. Heat oil in a deep non-stick pan, add mustard seeds and curry leaves, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Once the mustard seeds splutter, add -green chilli-coriander leaves paste. | 3. Saute on medium heat till oil separates. | 4. Add turmeric powder and mix well. | 5. Add sweet potatoes and mix well. | 6. Add salt and sauté for a minute. | 7. Switch off heat. | 8. Place 2 baguette slices on a worktop. | 9. Spread some green chutney over them. | 10. Sprinkle vada pav chutney on top. | 11. Spread some cooked mixture in a dome shape on top. | 12. Take boondis in a bowl and roll the prepared bread slices, upside down, in it till well coated with boondi. | 13. Make more deconstructed vada pavs in the same way. | 14. Transfer deconstructed vada pavs on a serving platter. | 15. Serve immediately, garnished with chives.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Double Decker Parantha
description: Just one such parantha will satiate you completely stuffed as it is with masaledar rajma and paneer
ingredients: 1 cup Red kidney beans (rajma), soaked overnight, boiled and mashed | 1 teaspoon Garam masala powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon Red chilli powder 1/ | 4 teaspoon Dried mango powder (amchur_) 1/ | 4 teaspoon Chaat masala 1/ | 2 teaspoon Turmeric powder | 1 cup Cottage cheese (paneer), grated 1/ | 2 cup Mozzarella cheese, grated 1/ | 2 cup Cheddar cheese, grated | 1 tablespoon Fresh mint leaves, finely chopped to sprinkle Chaat masala to shallow fry Ghee grams Yogurt | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida)
method: 1. Take kidney beans in a bowl, add garam masala powder, red chilli powder, dried mango powder, chaat masala and turmeric powder and mix well. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Add salt and mix well. | 4. Take cottage cheese in another bowl, add mozzarella cheese, cheddar cheese, mint leaves and chaat masala and mix well. | 5. Divide the dough into 4 small equal portions, 4 slightly bigger equal portions and 4 bigger equal portions. | 6. To prepare the middle layer rotis, sprinkle flour on a worktop, take each small portion of dough and roll it thinly. | 7. Heat a non-stick tawa, put this roti on it and cook, turning sides, till light brown specs appear on both sides. | 8. To prepare the first layer rotis, sprinkle flour on the worktop, take each bigger portion and roll thinly, ensuring that it is bigger than the middle layer roti. | 9. To prepare the third layer rotis, sprinkle flour and take each slightly big portion of dough and roll thinly into a size that is bigger than the middle layer roti but smaller than the first layer roti. | 10. Take the first layer roti, spread spoonful of rajma mixture all over it. | 11. Place the middle layer cooked roti over it. | 12. Spread the cheese mixture all over it. | 13. Cover with third layer roti. | 14. Press completely. | 15. Heat the non-stick tawa again, place a parantha on it and cook, on medium heat, turning sides, till golden brown specs appear on both sides. | 16. Drizzle ghee and continue to cook for 30 seconds on each side. | 17. Make more paranthe similarly. | 18. Keep each parantha on separate serving plates and serve hot with yogurt.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Dum ka Tinda
description: Scooped out tindas stuffed with a spicy paneer mixture and cooked in a rich gravy
ingredients: 9 Apple gourds (tinda) | 1 teaspoon Ginger, chopped | 2 Green chillies, finely chopped | 1 cup Cottage cheese (paneer), grated | 4 bunch Fresh coriander leaves 3- | 4 tablespoons Sunflower oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 teaspoon Cumin seeds | 1 1/ | 3 medium Tomatoes, chopped | 2 tablespoons Coriander powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon Cumin powder | 1 teaspoon Garam masala powder | 2 teaspoons Dried mango powder (amchur) 3/ | 4 cups Cashewnuts, boiled | 2 inches Ginger, cut into thin strips 2- | 3 Green chillies, cut into thin strips | 1 inch cubes and layers separated | 6 Big green chillies, stems removed and halved 1/ | 4 teaspoon Turmeric powder 1/ | 4 cup Khoya, grated | 1 Lemon to garnish Fresh mint sprig
method: 1. Peel the apple gourds. | 2. Cut off a thin slice from the top and reserve them. | 3. Scoop out the inner pulp with a scooper. | 4. Take ginger, green chillies, cottage cheese, 1 tsp red chilli powder and salt in a bowl. | 5. Blend the scooped portion of apple gourds to a fine paste and add to the cottage cheese mixture. | 6. Chop coriander leaves and add and mix well. | 7. Stuff the apple gourds with this mixture. | 8. Heat a non-stick grill pan, place the stuffed apple gourds on it with the reserved sliced off tops. | 9. Add 2 tbsps sunflower oil and grill, turning sides, till golden on all sides and till grill marks appear. | 10. Heat remaining oil in a non-stick kadai, add cumin seeds and sauté till it changes colour, then add a pinch of hing. | 11. Add and sauté till it turns golden brown. | 12. Add and sauté for a minute. | 13. Add tomatoes and salt. | 14. Cook till tomatoes turn pulpy. | 15. Sprinkle a little salt over the grilled apple gourds. | 16. Add coriander powder, 1 tsp red chilli powder, cumin powder, garam masala powder, 1 tsp dried mango powder in the kadai and sauté for 2-3 minutes. | 17. Add 10-12 cashewnuts and sauté till oil separates. | 18. Switch off heat. | 19. Let the mixture cool down.Transfer the mixture into blender jar and blend to a fine paste. | 20. In same kadai, add ginger strips, green chilli strips, and halved big green chillies and sauté for 1-2 minutes. | 21. Add the apple gourds and cook on medium heat for 4-5 minutes. | 22. Add remaining red chilli powder, turmeric powder and remaining dried mango powder. | 23. Add sufficient water. | 24. Cover and cook on medium heat for 4-5 minutes. | 25. Add sufficient water and add the ground paste. | 26. Add khoya, remaining cashewnuts, remaining ginger strips and juice of ½ lemon. | 27. Cover and cook on medium heat for 4-5 minutes. | 28. Transfer the apple gourds into a serving bowl along with the chillies and gravy. | 29. Serve hot garnished with cashewnuts and mint sprig.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Easy Lentil Salad

Transfer into a bowl, add the sesame seeds mixture and jaggery syrup and mix well Divide the mixture into equal portions and shape each portion into a laddoo and set aside on a plate to cool down to room temperature. | 9. Once cooled completely store in an airtight container or serve.

title: Jain Corn Tamatar Nu Shaak
description: Spicy, creamy and yet superbly Jain, even without the onion and garlic. Serve hot with parathas. This is
ingredients: 1 corn on the cob | 4 medium tomatoes, roughly chopped Gravy 2- | 3 tablespoons oil | 1 inch cinnamon stick 4- | 5 cloves 4- | 5 black peppercorns | 1 dried red chilli | 1 tablespoon melon seeds | 2 medium tomatoes, finely chopped | 2 tablespoons oil | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds ¼ teaspoon asafoetida (hing) | 1 medium green capsicum, finely chopped 2- | 3 green chillies, chopped | 1 teaspoon coriander powder ½ teaspoon garam masala powder | 1 teaspoon sugar | 2 tablespoons fresh cream ¼ teaspoon green cardamom powder | 1 teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) | 1 teaspoon lemon juice 1½ tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves Fresh coriander sprig for garnish Paranthe to serve
method: 1. Roast the corn on the cob on direct flame till well charred from all sides. | 2. Place the corn on the worktop and remove the corn kernels with the help of a knife. | 3. Put the corn kernels into a blender jar. | 4. Blend to a coarse paste. | 5. Heat oil in a non-stick pan. | 6. Add cinnamon stick, cloves, black peppercorns, and dried red chilli and sauté for a few seconds. | 7. Add tomatoes and mix well. | 8. Cook till soft and pulpy. | 9. Add melon seeds and roasted peanuts and mix well. | 10. Cook for 1-2 minutes. | 11. Add turmeric powder and salt and mix well. | 12. Add ½ cup water, mix and cook for 2-3 minutes. | 13. Switch the heat off, discard the cinnamon stick and allow the mixture to cool slightly. | 14. Put the mixture into a blender jar and blend to a fine paste. | 15. For the tempering, heat oil in a non-stick pan. | 16. Add cumin seeds and asafoetida and mix well. | 17. Add green capsicum and mix, cook for 1-2 minutes. | 18. Add green chillies, and tomatoes and mix well. | 19. Cook for 1-2 minutes. | 20. Add blended paste, mix and cook for 1 minute. | 21. Add blended corn mixture and mix well. | 22. Add 1 cup water and mix. | 23. Add coriander powder, garam masala powder, cumin powder, chaat masala, and red chilli powder and mix well. | 24. Cook for 2-3 minutes. | 25. Add cottage cheese and mix. | 26. Add salt, sugar and fresh cream and mix well. | 27. Add green cardamom powder, dried fenugreek leaves and lemon juice and mix well. | 28. Add coriander leaves, mix and switch the heat off. | 29. Transfer the mixture into a serving bowl. | 30. Garnish with coriander sprig. | 31. Serve hot with paranthe.

title: Jain Mix Vegetable Makhani
description: With coconut cream ,semi-ripe bananas and assorted vegetables , this makhani recipe gets a Jain twist. This is
ingredients: 10- | 2 small cauliflower florets, boiled and drained 6- | 8 French beans, cut into | 1 inch pieces, blanched and drained ¼ cup boiled green peas | 1 medium raw banana, boiled, peeled and cut into | 1 inch cubes | 2 tablespoons oil 5- | 6 black peppercorns | 1 inch cinnamon stick 2- | 3 cloves | 3 green cardamoms | 1 tablespoon red chilli paste | 1 teaspoon sugar | 1 tablespoon butter ¼ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves powder Fresh coriander leaves for garnish
method: 1. Heat oil in a non-stick pan. | 2. Add black peppercorns, cinnamon stick, cloves, green cardamoms, and mix well. | 3. Cook till fragrant. | 4. Add red chilli paste, mix and cook for 1-2 minutes. | 5. Add tomato puree, mix and cook till the oil separates. | 6. Add salt, turmeric powder and mix well. | 7. Cook for 1-2 minutes. | 8. Stir in the coconut cream and cook for 1-2 minutes. | 9. Add sugar and mix, cook till it melts. | 10. Add cauliflower, French beans, green peas, and raw bananas and mix well. | 11. Cook for 2-3 minutes. | 12. Add butter and mix well. | 13. Cook for 1-2 minutes. | 14. Add dried fenugreek leaves powder and mix well. | 15. Transfer into a serving bowl. | 16. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. | 17. Serve hot.

title: Jain Pakoda
description: Simple and delicious pakodas made with cabbage and gram flour. Simply irresistible ! In this Jain version, onions are replaced with cabbage . This is
ingredients: 1 small cabbage, thinly shredded | 1 cup gram flour (besan) | 2 tablespoons rice flour | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves | 1 teaspoon sugar ¼ teaspoon asafoetida (hing) | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder ½ teaspoon crushed black peppercorns | 2 teaspoons oil + for deep frying Jain green chutney to serve
method: 1. Take cabbage in a large bowl. | 2. Add gram flour, rice flour, coriander leaves, sugar, asafoetida, red chilli powder, crushed black peppercorns, lemon juice, salt, and oil and mix well. | 3. Add ½ cup water and mix till a thick mixture is formed. | 4. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 5. Gently slide in small portions of the mixture and deep fry till golden brown and crisp. | 6. Drain on an absorbent paper. | 7. Arrange the pakodas on a serving plate and serve hot with Jain green chutney.

title: Jain Seekh Kebab
description: Finger food gets a Jain twist. The joy of a pure veg seekh kebab with veggies and raisins that are very succulent. This is
ingredients: 4 medium raw bananas, boiled, peeled and mashed | 4 tablespoons oil 2- | 3 green chillies, finely chopped ½ cup finely chopped cabbage ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder | 5 raisins (kismis) | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves | 1 tablespoons finely chopped fresh mint leaves | 2 tablespoons roasted gram flour Cabbage salad to serve Jain green chutney to serve Fresh mint leaves for garnish Lemon wedges to serve
method: 1. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a non-stick pan. | 2. Add green chillies and cabbage and mix well. | 3. Add turmeric powder and mix well. | 4. Cook for 1 minute. | 5. Take the pan off the heat and allow to cool slightly. | 6. Take boiled raw banana in a large bowl, add the cabbage mixture, red chilli powder, chaat masala, salt, green cardamom powder, raisins, coriander leaves, mint leaves, and roasted gram flour and mix till well combined. | 7. Dip your fingers in some water and take a portion of the mixture and spread it on to a satay stick and shape it like a seekh. | 8. Heat remaining oil in a non-stick pan. | 9. Place the seekh on it and cook for 3-4 minutes, rotating the seekh in between till golden brown on all sides. | 10. Arrange the seekhs on a serving plate, garnish with mint leaves and serve hot with salad, Jain green chutney, and lemon wedges.

title: Jeera Kulcha Biscuits
description: The delightful fusion of Indian spices and buttery goodness in every crunchy nibble.
ingredients: 1 teaspoon cumin seeds Caraway seeds (shahi jeera) to sprinkle | 0 grams salted butter, softened | 0 grams powdered sugar | 1 teaspoon vanilla essence | 0 grams refined flour (maida) | 0 grams rice flour | 2 tablespoons chopped mixed nuts (almonds and pistachios) + to sprinkle White sesame seeds (safed til) to sprinkle Sea salt to sprinkle
method: 1. Preheat oven to 180º C. | 2. Take butter and powdered sugar in a bowl and cream with a wooden spoon till the mixture becomes light in colour. | 3. Add vanilla essence and cumin seeds and mix well. | 4. Sift together refined flour, rice flour into the butter mixture. | 5. Add mixed nuts and mix well. | 6. Sprinkle little water and make medium soft dough. | 7. Divide the dough into equal portions and shape into thick discs. | 8. Grease a baking tray with oil. | 9. Place the discs on it. | 10. Sprinkle caraway seeds, white sesame seeds and sea salt and press lightly. | 11. Sprinkle chopped mixed nuts and press lightly. | 12. Place the tray in the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes. | 13. Allow to cool. | 14. Arrange on a serving platter and serve.

title: Jowar Pumpkin Gnocchi
description: A wholesome twist on classic gnocchi, this dish blends sweet potato, pumpkin, and sorghum flour, tossed in a fragrant garlic-herb pumpkin sauce and topped with parmesan.
ingredients: 1 1/ | 2 cups sorghum flour (jowar ka atta) | 2 cups boiled pumpkin puree | 3 tablespoons olive oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 tablespoon chopped fresh mixed herbs 8- | 0 fresh basil leaves + for garnish | 1 teaspoon red chilli flakes | 0 grams parmesan cheese
method: 1. Take sorghum flour in a mixing bowl, add sweet potatoes and mash with a fork. | 2. Add 1 cup pumpkin puree, salt and crushed black peppercorns, and knead into a dough. | 3. To make gnocchi, take small portions of the dough and shape them into cylinders. | 4. Flatten the edges. | 5. Heat sufficient water in a deep non-stick deep pan and let it come to a boil, then add a pinch of hing. | 6. Drop in the gnocchis into the boiling water and cook till they float on the surface. | 7. Meanwhile to make the sauce, heat oil in a shallow non-stick pan, add and sauté well. | 8. Add the remaining pumpkin puree and mix well. | 9. Strain the cooked gnocchi and add to the sauce and toss gently till they are well coated with the sauce. | 10. Add salt and mixed herbs. | 11. Tear basil leaves and add to the gnocchi. | 12. Add red chilli flakes and gently toss on high heat. | 13. Transfer into a serving plate, grate parmesan cheese on top and garnish with basil leaves. | 14. Serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Kadai Kala Chana
description: Freshly ground kadai masala makes this kala chana dish extra special. Brown Bengal gram cooked with Indian spices.
ingredients: 2 cups brown Bengal gram (kala chana), soaked overnight and boiled | 1 tablespoon coriander seeds | 1 teaspoon fennel seeds | 4 dried red chillies | 2 teaspoons cumin seeds | 3 tablespoons mustard oil | 2 bay leaves | 2 green chillies, finely chopped 1/ | 2 cup tomato puree | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder | 1 1/ | 2 teaspoons rried mango powder (amchur) 1/ | 2 teaspoon turmeric powder | 2 tablespoons coriander powder | 2 medium tomatoes | 1 inch ginger, cut into thin strips + to garnish 8- | 0 fresh coriander sprigs to garnish 1/ | 2 tablespoon lemon juice
method: 1. To make the kadai masala, dry roast coriander seeds, fennel seeds, 2 dried chillies and 1 tsp cumin seeds till fragrant. | 2. Remove from heat, cool and grind to a coarse powder. | 3. Heat mustard oil in a non-stick kadai till it begins to smoke. | 4. Cool it slightly, add bay leaves and remaining dried chillies and sauté for 10 seconds. | 5. Add green chillies and remaining cumin seeds, mix and sauté for 2-3 minutes. | 6. Add tomato puree, mix well and cook for 2 minutes. | 7. Add red chilli powder, dried mango powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder and chaat masala and mix well. | 8. Add salt and mix. | 9. Roughly chop tomatoes, add, mix and cook till they turn soft and pulpy. | 10. Add ginger strips and mix well. | 11. Add brown Bengal gram and mix. | 12. Add water as required, mix and cook for 5-6 minutes. | 13. Chop some coriander sprigs, add and mix. | 14. Add the kadai masala and lemon juice and mix well. | 15. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with ginger strips and coriander sprigs and serve hot.

title: Kaddu ki Khatti Meethi Subzi
description: Red pumpkin is a vegetable not many like to eat, but when cooked this way they will not be able to resist
ingredients: 0 grams red pumpkin (kaddu), peeled and cut into | 1 inch cubes | 3 tablespoons tamarind paste 1/ | 2 cup grated jaggery | 2 tablespoons oil | 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger, 1- | 2 green chillies, finely chopped 1/ | 4 teaspoon asafoetida 1/ | 4 teaspoon turmeric powder | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder | 1 tablespoon chopped fresh coriander leaves | 1 green chilli, slit Fresh coriander sprig for garnish
method: 1. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add ginger and green chillies, mix and sauté till fragrant. | 2. Add asafoetida, mix and sauté for a minute. | 3. Add pumpkin cubes and cook for 4-5 minutes. | 4. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, 1 tsp garam masala powder and salt, mix well and sauté for a few minutes. | 5. Add tamarind paste and jaggery, mix and cook till the jaggery melts. | 6. Add some water, mix, cover and cook for 8-10 minutes. | 7. Mash the pumpkins slightly, add coriander leaves and remaining garam masala powder and mix well. | 8. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with slit green chilli and coriander sprig and serve hot.

title: Kalakand Fruit Custard
description: If you have lots of leftover kalakand, don’t despair. Just follow this recipe and turn out a unique dessert
ingredients: 5 pieces Kalakand | 1 Red apple, cored, cut into 1/ | 2 inch pieces and soaked in wa | 1 Pear, cored and cut into 1/ | 2 inch pieces | 2 1/ | 2 tablespoons Custard powder 15- | 0 Walnuts | 1 1/ | 2 cups Milk 4- | 5 tablespoons Caster sugar to garnish Orange segments to garnish Fresh pomegranate pears to garnish Red grapes, seeded and halved
method: 1. Roughly chop walnuts. | 2. Dry roast the walnuts in a non-stick pan till crisp. | 3. Transfer into a bowl. | 4. Take milk in another bowl, add custard powder and mix well. | 5. Transfer the custard mixture into a deep non-stick pan and cook on medium heat, whisking continuously till the mixture starts thickening and reaches pouring consistency. | 6. Add castor sugar. | 7. Break kalakand pieces and add and mix well with a whisk till the mixture thickens further. | 8. Add half the roasted walnuts and apple and mix well. | 9. Pour half the custard into a deep serving plate. | 10. Add pears and pour the remaining custard mixture. | 11. Garnish with orange segments, pomegranate pearls, remaining roasted walnuts and red grapes. | 12. Refrigerate for 1-2 hours or till set. | 13. Serve chilled.

title: Kale Chane ki Kadhi
description: Kale chane simmered in a flavoured curd gravy and served with steamed rice is an absolute soul food. This is
ingredients: 1 cup boiled brown chickpeas (kala chana) 1½ cups yogurt | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder | 2 teaspoons coriander powder | 1 teaspoon garam masala powder | 2 green chillies, chopped | 2 tablespoons ghee | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds ¼ teaspoon asafoetida (hing) | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves Red chilli oil for drizzling Steamed rice to serve
method: 1. Take yogurt in a large bowl, add gram flour, turmeric powder, salt, red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder and green chillies and whisk till a lump free mixture is formed. | 2. Add 3 cups water and mix well. | 3. Heat ghee in a deep pan. | 4. Add cumin seeds and let them change colour. | 5. Add asafoetida and cook for a few seconds. | 6. Add boiled brown chickpeas and mix well. | 7. Add the yogurt mixture and mix well. | 8. Cook for 10-15 minutes or till done. | 9. Add coriander leaves, mix and cook for 1 minute. | 10. Transfer the kadhi into a serving bowl and serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Kale Chane with Rajgira Puri
description: Whole black gram soaked and cooked with simple spices. This flavourful bowl is best had with rajgira puris. This recipe featured on Food Food Channel.
ingredients: 1½ cups black Bengal gram (kale chane), soaked overnight, drained and boiled in fresh water | 1 cup amaranth (rajgira) flour | 1 tablespoon + | 1 teaspoon oil + for deep-frying | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds | 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger
method: 1. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a non-stick pan, add cumin seeds and once the seeds change colour, add ginger and sauté for ½ a minute. | 2. Add coriander powder and mix well. | 3. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 4. Add black gram, black salt, red chilli powder and black Bengal gram stock to the pan and mix well. | 5. Cook till the black gram is done. | 6. Put amaranth flour into a bowl, grate potato into it. | 7. Add salt and mix well. | 8. Add ½ cup water and knead into a soft dough. | 9. Add 1 tsp oil and knead again. | 10. Divide the dough into equal portions, shape them into balls and roll out into small pooris. | 11. Deep-fry pooris in hot oil till brown and crisp on both the sides. | 12. Drain on absorbent paper. | 13. Transfer the chane into a serving bowl, place it on one side of the serving plate, place fried pooris on the other side and serve immediately.

title: Kapnya
description: Traditional Maharashtrian snack – ideally should be served with cups of hot tea
ingredients: 1 cup Whole wheat flour (gehun ka atta) 1/ | 4 cup Jaggery, chopped 1/ | 4 cup Gram flour (besan) a pinch Salt 1/ | 2 tablespoon Fennel sees (saunf) 1/ | 2 tbsp + to deep fry Hot oil to sprinkle Poppy seeds
method: 1. Heat ¼ cup water in a deep non-stick pan, add jaggery and cook till it melts. | 2. Take whole wheat flour in a bowl, add gram flour, salt, fennel seeds and hot oil and mix well. | 3. Add the jaggery mixture and knead into stiff dough. | 4. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 5. Roll out the dough into a thin disk and sprinkle poppy seeds on top. | 6. Press lightly. | 7. Cut into diamonds, slide them into hot oil and deep fry, on medium heat, till crisp. | 8. Drain on absorbent paper. | 9. Allow to cool and serve or store in airtight containers.

title: Karachi Halwa
description: Chewy rich halwa. This recipe has featured on the show Khanakhazana.
ingredients: 1 1/ | 4 cup Cornflour Sugar | 2 cups Sugar | 1 teaspoon Lemon juice 1- | 2 drops Orange essence | 0 grams Ghee a few drops Rose water 10- | 2 Almonds finely chopped 10- | 2 Pistachios finely chopped
method: 1. Dissolve cornflour in three fourth cup of water. | 2. Grease a tray. | 3. Mix sugar with one cup of water and bring it to a boil. | 4. Add lemon juice, bring it to a boil again and then remove scum. | 5. Add edible orange colour and mix. | 6. Reduce heat to medium, add a little cornflour mixture and stir continuously. | 7. When it boils add a little cornflour mixture again. | 8. Continue the same process until all the cornflour mixture is used up. | 9. Cook till mixture is thickened and starts sticking. | 10. Reduce heat to low. | 11. Add a little melted ghee to it and mix well. | 12. When ghee is absorbed, add a little more and let it absorb. | 13. Continue till all the ghee is used up. | 14. Take one teaspoon of mixture onto a plate, cool it and if it does not stick to the plate, remove pan from heat. | 15. Add rose water, mix well and then spread it on a greased tray. | 16. Sprinkle chopped almonds and pistachios on top. | 17. Cut into desired size and shape. | 18. Serve when cool. | 19. Nutrition Info Calories 3434 Carbohydrates 576.8 Protein 21.9 Fat 115.

title: Kayloli
description: Dosa with a twist! Enjoy a tantalizing snack with spiced batter, promising a deliciously unique flavor experience.
ingredients: 1½ cups rice flour ½ cup semolina (rawa)
method: 1. Take rice flour in a bowl, add semolina, green chillies, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt and mix well. | 2. Add milk, 1½ cups water and mix well. | 3. Set aside for 10-15 minutes. | 4. Heat a non-stick tawa. | 5. Spread a ladleful of batter evenly. | 6. Drizzle some oil around and cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes or till the underside is golden brown. | 7. Flip and cook on other side for 2-3 minutes more. | 8. Serve hot with coconut chutney.

title: Kharzi
description: Check out this cheesy and easy Rice recipe kharzi from Arunachal Pradesh. Mozzarella cheese, chillies and ginger are blended to make a fine paste. Then cooked rice is tossed in this paste along with...
ingredients: 1½ cups grated mozzarella cheese | 5 dried red chillies, stemmed and soaked | 1 inch ginger, chopped | 2 tablespoons oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 3 cups cooked rice | 1 teaspoon red chilli flakes
method: 1. Transfer mozzarella cheese in a blender jar. | 2. Drain dried red chillies and add along with ginger. | 3. Add ¾ cup water and grind to a fine paste. | 4. Heat oil in a nonstick pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 5. Add and sauté for a minute. | 6. Add the prepared paste and sauté for 3-4 minutes. | 7. Add salt, crushed black peppercorns and mix well. | 8. Add rice and mix well. | 9. Cook for 2-3 minutes. | 10. Add red chilli flakes and mix well. | 11. Serve hot garnished with .
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Kurmura Energy Bars
description: An excellent snack to be had when hunger pangs strike in between meals – full of nutrients it is very healthy.
ingredients: 3 cups puffed rice (kurmura) ¾ cup chopped jaggery | 2 tablespoons ghee + for greasing ¼ cup creamy peanut butter | 1 tablespoon mixed fruit jam ¼ cup crushed roasted peanuts | 1 cup melted dark chocolate Salt for garnishing Walnuts for garnishing Roasted and peeled peanuts for garnishing Roasted Bengal gram for garnishing Fresh coriander sprigs for garnishing
method: 1. Heat a non-stick wok, add jaggery, ghee and peanut butter and cook until jaggery melts.Add jam and mix well, add puffed rice, crushed peanuts and mix well. | 2. Grease an aluminum tray with ghee. | 3. Transfer the mixture onto the tray and spread it evenly, roll a rolling pin over it to level the top and set aside until it sets.Demould and cut into thick sticks and then cut each stick into half.Dip half the bar pieces in melted dark chocolate and place it on a wire rack, sprinkle some salt on it. | 4. Garnish the rest with walnuts, peanuts, roasted Bengal gram and coriander sprigs.

title: Kuttu ka Parantha
description: A healthy and wholesome buckwheat flour paratha. Kuttu ka paratha is a flatbread that can be eaten with some condiment or as it is alone. This is
ingredients: 1 cup buckwheat flour (kuttu) | 1 tablespoon ginger-green chilli paste Ghee as required
method: 1. Grate potatoes in a bowl. | 2. Add chopped coriander, peanuts, rock salt, ginger-green chilli paste and buckwheat flour and mix well. | 3. Add some water and knead into a stiff dough. | 4. Cover with a damp muslin cloth and set aside for 15 minutes. | 5. Divide the dough into equal portions and shape them into balls. | 6. Put some ghee on a non-stick tawa. | 7. Place a ball over, dip palms in water and spread the ball to make a thin disc. | 8. Switch on the heat, spread some ghee, cover and cook from both sides on medium heat till golden and crisp. | 9. Serve hot.

title: Lachchedar Aloo Tikki Chaat
description: A very popular chaat in North India – potato tikkis served with yogurt and other chaat ingredients.
ingredients: 4 large potatoes, boiled and peeled | 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger | 1 teaspoon chaat masala + to sprinkle | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder + to sprinkle | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds ½ teaspoon carom seeds (ajwain) | 2 tablespoons raisins Ghee for shallow frying 1½ cups thick yogurt, beaten 1½ tablespoons powdered sugar | 1 teaspoon rose water Tamarind chutney as required Green chutney as required Nylon sev as required Pomegranate pearls as required Fresh mint sprig for garnish
method: 1. Grate potatoes into a bowl. | 2. Add ginger, green chillies, salt, chaat masala, red chilli powder, cumin seeds, carom seeds and raisins and mix the mixture lightly. | 3. Divide the mixture equal portions and shape them into tikkis. | 4. Heat ghee in a pan, add the tikkis and shallow fry, turning sides, on medium heat till evenly golden brown on both sides. | 5. They should be soft from inside and crisp on the outside. | 6. Take yogurt in a bowl, add powdered sugar and rose water and mix well.Drain tikkis on absorbent paper. | 7. Spread a spoon of yogurt mixture on a serving platter with back of a spoon. | 8. Place tikkis over it, drizzle yogurt, a spoon of tamarind chutney and few drops of green chutney over the tikkis. | 9. Sprinkle sev and add pomegranate pearls on top. | 10. Sprinkle chaat masala and red chilli powder. | 11. Garnish with a mint sprig and serve immediately.

title: Lachchedar Mathri
description: Crispy layers of indulgence, boasting a flaky texture and a tantalizing blend of spices for a snack that's both crunchy and addictive.
ingredients: 2 cups refined flour (maida) + for dusting | 1 teaspoon carom seeds (ajwain) | 6 tablespoons ghee + as required Green Thai curry paste as required Red Thai curry paste as required Oil for greasing and brushing
method: 1. Preheat oven to 180˚ C. | 2. Mix together refined flour, carom seeds, crushed peppercorns, salt and 6 tablespoons ghee in a bowl. | 3. Add water as required and knead into a stiff dough. | 4. Divide the dough into two equal portions and roll out each into a thin sheet. | 5. Spread some green Thai curry paste on one sheet, top with some ghee, dust some flour and roll into a cylinder. | 6. Spread some red Thai curry paste on the other sheet, top with some ghee, dust some flour and roll into a cylinder. | 7. Cut both cylinders into 1 inch pieces and flatten out each piece into small discs or mathris. | 8. Grease a baking tray with some oil and place the mathris on it. | 9. Brush the mathris with ghee on top. | 10. Place the tray in the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes. | 11. Remove from oven, brush some ghee, flip and brush some more ghee on mathris. | 12. Put the tray back in the preheated oven and bake for 5-6 minutes. | 13. Remove from oven and cool. | 14. Arrange the mathris on a serving platter and serve or store in an airtight container to serve when required.

title: Leftover Dal Appe
description: Wondering how to give your leftover dal a makeover? We’ve got the perfect recipe for you! Transform it into crispy and delicious 'Dal Appe' by following this simple and easy recipe.
ingredients: 1½ cups leftover dal ¼ cup blanched and crushed green peas ¼ cup grated carrots | 1 cup semolina (suji) | 3 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves 2- | 3 tablespoons yogurt ½ teaspoon baking soda Ghee as required Coconut chutney to serve
method: 1. Take leftover dal in a bowl. | 2. Add green peas, carrots, semolina, green chillies, salt, coriander, yogurt and 1 cup water and mix well. | 3. Set aside for 10-15 minutes. | 4. Add baking soda and mix well. | 5. Heat a paniyaram pan and drizzle ghee. | 6. Put a spoonful of batter in each dent, cover and cook for 5-6 minutes. | 7. Flip and cook for 5-6 minutes more. | 8. Serve hot with coconut chutney.

title: Leftover Rice Muthia
description: A delicious snack using leftover rice, gram flour and a few Indian spices to enjoy during your chai-time. This is
ingredients: 2 cups leftover cooked rice ½ cup gram flour (besan) | 1 teaspoon sugar ¼ cup yogurt A pinch of baking soda | 2 teaspoons oil | 3 tablespoons whole wheat flour Tempering | 1 tablespoon oil | 1 teaspoon mustard seeds | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds ½ tablespoon white sesame seeds A pinch of asafoetida (hing) 10- | 5 curry leaves 2- | 3 green chillies, slit | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves
method: 1. Take rice in a bowl, add gram flour, ginger-green chilli paste, coriander powder, salt, turmeric powder, chilli powder, cumin powder, sugar and yogurt and mix well. | 2. Add baking soda and oil, mix well. | 3. Add whole wheat flour and knead into soft dough. | 4. To make muthia, grease your palms with oil, take a portion of the dough and shape into a cylinder. | 5. Heat sufficient water in a steamer. | 6. Place the muthia in the steamer basket and steam for 12-15 minutes. | 7. Switch the heat off and allow to cool completely. | 8. Cut muthiya into 1 inch roundels. | 9. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add mustard seeds once they splutter, add cumin seeds, sesame seeds, asafoetida, curry leaves and green chillies and sauté for a few seconds. | 10. Add muthiya roundels and coriander leaves and toss well. | 11. Take the pan off the heat and transfer into a serving bowl. | 12. Serve hot.

title: Leftover Roti Halwa
description: You wont believe that you can make such a delicious and amazing halwa with your leftover Rotis. It is a very quick and simple recipe. This is
ingredients: 8 leftover rotis | 2 tablespoons ghee | 1 cup milk ½ cup sugar ¼ teaspoon green cardamom powder A pinch of salt | 1 tablespoon chopped almonds | 1 tablespoon chopped cashew nuts | 1 tablespoon chopped pistachios + for garnish
method: 1. Put the rotis in a blender jar and blend to a coarse powder. | 2. Heat ghee in a non-stick pan, add the bended powder and sauté for 2-3 minutes. | 3. Add milk, mix and cook for 2-3 minutes. | 4. Add sugar, mix and cook for 3-4 minutes. | 5. Add almonds, cashew nuts and pistachios and mix well. | 6. Cook for 1-2 minutes. | 7. Serve warm garnished with pistachios.

title: Leftover Upma Cutlets
description: Got leftover upma? Make these stunning tikkis and enjoy a fabulous snack! Serve these cutlets hot with tomato ketchup. This is
ingredients: 1½ cups leftover rava upma | 1 tablespoon oil + for shallow frying | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 tablespoon butter 2- | 1 green chilli, chopped | 1 medium carrot, chopped | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves ½ lemon ¼ cup bread crumbs Tomato ketchup to serve
method: 1. Heat oil and butter in a non-stick pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add and green chilli and sauté for a few seconds. | 3. Add and sauté for a few seconds. | 4. Add cabbage, carrot, capsicum and a pinch of salt and sauté for 1-2 minutes. | 5. Add crushed black peppercorns, chaat masala, and red chilli flakes and roasted cumin powder and mix well. | 6. Take the pan off the heat and allow this mixture to cool slightly. | 7. Take the leftover upma in a large bowl. | 8. Add the vegetable mixture, coriander leaves, and squeeze the juice of lemon. | 9. Add bread crumbs and mix well. | 10. Take a portion of the mixture and shape it into a ball and gently flatten it to make triangle shaped cutlets. | 11. Heat sufficient oil in a shallow pan. | 12. Place the cutlets and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side till golden brown and crisp. | 13. Drain on an absorbent paper. | 14. Serve hot with tomato ketchup.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Lotus Stem Honey Crunch
description: Indulge in the tantalizing combination of crispy lotus stem slices delicately coated in the rich sweetness of honey.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon cornflour | 2 tablespoons oil + to deep fry | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 6 dried Kashmiri red chillies | 3 tablespoons tomato ketchup | 3 tablespoons honey 3- | 1 teaspoon vinegar
method: 1. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Cut lotus stems into thin diagonal slices and put them in a bowl. | 3. Add salt, cornflour and mix well. | 4. Slide these into hot oil, taking care not to crowd the kadai, and deep fry till crisp and golden. | 5. Drain on absorbent paper. | 6. Roughly chop red chillies and remove seeds. | 7. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a non stick wok, add red chillies and sauté. | 8. Add tomato ketchup, honey and salt and mix. | 9. Chop . | 10. Add fried lotus stems to the sauce and toss. | 11. Add vinegar and mix. | 12. Transfer into a serving dish, garnish with and serve hot. | 13. Nutrition Info Calories 573 Carbohydrates 69.3 Protein 5.2 Fat 30.5 Other Fiber Vitamin C- 55mg.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Malai Broccoli
description: Broccoli florets marinated with fresh cream and some spices and cooked on the barbecue
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 0 large broccoli florets, blanched | 2 teaspoons mustard sauce 2- | 3 tablespoons milk Salad to serve
method: 1. Take fresh cream and salt in a large bowl and mix well. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Add crushed black peppercorns and mix well. | 4. Squeeze the juice of ½ lemon and mix well. | 5. Add broccoli pieces and mix well so that all the broccoli florets are well coated with the marinade. | 6. Light sufficient charcoal in a barbeque, meanwhile, skewer the broccoli pieces on skewers and place them on the barbeque grill and cook for 10-12 minutes. | 7. To make the dressing, take cream cheese, black peppercorns and mustard sauce in a bowl and mix well. | 8. Add milk and mix. | 9. Drizzle a little dressing on the broccoli and cook for 1-2 minutes. | 10. Take them off the barbecue. | 11. Arrange the broccoli florets on a serving plate and drizzle remaining dressing over them and serve hot with salad.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Masala Aloo Kulcha + Tamatar ki Chutney
description: Kulchas stuffed with a spicy potato mixture and cooked and served with a pungent tomato chutney – absolutely delicious
ingredients: 1 cup whole wheat flour (atta) ½ cup refined flour (maida) + for dusting | 2 medium potatoes, boiled and peeled | 1 tablespoon coriander seeds | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds (jeera) | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger 2- | 3 green chillies, finely chopped | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder | 1 teaspoon dried mango powder (amchur) | 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh coriander leaves | 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh mint leaves | 1 teaspoon garam masala powder | 1 tablespoon oilTamatar ki chutney | 2 medium tomatoes (tamatar), finely chopped | 3 tablespoons tomato ketchup | 1 teaspoon chaat masala Juice of ½ lemon | 2 tablespoons finely chopped fresh coriander leaves | 2 teaspoons sugar
method: 1. Mix together wheat flour, refined flour, carom seeds, salt and 2 tablespoons oil in a parat. | 2. Add water as required and knead a soft dough. | 3. Add remaining oil, knead and set aside. | 4. To make the filling, dry roast coriander seeds and cumin seeds till fragrant, then add a pinch of hing. | 5. Crush in a mortar with pestle to a coarse powder and put in a bowl. | 6. Add ginger, green chillies, chilli powder, dried mango powder, coriander leaves, mint leaves, potatoes and garam masala powder. | 7. Mash and mix well. | 8. Add oil and mix again. | 9. To make the tamatar ki chutney, mix together tomatoes, tomato ketchup, chilli powder, dried mango powder, chaat masala, lemon juice, coriander leaves and sugar in a bowl. | 10. Place an aluminium kadai upside down on heat. | 11. Divide the dough into equal portions and brush with some oil. | 12. Stuff each with a portion of the filling and shape into balls. | 13. Dust the worktop with some refined and sprinkle some coriander leaves on it. | 14. Place the stuffed balls on it and flatten them into discs. | 15. Apply some oil on the inverted kadai, stick the kulchas on it and roast till fully done, basting with oil. | 16. Serve hot with tamatar ki chutney.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Masala Rawa Puri
description: These super crisp puris are made with a dough of rawa and some spice powders
ingredients: 1 cup Fine semolina (barik rawa) | 2 teaspoons Ginger-green chilli paste | 1 teaspoon Red chilli powder | 1 teaspoon Coriander powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon Cumin powder 1/
method: 1. Put the semolina in a mixer jar and grind to a fine powder. | 2. Transfer into a large bowl. | 3. Add ginger-green chilli paste, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder and salt and mix well. | 4. Add ½ cup water and knead into soft dough. | 5. Cover with a damp muslin cloth and set aside for 10-15 minutes. | 6. Grease the worktop with some oil. | 7. To make puris, divide the dough into small equal portions and shape them into balls. | 8. Roll out each ball, on the greased worktop, into a small thin disc. | 9. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 10. Gently slide the discs, one at a time, into the hot oil and deep fry till they puff up and turn crisp. | 11. Drain on absorbent paper. | 12. Arrange them on a serving platter and serve hot with chai.

title: Mawa Gujiya
description: These gujiyas stuffed with a rich stuffing is a must at Holi celebrations.
ingredients: 2 cups Khoya/mawa grated | 2 tablespoons Castor sugar | 1 tablespoon Walnuts chopped | 1 tablespoon Cashewnuts chopped | 1 tablespoon Dried figs chopped | 1 tablespoon Almonds chopped | 1 tablespoon Dates chopped | 2 cups Refined flour (maida) | 3 tablespoons Ghee to deep fry Oil
method: 1. Heat a non-stick pan. | 2. Add khoya and castor sugar, mix and cook till khoya melts. | 3. Add walnuts, cashewnuts, figs, almonds and dates, mix well and cook for a minute. | 4. Remove from heat and set aside to cool. | 5. Knead together flour and 3 tablespoons ghee with sufficient cold water to a stiff dough. | 6. Divide the dough into equal portions, grease with some ghee and roll out into small discs. | 7. Stuff each disc with a portion of khoya mixture and shape into gujiyas. | 8. Heat sufficient ghee in a kadai. | 9. Deep-fry gujiyas on medium heat till golden brown. | 10. Drain on an absorbent paper. | 11. Serve hot.

title: Mawa Muffin with Shrikhand Frosting
description: A jolly good fusion of two different cuisines. The little ones are going to gobble up these muffins within no time
ingredients: 0 grams Mawa / khoya, grated | 0 grams Refined flour (maida) | 0 grams Butter, softened | 0 grams Sweet condensed milk 1/ | 2 teaspoon Baking powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon Baking soda | 5 millilitres Milk | 1 teaspoon Vanilla essence as required Kesar shrikhand to garnish Sutarfeni to garnish Wafer biscuits to garnish Chocolate chips to garnish Small carrot triangles, blanched
method: 1. Preheat the oven to 180° C. | 2. Take butter in a bowl and cream it lightly with an electric hand blender. | 3. Add khoya and continue to cream with the hand blender at a low speed. | 4. Add condensed milk and mix well. | 5. Sift together refined flour, baking powder and baking soda into another bowl. | 6. Gradually add this flour mixture to the butter mixture and mix in cut and fold method ensuring there are no lumps formed. | 7. Add milk and vanilla essence and continue to mix. | 8. Put this mixture into muffin moulds till they are ¾ full. | 9. Keep the moulds on a baking tray. | 10. Keep the tray in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes. | 11. Remove the tray from the oven and let them cool down to room temperature. | 12. Remove the muffins from the moulds and keep them on a serving plate. | 13. Put the shrikhand in a piping bag and pipe out over each muffins. | 14. Garnish each muffin with sutarfeni, a wafer biscuit, chocolate chips and carrot triangles and serve.

title: Mawa Wale Aloo
description: A rich potato dish fit for a royal banquet. Rich gravy made from mawa, tomatoes and Indian spices and cooked with potatoes.
ingredients: 1 cup grated mawa + to garnish | 4 medium potatoes, boiled and peeled | 1 tablespoon oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 tbsp + | 1 teaspoon ghee 1/ | 4 cup raisins 1/ | 4 cup cashewnuts, roughly chopped | 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger | 1 1/ | 2 teaspoons red chilli powder | 2 tablespoons coriander powder | 1 tablespoon garam masala powder | 1 teaspoon cumin powder 1/ | 4 teaspoon turmeric powder | 3 tablespoons roughly crushed cashewnuts | 1 cup milk 2- | 3 green chillies, finely chopped
method: 1. Heat oil and 2 tbsps ghee in a deep non-stick pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Cut the potatoes into thick slices, add to the pan and cook on medium heat shallow-fry, turning sides, till potatoes turn light golden brown on both sides. | 3. Drain into a bowl and set aside. | 4. Add raisins to the same pan, sauté for a few seconds. | 5. Drain into another bowl. | 6. Add ¼ cup roughly chopped cashewnuts to the same pan and sauté till lightly brown. | 7. Drain into a bowl. | 8. Add ginger and to the same pan and sauté till turn golden brown. | 9. Add tomatoes, salt, red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder and cumin powder and mix well. | 10. Add turmeric powder and crushed cashewnuts, mix and cook till tomatoes are pulpy. | 11. Switch off heat and transfer the mixture into a blender and ½ cup milk and blend to a fine paste. | 12. Switch on heat, add remaining ghee and green chillies to the same pan. | 13. Add remaining milk to the ground mixture and add the mixture to the pan. | 14. Rinse the blender jar with ½ cup water and add to the remaining gravy. | 15. Add ½ cup water to the gravy and mix well. | 16. Add the fried potatoes, half the fried cashewnuts, 1 tbsp fried raisins and mawa, mix and cook on medium heat for 4-5 minutes. | 17. Transfer into a serving plate, garnish with mawa, remaining fried cashewnuts and remaining fried raisins. | 18. Serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Methi Tamatar ki Sabzi
description: A healthy meal – fenugreek and tomatoes cooked with tomatoes, Indian spices and served with missi rotis.
ingredients: 1 Large bunch fenugreek leaves (methi), cleaned 2- | 3 medium tomatoes, finely chopped | 2 tbsps mustard oil + for drizzling 1/ | 2 teaspoon asafoetida | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds | 1 tablespoon chopped ginger | 3 green chillies, finely chopped | 1 tablespoon coriander powder | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder 1/ | 1 tablespoon lemon juice Fresh mint leaves to garnish
method: 1. Heat kachchi ghani mustard oil in a metal kadai till it begins to smoke. | 2. Cool it slightly, add asafoetida, cumin seeds, ginger and mix and sauté for 2-3 minutes. | 3. Add green chillies and mix and sauté till turn translucent. | 4. Add fenugreek leaves and mix well. | 5. Add coriander powder, red chilli powder, turmeric powder and salt, mix and sauté for 4-5 minutes. | 6. Add tomatoes, mash and cook till it turns soft and pulpy. | 7. Divide the dough into equal portions and roll out into medium size discs or rotis dusting with flour. | 8. Heat a non-stick tawa. | 9. Place the discs on it, one at a time, and roast till well browned and evenly cooked from both the sides. | 10. Keep them on a serving platter. | 11. Add lemon juice to the sabzi and mix well. | 12. Drizzle some mustard oil on top. | 13. Brush some mustard oil on the missi rotis Transfer sabzi into a serving bowl, garnish with mint leaves and serve hot with missi rotis.

title: Methi Thalipeeth
description: A nutritious Indian flatbread made with fenugreek leaves and a blend of spices, cooked to perfection on a griddle, offering a delightful burst of flavors in every bite.
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | ½ medium bunch fenugreek leaves (methi), chopped
method: 1. Take fenugreek leaves in a bowl, add salt and set aside. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Take bhajnee flour, turmeric powder and salt in a mixing bowl. | 4. Squeeze water from the methi leaves and add to the bhajnee mixture. | 5. Add sufficient water and knead to medium soft dough. | 6. Place a plastic sheet on a worktop and grease it with ghee. | 7. Take a big portion of mixture and place it on the sheet. | 8. Apply ghee on another sheet, place it over the mixture and flatten the mixture. | 9. Make more thalipeeth with the remaining mixture in the same way. | 10. Heat a non-stick tawa. | 11. Place a star shaped cookie cutter over the thalipeeth and cut out stars. | 12. Gently remove the cut outs and place them on the heated tawa. | 13. Make a cavity in centre of each star. | 14. Pour little ghee into the cavities and shallow fry, turning sides, till crisp. | 15. Serve hot with yogurt.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Mexican Cheese Pani Puri
description: A fusion delight blending the tangy, spicy flavors of traditional Indian pani puri with the creamy richness of Mexican cheese.
ingredients: 150gms processed cheese, cut into small cubes 1½ cups grated processed cheese + to sprinkle | 4 crisp puris | 4 tablespoons oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 medium 1mediumgreen capsicum, chopped | 2 medium tomatoes, chopped 1cup corn kernels, boiled | 3 tablespoons red chilli sauce | 1 tablespoon + | 2 teaspoons dried mixed herbs + to sprinkle 1tablespoon + | 2 teaspoons red chilli flakes + to sprinkle | 2 cups milk ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder Chaat masala to sprinkle Micro greens for garnish
method: 1. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add capsicum, tomatoes, corn kernels, green peas, salt, tomato ketchup, red chilli sauce, 1tablespoon mixed herbs and 1tablespoon red chilli flakes and sauté on medium heatfor 2-3 minutes, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Transfer the filling into a bowl. | 3. Add processed cheese cubesand mix well. | 4. To prepare cheese sauce, heat milk in a non-stick pan. | 5. Add grated processed cheese, turmeric powder and mix well. | 6. Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously, for 2-3 minutesor till the mixture boils and thickens.Transfer into another bowl. | 7. Add remaining mixed herbs and remaining red chilli flakes to the cheese sauceand mix well. | 8. Set aside to cool down. | 9. Arrangesmall serving glasses on a platter. | 10. Pour cheese sauce in each glass. | 11. Make a dent in centre of each puri. | 12. Stuff each puri with the filling and place one on the rim of each serving glass. | 13. Sprinkle mixed herbs, red chilli flakes and chaat masala on them. | 14. Garnish each puri with micro greens and sprinkle grated processed cheese on top. | 15. Serve immediately.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Millet Phirni
description: This is the recipe for the classic sweet pudding with a healthy twist - Black Raisin & Millet Phirni, Yumm! This is
ingredients: ½ cup Premium Black Raisins + for garnish | 4 cups milk ¼ cup barnyard millet (samo) ¼ cup sugar ½ teaspoon green cardamom powder Silver warq for garnish Blanched, peeled, and slivered pistachios for garnish
method: 1. Take Premium Black Raisins in a bowl. | 2. Add 1 cup water, set aside to soak for 10-15 minutes. | 3. Bring milk to a boil in a deep non-stick pan. | 4. Add barnyard millet, mix and cook for 8-10 minutes while stirring in between. | 5. Meanwhile, put the soaked raisins into a blender jar and blend to a fine paste. | 6. Once the millet is cooked and the mixture thickens, add the blended paste and mix well. | 7. Add sugar, mix and cook for 2-3 minutes or till sugar melts. | 8. Add green cardamom powder and mix well. | 9. Switch the heat off, transfer into individual serving earthen bowls and allow to cool. | 10. Once cooled, refrigerate for 1-2 hours or till chilled. | 11. Garnish with silver warq, Premium Black Raisins and slivered pistachios.

title: Mini Uttapam
description: An integral part of South Indian breakfast snack menu, these uttapams are made especially to lure the kids to eat healthy.
ingredients: 1 cup finger millet (ragi) flour ¼ cup rice flour
method: 1. Take nachni flour, rice flour, urad dal flour and yogurt in a bowl and mix well. | 2. Add sufficient water and make a semi thick batter. | 3. Set aside to ferment overnight. | 4. Add salt, asafoetida and turmeric powder to the batter and mix well. | 5. Heat oil in a non-stick pan and spread small portions of batter with a small spoon. | 6. Place 2 green peas as eyes, small carrot piece as nose, small beetroot crescent as smiling face. | 7. Press slightly and turn it over and cook on other side. | 8. Cook on medium heat till golden brown on both sides Line a serving platter with a banana leaf. | 9. Transfer the uttapams on it. | 10. Serve hot with sambhar and coconut chutney.

title: Mixed Palak Soya Saag
description: Rich in all the essential nutrients, this delicious dish is a winner. A seasonal recipe made from spinach and mustard leaves.
ingredients: 2 medium bunches fresh spinach leaves, shredded | 2 medium bunches fresh mustard leaves, shredded | 1 medium bunch fresh bathua leves, roughly chopped | 1 medium bunch fresh fenugreek leaves, shredded | 2 cups soya chunks, soaked and boiled | 4 tablespoons oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds | 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger | 3 green chillies, finely chopped | 1 1/ | 2 tablespoons coriander powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon turmeric powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon cumin powder | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder | 1 teaspoon garam masala powder + to sprinkle | 1 teaspoon lemon juice Cottage cheese (paneer), grated to garnish
method: 1. Heat soya oil in a metal kadai, add cumin seeds and sauté till fragrant, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add ginger, and sauté till turns light brown. | 3. Add green chillies and mix and sauté till turn light brown. | 4. Add tomatoes and mix. | 5. Add salt, mix well and cook till tomatoes turn soft and pulpy. | 6. Add coriander powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, red chilli powder and garam masala powder, mix and cook till the oil separates. | 7. Add spinach leaves, mustard leaves, bathua leaves and fenugreek leaves, mix well and cook till the leaves wilt. | 8. Transfer half the cooked saag into a blender jar leaving the remaining in the kadai, blend into a fine paste and add back to the kadai. | 9. Mix well. | 10. Add water as required mix well. | 11. Add soya chunks, mix well and cook on low heat for 6-8 minutes. | 12. Add lemon juice and mix well. | 13. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with cottage cheese, sprinkle some garam masala powder on top and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Moongphalli ka Halwa
description: Halwas can be made variously and this one is as tasty as the rest of them
ingredients: 4 cup Peanuts (moongphalli) 3/ | 4 cup Peanut butter 1/ | 2 cup Ghee 1/ | 4 cup Semolina (suji/rawa) 1/ | 2 cup Jaggery, grated | 1 teaspoon Green cardamom powder a pinch Saffron strands | 1 cup Khoya (mawa)
method: 1. Heat ghee in a non-stick pan, add semolina and sauté till fragrant. | 2. Meanwhile heat another non-stick pan, add some water, jaggery and green cardamom powder, mix and cook, stirring, till jaggery melts. | 3. Add peanuts and peanut butter to the semolina and mix well. | 4. Add jaggery water and cook till the mixture thicken, stirring continuously. | 5. Add saffron and mix. | 6. Add khoya and mix till it melts. | 7. Transfer into a serving bowl and serve warm.

title: Mughal Sarai Dal Kachori
description: Deep fried spicy moong dal stuffed kachoris served with a tangy chutney
ingredients: 1 cup split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal), soaked for | 1 hour and boiled with turmeric powder and salt | 0 grams readymade kachori dough | 2 tablespoons oil + for deep-frying | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds (jeera) | 1 teaspoon carom seeds (ajwain) | 1 teaspoon fennel seeds (saunf) | 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder 1½ tablespoons coriander powder | 1 teaspoon garam masala powder 1½ teaspoons dried mango powder (amchur) | 1 small potato, boiled, peeled and mashed | 2 tablespoons oil | 3 tablespoons tamarind paste | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder | 1 teaspoon garam masala powder ½ cup sugar | 1 small potato, boiled and finely chopped
method: 1. To make filling, heat 2 tablespoons oil in a non-stick pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add cumin seeds and sauté till fragrant. | 3. Add carom seeds, fennel seeds and ginger, mix and sauté for 1-2 minutes. | 4. Add green gram, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder and dried mango powder, mix well and sauté for 2-3 minutes. | 5. Transfer into a bowl. | 6. Add potato, and salt and mix well. | 7. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 8. Divide the kachori dough into equal portions and roll out into small discs. | 9. Place a spoonful of filling in the centre and bring the edges together to make a potli. | 10. Flatten a little into kachoris. | 11. Slide the kachoris, a few at a time, into hot oil and deep-fry till golden brown. | 12. Drain on absorbent paper. | 13. To make chutney, heat oil on a non-stick pan, add fennel seeds, cumin seeds, carom seeds and sauté for 2-3 minutes. | 14. Add tamarind paste and mix. | 15. Add 1½ cups water, red chilli powder, garam masala powder and sugar. | 16. Mix, bring to a boil and strain into a bowl. | 17. Add potato and and mix well. | 18. Chop coriander sprigs and add. | 19. Crack the kachoris from centre, fill in the prepared chutney, arrange them on a serving plate and serve.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Multi Grain Dal Dhokli
description: A popular Gujarati one meal dish made healthier by using multi grain flour
ingredients: 1 cup Multi grain flour | 1 cup Split pigeon pes (tuvar dal), soaked for 1/ | 2 tsp + pinches Turmeric powder 1/ | 2 cup Raw peanuts | 2 tablespoons Gram flour (besan) | 1 pinch Red chilli powder | 1 tablespoon Ghee 1/ | 2 teaspoon Mustard seeds 1/ | 4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing) 1/ | 2 teaspoon Fenugreek seeds (methi dana) | 6 Curry leaves | 2 Dried red chillies | 1 1/ | 2 tablespoons Dal tadka masala | 1 teaspoon Jagger, chopped | 1 tablespoon Fresh coriander leaves, chopped to serve Khakras
method: 1. Heat a pressure cooker, add split pigeon peas, salt, ½ tsp turmeric powder, peanuts and 2 cups water. | 2. Cover the cooker and cook under pressure till 4-5 whistles are released. | 3. Meanwhile take multigrain flour, gram flour, salt, remaining turmeric powder and red chilli powder in a bowl and mix well. | 4. Add sufficient water and knead into stiff dough. | 5. Cover with a damp muslin cloth and set aside for 10-15 minutes. | 6. Take small portions of dough and roll them on back of a fork to shape into gnocchis. | 7. Uncover the cooker when the pressure settles completely. | 8. Heat ghee in a deep non-stick pan, add mustard seeds and let them splutter. | 9. Add asafoetida, fenugreek seeds, curry leaves, dried red chillies and the cooked pigeon peas. | 10. Mix well for 1 minute on medium heat. | 11. Add dal tadka masala and mix well. | 12. Add sufficient water to get the required consistency and mix well. | 13. Add the gnocchis and mix well. | 14. Cover and cook on medium heat for 15-20 minutes. | 15. Adjust salt, add jaggery and coriander leaves and mix till jaggery dissolves. | 16. Switch the heat off. | 17. Put ladles full of cooked dal dhokli into serving plates and serve hot with khakras.

title: Nachni Idiappam
description: Finger millet or nachni is very high in proteins and very good for growing children. When the idiappams resemble nests, the children will simply devour them without complaint
method: 1. Boil 1 cup water in a deep non-stick pan. | 2. Add ghee and salt and mix well. | 3. Add nachni flour and mix well, stirring continuously till the mixture is smooth with no lumps. | 4. Cover and cook on low heat for 2-3 minutes till mixture thickens and forms soft dough. | 5. Grease the inner portion of chakli press with ghee. | 6. Transfer the cooked mixture into a bowl and spread it to let it cool down. | 7. Grease your palms with ghee and knead the dough lightly. | 8. Divide the dough into 2 portions. | 9. Shape a portion into a cylinder and put it into the chakli press fitted with sev attachment. | 10. Heat sufficient water in a steamer. | 11. Grease the idli moulds with ghee. | 12. Press the dough through the chakli press into greased moulds. | 13. Place the idli moulds in the steamer and steam for 8-10 minutes. | 14. Pour chutney in center of each idiyappam, and place the tempered potatoes in the centre of the nest. | 15. Serve immediately.

title: Neyyappam
description: A traditional sweet from Kerala – it is a sweet deep-fried rice and jaggery dumpling.
ingredients: 1 cup raw rice, washed and soaked for 4- | 5 hours | 2 tablespoons refined flour 1½ cups chopped jaggery | 2 teaspoons ghee ⅓ cup coconut slices | 1 tablespoon black sesame seeds | 1 teaspoon green cardamom powder ½ teaspoon cumin seeds ½ teaspoon dried ginger powder ¼ teaspoon nutmeg powder A pinch of salt ½ teaspoon soda bicarbonate Oil for deep frying
method: 1. Heat a non-stick pan, add jaggery and ½ cup water and cook, stirring till a thick syrup is formed. | 2. Remove the impurities and set aside to cool down. | 3. Heat ghee in another non-stick pan, add coconut slices and saute till light brown. | 4. Drain rice and put it in a blender jar, add jaggery syrup and grind to a fine paste. | 5. Transfer into a mixing bowl, add refined flour and mix well. | 6. Add fried coconut slices, sesame seeds, cardamom powder, cumin seeds, ginger powder, nutmeg powder, salt and soda bicarbonate, mix well. | 7. Let the batter rest for 1 hour. | 8. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai, pour a ladleful of batter and deep fry on a medium low heat till golden brown in colour. | 9. Drain on an absorbent paper. | 10. Make more neyyappam with the remaining batter. | 11. Arrange them on a serving platter and serve hot with tea.

title: Nimboo Mirch Wale Chane
description: An excellent starter – marinated boiled chickpeas batter fried and sautéed with a spicy mixture .
ingredients: 2 cups chickpeas (kabuli chane), soaked overnight and boiled | 1 lemon | 2 1/ | 2 teaspoons chaat masala | 3 tablespoons Punjabi chole masala | 2 tablespoons oil + to deep fry | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 1/ | 2 cups refined flour (maida) | 1 cup cornflour | 1 pinch baking soda | 2 green chillies, finely chopped | 1 inch ginger, cut into thin strips | 1 teaspoon crushed black peppercorns 1/ | 4 bunch fresh coriander leaves Green capsicum, thinly sliced to garnish Red capsicum, thinly sliced to garnish
method: 1. Take chickpeas in a bowl. | 2. Add 1½ tsps red chilli powder, salt, 1 tsp chaat masala and 2 tbsps Punjabi chole masala and mix well. | 3. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai, then add a pinch of hing. | 4. Take refined flour, cornflour, baking soda, salt and 1 tbsp oil in another bowl. | 5. Add little water and mix well to make a thick batter. | 6. Take 1 cup water in another bowl. | 7. Add marinated chickpeas to the batter and mix well. | 8. Dampen your fingers with water and pick each chickpea along with batter and slide into the hot oil. | 9. Separate the chickpeas with a spoon and deep fry till they are half done and float on top. | 10. Drain on absorbent paper. | 11. Reheat the oil in kadai to high temperature. | 12. Meanwhile heat 1 tbsp oil in a non-stick pan, add and sauté till lightly brown. | 13. Add green chillies, ginger strips and sauté till the mixture turns a light brown. | 14. Add the half fried chickpeas back in kadai, and deep fry till golden and crisp. | 15. Drain on absorbent paper. | 16. Add chickpeas to the pan. | 17. Add remaining red chilli powder, remaining Punjabi chole masala, remaining chaat masala, crushed black peppercorns and lemon juice. | 18. Chop coriander leaves and add, mix well and cook on medium heat for 1-2 minutes. | 19. Transfer into a serving bowl. | 20. Garnish with green and red capsicum slices and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Noon Chai Kashmiri
description: A warm hug from the valley in every sip, blending tradition and comfort seamlessly.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon regular tea leaves A pinch of baking soda Sugar as required 1½ cups milk ½ teaspoon salt Salted jeera biscuits to serve
method: 1. Mix tea leaves and baking soda in a bowl. | 2. Boil 2 cups water in a small stainless steel saucepan, add the tea leaves mixture. | 3. Constantly lift ladle full of mixture and pour it back into the pan on medium heat till colour changes. | 4. Add sugar as required and bring the mixture to a boil. | 5. Add milk and again lift ladle full of mixture and pour it back into the pan till colour changes. | 6. Add salt and again lift ladle full of mixture and pour it back into the pan. | 7. Strain the tea into serving cups and serve hot with salted jeera biscuit.

title: Oats Thalipeeth
description: One of the most popular breakfast snacks amongst the Maharashtrian, this thalipeeth has oats flour replacing the traditional bhajanee flour
ingredients: 1½ cups oats flour | 1 small carrot, grated | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves ½ teaspoon cumin powder ½ teaspoon coriander powder | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 3 green chillies, chopped Oil for drizzling Green chutney to serve
method: 1. Take oats flour in a bowl. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Add carrot, coriander leaves, cumin powder, coriander powder, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala powder, salt and green chillies and mix well. | 4. Add 1 cup water, and knead into soft dough. | 5. Take a non-stick tawa, add a portion of the dough, dip your fingers in water and spread it into a thick disc. | 6. Make a few holes in the center. | 7. Place the non-stick tawa on heat and cook for 4-5 minutes on medium heat. | 8. Drizzle a little oil. | 9. Flip and cook the other side for 2-3 minutes or until golden and crisp. | 10. Serve hot with green chutney.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Onion Capsicum Arachuvitta Sambhar
description: Arachuvitta means freshly ground and this traditional sambhar from Tamil nadu is made with freshly ground masala
ingredients: 1 medium Green capsicum, thickly sliced 1/ | 2 cup Split pigeon peas (tuvar dal), pressure cooked with ¼ tsp turmeric powder | 1 tablespoon Oil a pinch Fenugreek seeds (methi dana) 10- | 2 Curry leaves 1/ | 4 teaspoon Asafoetida 1/ | 2 teaspoon Turmeric powder | 1 1/ | 1 lemon si Tamarind, soaked in | 1 cup warm water for | 0 minutes | 1 tablespoon Jaggery, chopped | 2 tablespoons Fresh coriander leaves, chopped Masala paste 1/ | 4 cup Coriander seeds | 1 tablespoon Cumin seeds | 2 tablespoons Split pigeon peas (tuvar dal) | 1 tablespoon Split Bengal gram (chana dal) 1/ | 2 cup Fresh coconut, scraped 1/ | 2 teaspoon Oil 5- | 6 Dried red chillies (pandi) Tempering | 1 tablespoon Ghee | 1 teaspoon Mustard seeds a pinch Asafoetida 5- | 6 Curry leaves
method: 1. To make the masala paste, dry roast coriander seeds and cumin seeds till fragrant in a non-stick pan. | 2. Transfer onto a plate and set aside to cool. | 3. Similarly, dry roast pigeon peas and Bengal gram in the same pan till they turn golden brown. | 4. Transfer onto the same plate and let them cool. | 5. Dry roast coconut in the same pan till it turns golden brown. | 6. Transfer onto the same plate and let it cool. | 7. Heat oil in the same pan, add the dried red chillies and saute for a minute. | 8. Transfer onto the same plate and let them cool completely. | 9. Put all these ingredients into a mixer jar, add ½ cup water and blend to a semi-smooth paste. | 10. To make the sambhar, heat oil in a non-stick deep pan. | 11. Add fenugreek seeds and 5-6 curry leaves and sauté for a few seconds. | 12. Add the and sauté till translucent. | 13. Add the capsicum and sauté till the turn golden brown. | 14. Add turmeric powder, sambhar masala and salt and sauté for a minute. | 15. Squeeze the soaked tamarind to extract the pulp and add along with the water and mix well. | 16. Let the mixture come to a boil. | 17. Continue to cook for 10-12 minutes. | 18. Add the ground paste and mix well. | 19. Cook for 2-3 minutes. | 20. Add the cooked pigeon peas and mix. | 21. Add the remaining curry leaves and mix well. | 22. Cook for 1-2 minutes. | 23. Add jaggery and mix, cook till it melts. | 24. Add coriander leaves and mix well. | 25. To make the tempering, heat ghee in a small pan. | 26. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter. | 27. Add asafoetida and curry leaves and mix. | 28. Pour this into the sambhar and mix well. | 29. Serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Orange and Quinoa Salad
description: This vibrant quinoa salad combines juicy orange segments, roasted walnuts, and crisp radishes with tender cherry tomatoes and crunchy microgreens.
ingredients: 2 medium oranges | 2 cups quinoa, cooked | 0 cherry tomatoes 8- | 0 walnuts, roasted and roughly chopped 8- | 0 red radishes, thinly sliced 1/ | 2 cup microgreens Feta cheese to sprinkle Dressing | 6 tablespoons olive oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 4 lemons | 3 tablespoons honey
method: 1. Peel orange and separate the segments. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Remove seeds and halve them. | 4. Thinly slice and gently separate the layers. | 5. To make the dressing, take marmalade in a small jar, add salt, crushed black peppercorns, olive oil and mix till well combined. | 6. Halve the lemons and squeeze their juice directly into the jar and mix well. | 7. Add honey, place the lid on and shake the mixture till everything is well combined. | 8. Halve the cherry tomatoes. | 9. Take quinoa in a large bowl, add walnuts, cherry tomatoes, red radishes, micro greens and halved orange segments. | 10. Pour the dressing into it and toss lightly. | 11. Transfer the salad onto a serving plate, crumble and sprinkle feta cheese on top. | 12. Serve immediately.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Orange Fizz
description: A refreshing and fizzy beverage made using fresh oranges and tonic water! Check out this fizzy nonalcoholic drink - Orange Fizz.
ingredients: 9 oranges | 8 teaspoons powdered sugar Ice cubes as required Mint sprigs as required Tonic water as required
method: 1. Halve 8 oranges and slice 1 . | 2. Squeeze halved to extract juice and strain through a fine strainer. | 3. Add powdered sugar and mix well. | 4. Add ice cubes in 4 serving glasses and add 1 orange slice in each glass. | 5. Top up with more ice. | 6. Add mint sprigs and top up with orange juice. | 7. Finally top up with tonic water. | 8. Serve chilled.

title: Orange Pineapple Chia Drink
description: A refreshing nutritious drink made with Orange, Pineapple and Chia tastes amazing as well as looks great. So whip up a quick drink to sip on this afternoon.

title: Palak Moongphalli Til Pulao
description: Appearance of a dish is as important as its taste and this pulao scores high on both points
ingredients: 1 medium bunch Fresh spinach (palak), shredded | 1 cup Spinach puree | 1 cup Raw peanuts (kachchi moongphalli), soaked and boiled | 1 tablespoon White sesame seeds (til) | 3 cups Basmati rice, cooked | 1 tablespoon Oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 teaspoon Cumin seeds | 1 Bay leaf 10- | 2 Black peppercorns | 4 medium Potatoes, peeled | 1 teaspoon Red chilli powder 1/ | 4 teaspoon Turmeric powder | 1 tablespoon Lemon juice to garnish Fresh red chilli to garnish Tomato wedges
method: 1. Heat oil in a deep non-stick pan, add sesame seeds and saute till light brown, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add cumin seeds, bay leaf and peppercorns, mix and sauté for a few minutes. | 3. Cut potatoes into cubes, add to the pan and mix. | 4. Add mix and cook till the potatoes turn golden. | 5. Add peanuts, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, garam masala powder and salt, mix well and cook for 1-2 minutes. | 6. Add spinach leaves, mix and cook till the leaves wilt. | 7. Add spinach puree, lemon juice and Fortune Biryani Rice, mix lightly and cook till rice gets heated through. | 8. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with red chilli and tomato wedges and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Palak Paneer
description: Cottage cheese cooked in spinach-based gravy and fresh Indian spices is a household staple. Master the art of making this simple, wholesome and nutritious veg dish by following this recipe.
ingredients: 0 grams paneer (cottage cheese) | 2 medium bunches of spinach | 1 green chilli | 2 tablespoons oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 4 teaspoon garam masala | 2 tablespoons fresh cream + for garnish A large pinch of dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) powder Parantha to serve
method: 1. Finely chop and cut paneer into 1-inch cubes. | 2. Boil sufficient water in a deep pan. | 3. Add spinach leaves and blanch fir 1 minute. | 4. Drain into iced cold water. | 5. Drain the spinach leaves and squeeze out excess water and put into a mixer jar. | 6. Add green chilli and 1/2 cup water and grind to a fine paste. | 7. Heat oil in a non stick pan, add cumin seeds and let them change colour, then add a pinch of hing. | 8. Add the and sautè till translucent. | 9. Add mix and cook for a minute. | 10. Add the ground spinach paste, mix and cook for 1-2 minutes. | 11. Add salt, garam masala powder, paneer cubes and fresh cream and gently mix. | 12. Cook for 1 minute. | 13. Add dried fenugreek leaves powder, mix and cook for 1 minute. | 14. Take the pan off the heat. | 15. Garnish with fresh cream and serve hot with parantha.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Paneer Matar Makhana
description: This vegetarian main course recipe is made with cottage cheese, green peas and lotus seeds cooked in a rich velvety gravy. You can enjoy this with hot paranthas, chapatis or rotis.
ingredients: 0 grams Paneer (cottage cheese) cut into small cubes 1/ | 4 cup Green peas boiled | 2 cups Lotus seeds | 5 tablespoons Ghee | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 Tomato | 1 Green chilli | 1 inch Ginger roughly chopped 4- | 1 teaspoon Cumin seeds | 1 teaspoon Red chilli pepper | 1 tablespoon Coriander powder | 1 teaspoon Cumin powder 1/ | 4 teaspoon Turmeric powder 1/ | 4 teaspoon Garam masala powder 1/ | 4 teaspoon Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) powder | 2 tablespoons Fresh cream
method: 1. Heat 1 tsp ghee in a non-stick pan, add lotus seeds and saute on low heat for 5-6 minutes or till crisp. | 2. Transfer onto a plate and set aside. | 3. Heat 2 tsps ghee in the same pan, add and saute till golden brown. | 4. Add tomato, green chilli, ginger and and saute well. | 5. Add salt and mix well. | 6. Cover and cook for 5-6 minutes. | 7. Set aside to cool slightly. | 8. Transfer the mixture into a blender jar and add ¼ cup water and blend to a smooth paste. | 9. Heat remaining ghee in the same pan, add cumin seeds and sauté till their colour changes, then add a pinch of hing. | 10. Add the ground paste, mix well, cover and cook for 5-6 minutes. | 11. Add red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder and turmeric powder, mix well and saute for a few seconds. | 12. Add ¾ cup water, mix, and cook for a minute. | 13. Add green peas, cottage cheese and the roasted lotus seeds and mix well. | 14. Sprinkle garam masala powder, dried fenugreek leaves powder and fresh cream and mix well. | 15. Add honey and mix and take the pan off the heat. | 16. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprigs and serve hot with paranthe, wedges, green chillies and lemon wedges.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Paneer Parantha
description: Paranthe can be stuffed with different stuffings. Paneer paranthe is one of them that is both healthy and delicious
