ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 litre full cream milk | 1 cup fresh cream 1/ | 2 cup vinegar Dough, made with | 1 1/ | 2 cups whole wheat flour 1/ | 2 cup grated processed cheese | 2 green chillies, chopped | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon garam masala powder | 2 teaspoons chaat masala 1/ | 2 teaspoon carom seeds (ajwain)
method: 1. To make paneer heat milk in a deep non-stick pan, add cream and bring it to a boil, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add vinegar and mix well. | 3. Keep a large sieve over a bowl and line it with a muslin cloth. | 4. When the milk curdles, pour it in the sieve and strain the excess whey. | 5. Gather the edges of the cloth together and put it in cold water. | 6. Remove from water, keep it again in the sieve and keep a heavy weight over it. | 7. Set aside for 2 hours to let all the water drain away. | 8. To make the stuffing, crumble the paneer into a bowl. | 9. Add cheese, green chillies, red chilli powder, garam masala, chaat masala, carom seeds, fresh coriander leaves and salt and mix well. | 10. Divide the dough into equal portions. | 11. Shape into balls and press between your palms, make a cavity in the centre of each ball and add spoonful of paneer stuffing, bring the edges together and seal completely. | 12. Roll them out into five inch diameter paranthe. | 13. Heat a non-stick tawa, place one parantha at a time on it and cook for ½ minute. | 14. Flip it and apply ghee on the cooked side. | 15. Turn it over again and spread a little more ghee on the other side too. | 16. Cook, turning sides, till both sides are evenly cooked and golden brown. | 17. Wrap the paranthe in Freshwrapp and pack it in tiffin with yogurt packed in a small container with lid.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Pani Puri
description: Pani puri is a popular Indian street food snack, crispy puri shells filled with spicy, tangy flavored water (pani), along with a mixture of mashed potatoes, chickpeas, onions, and a tamarind chutney.
ingredients: Puris as required | 4 medium potatoes, boiled and peeled 1½ cups moong sprouts, blanched | 1 teaspoon roasted cumin powder | 1 teaspoon chaat masala + for sprinkling | 2 cups ragda Fried papdis as required Sev for sprinkling Imli chutney | 1 cup tamarind (imli), soaked in warm water for | 0 minutes | 1 cup deseeded dates, soaked in warm water for | 0 minutes ¼ cup melted jaggery | 1 small bunch fresh mint leaves (pudina) ½ small bunch fresh coriander leaves 4- | 2 teaspoons roasted cumin powder | 2 tablespoons pani puri masala Juice of | 1 lemon Ice cubes as required
method: 1. To prepare imli chutney, drain the excess water from tamarind and dates. | 2. Strain the tamarind in a bowl and squeeze to get the pulp. | 3. Grind dates alongwith some water into a smooth and thick puree. | 4. Add the date puree, melted jaggery, cumin powder, chilli powder and salt to the tamarind pulp and mix. | 5. Add 1 cup water and mix well. | 6. To prepare pudine ka pani, separate the mint leaves from the sprigs. | 7. Blend together coriander sprigs, mint leaves and broken green chillies alongwith some water into a smooth mixture. | 8. Transfer in a bowl. | 9. Add salt, cumin powder, chaat masala, black salt, pani puri masala, lemon juice and 4 cups water to the ground mint leaves mixture and mix well. | 10. Add few ice cubes and mix well. | 11. Mash the potatoes in another bowl. | 12. Add moong sprouts and mix. | 13. Add cumin powder, 1 teaspoon chaat masala, chilli powder, black salt and salt and mix well. | 14. Add salted boondi to pudine ka pani and mix well. | 15. Crack the top of each puri, put some radga, potato-moong mixture, pour some imli chutney and pudine ka pani and serve immediately. | 16. To prepare sukka puri, put some potato-moong mixture on fried puris, sprinkle some salt, chaat masala, black salt and sev on top and serve immediately.

title: Papaya Parantha
description: Begin your day with these healthy paranthas - an ideal breakfast snack
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 medium Raw papaya, peeled and grated | 0 grams Whole wheat flour (atta) | 1 tablespoon Ginger, finely chopped 2- | 3 Green chillies, finely chopped 1/ | 4 cup Salted peanuts, coarsely crushed | 1 teaspoon Red chilli powder | 1 teaspoon Garam masala powder | 1 tablespoon Sugar
method: 1. Mix together raw papaya, whole wheat flour, ginger, green chillies, salted peanuts, red chilli powder, garam masala powder, sugar, lemon juice and salt in a bowl. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Add sufficient water and knead into a dough. | 4. Heat a non-stick tawa. | 5. Divide the dough into equal portions and roll out into large discs. | 6. Place the paranthas on the hot tawa, one at a time, and cook, basting with oil and turning sides, till evenly browned on both the sides. | 7. Cut each parantha into 4 pieces, arrange them on a serving platter and serve hot with yogurt.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Pav Bhaji Pull Apart
description: Indulge in the ultimate comfort food hug with our Pav Bhaji Pull Apart – a spicy, buttery delight that'll have you coming back for more with every tear!
ingredients: A set of | 6 multigrain pavs | 1 tablespoon oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 teaspoons butter + for brushing | 1 large tomato, finely chopped | 1 medium green capsicum, finely chopped ½ cup grated carrot ½ cup grated cauliflower 2- | 3 medium potatoes, boiled, peeled and mashed ¼ cup frozen green peas 1½ tablespoons pav bhaji masala | 1 green chilli, finely chopped | 1 teaspoon red chilli paste | 1 teaspoon garam masala powder | 2 teaspoons lemon juice | 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh coriander leaves ½ cup grated mozzarella cheese | 2 cheese slices
method: 1. Preheat the oven at 180º C. | 2. Heat oil and 2 teaspoons butter in a non-stick pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 3. Add and sauté till translucent. | 4. Add a pinch of salt and sauté till turn golden brown. | 5. Add and sauté for a while. | 6. Add tomato and cook till it turns pulpy. | 7. Add capsicum, carrot and cook for a while. | 8. Add cauliflower, potatoes and frozen green peas. | 9. Mix well. | 10. Add salt, pav bhaji masala, green chilli, red chilli paste and garam masala powder. | 11. Sauté well. | 12. Mash the mixture well. | 13. Add lemon juice and coriander leaves. | 14. Add little water and mash again. | 15. Line a baking tray with parchment paper. | 16. Take the set of pavs on a worktop and halve it horizontally. | 17. Place the base layer on the tray lined with parchment paper and spread bhaji all over it. | 18. Sprinkle mozzarella cheese all over and place the cheese slices. | 19. Cover with top layer. | 20. Brush top of pavs with butter. | 21. Keep the tray in the preheated oven and bake for 4-5 minutes or till cheese melts. | 22. Place on a serving wooden platter with the parchment paper. | 23. Pull apart individual pav from the whole set and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Pesarattu
description: A traditional Andhra snack made from green gram (mung bean) batter, typically served with chutney or spicy fillings."This is healthy and tasty too.
ingredients: 1 cup whole green gram (sabut moong), soaked overnight | 2 tbsps rice, soaked overnight | 1 green chilli | 1 inch ginger, finely chopped 7- | 8 curry leaves | 0 grams cottage cheese (paneer), cut into 1½ inch cubes 2- | 3 medium tomato, sliced Gunpowder (molaga podi) to sprinkle Tomato ketchup to serve Coconut chutney to serve
method: 1. Blend green gram, rice, green chilli, ginger, curry leaves and coriander leaves with sufficient water to a fine paste. | 2. Transfer into a bowl and set aside for 1 hour. | 3. Add salt and mix well. | 4. Heat a non-stick pan, drizzle and little oil rub with muslin cloth. | 5. Pour a ladle of batter, and spread it evenly. | 6. Drizzle some more oil all around. | 7. Flip when the underside is golden and cook the other side till golden brown, Place paneer cubes and tomato slices alternately and sprinkle gunpowder over them. | 8. Fold and remove the pesarattu from the pan onto a serving plate. | 9. Serve hot with tomato ketchup and coconut chutney.

title: Pindi Chole Gujiya
description: A fusion delicacy combining the flavors of Pindi Chole with the concept of Gujiya, resulting in a savory pastry filled with spiced chickpeas, often enjoyed as a festive snack or appetizer.
ingredients: 1½ cups chickpeas (kabuli chane), soaked overnight, boiled with teabags | 1 tablespoon ghee | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 cup tomato puree | 1 tablespoon Punjabi chole masala Whole wheat flour dough as required Oil for deep-frying Green chutney to serve
method: 1. Heat ghee in a non-stick pan. | 2. Add and sauté on medium heat till turn golden brown. | 3. Add and sauté till fragrant. | 4. Add tomato puree, mix well and sauté for 3-4 minutes till oil separates. | 5. Add salt and mix well. | 6. Add chole masala, mix well and sauté for 2-3 minutes. | 7. Add boiled chickpeas with cooking liquor and mix well. | 8. Cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. | 9. Divide the dough into equal portions, apply little ghee on worktop, apply ghee on each portion and roll out into thin discs. | 10. Cut out medium round discs with a cookie cutter Mash the chole slightly and cook till the mixture dries up. | 11. Transfer into a plate and set aside to cool. | 12. Place a little pindi chole in the centre of each disc. | 13. Apply water on edges, fold into half and seal the edges and shape into gujiyas by slightly pinching the sealed edges and folding inwards. | 14. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai on medium heat, slide in gujiyas, a few at a time, and deep-fry till golden brown, then add a pinch of hing. | 15. Drain on absorbent paper. | 16. Transfer into a serving basket lined with butter paper and serve hot with green chutney.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Pineapple Chia Smoothie
description: Pinneaple Chia Smoothie is a healthy smoothie for a post-workout breakfast or even something you replace your usual morning drink with to go on the fitter side!
ingredients: For one portion | 2 tinned pineapple slices | 1 tablespoon chia seeds | 2 medium bananas 5- | 6 fresh spinach leaves 8- | 0 almonds ½ cup milk ¼ cup yogurt ¼ cup tender coconut water | 1 tablespoon honey
method: 1. Peel the bananas, roughly chop and add to the blender jar. | 2. Add spinach leaves, pineapple slices, almonds, milk, yogurt, tender coconut water, honey and chia seeds and blend into a smooth mixture. | 3. Serve immediately.

title: Pineapple Sandesh
description: A signature mithai from Bengal, sandesh can be made in different flavours – here pineapple graces it
ingredients: 1 litre Milk, curdled with | 1 tsp citric acid 7- | 8 Canned pineapple slices, soaked in water | 4 tablespoons Powdered sugar 1/ | 2 teaspoon Green cardamom powder to garnish Almonds, blanched, peeled and slivered to garnish Pistachios, blanched, peeled and slivered to garnish Saffron strands
method: 1. Strain the curdled milk (chenna) through a muslin cloth and squeeze out excess water completely. | 2. Transfer the chenna onto a large plate and knead lightly. | 3. Add powdered sugar and green cardamom powder and knead again. | 4. Transfer chenna into a piping bag. | 5. Drain the pineapple slices on an absorbent paper till they are completely dry and place on a serving platter. | 6. Pipe out the chenna mixture on the edges of pineapple slices in floral shape. | 7. Garnish each slice with almond slivers, pistachio slivers, few saffron strands and serve.

title: Pink Khandvi
description: A vibrant twist on the traditional Indian snack, featuring delicate rolls made from gram flour and yogurt, infused with a hint of beetroot puree for a visually appealing and flavorful treat
ingredients: 4 cup Beetroot puree | 1 cup Gram flour (besan) | 1 tablespoon Refined flour (maida) 1/ | 2 teaspoon Turmeric powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon Red chilli powder 1/ | 3 cups Buttermilk for greasing Oil to garnish Fresh coconut, scraped to garnish Fresh coriander leaves, chopped Tempering | 2 tablespoons Oil | 1 teaspoon Mustard seeds 1/ | 4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hig) | 1 sprigs Curry leaves
method: 1. Put gram flour in a bowl, add refined flour, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, ginger-green chilli paste and salt and mix well. | 2. Add buttermilk and mix till there are no lumps. | 3. Heat a deep non-stick pan. | 4. Pour the batter into it and cook on medium heat, whisking continuously till the mixture thickens. | 5. Grease the back side of steel thalis with some oil. | 6. Add beetroot puree to the cooking mixture and mix well and cook further on medium heat for 1-2 minutes. | 7. While the mixture is still hot spread a thin layer of the batter with a palette knife on the greased thalis and set aside to cool. | 8. After the mixture cools down, sets and the colour deepens, cut the layers vertically into thick strips, and roll each strip starting from one end to another. | 9. Insert coloured toothpick in center of each khandvi and arrange them on a plate. | 10. To prepare the tempering, heat oil in a non-stick tempering pan. | 11. Add mustard seeds and let them sputter. | 12. Add asafoetida and curry leaves and mix well. | 13. Pour the tempering over khandvis, garnish with coconut and coriander leaves and serve.

title: Pista Chickpea Hummus
description: Creamy, dreamy, and full of pistachio goodness - our Pista Chickpea Hummus is a must-try. Serve this with toasted sourdough bread slices. This is
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 cups pistachios 1½ cups boiled chickpeas 3-
method: 1. Peel pistachios. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Boil sufficient water in a nonstick pan, add pistachios and boil for 4-5 minutes. | 4. Drain and peel. | 5. Transfer boiled chickpeas in a processor. | 6. Reserve a few pistachios for garnish and add remaining pistachios. | 7. Add parsley, salt, crushed black peppercorns and extra virgin olive oil and process to a fine mixture. | 8. Transfer on a serving plate and spread in circular motion. | 9. Arrange pickled carrot, cucumber and slices on top. | 10. Place black olives, cherry tomatoes quarters and reserved pistachios on top. | 11. Drizzle extra virgin olive oil and serve with toasted sourdough bread slices.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Pista Pesto Crostini
description: This delightful dish combines the rich flavors of pistachios with the freshness of pesto, all served on crispy crostini. It's a perfect way to indulge your taste buds while nourishing your body.
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | ½ cup Select California Roasted and Salted Pistachios | 4 toasted sourdough bread slices 1½ cups fresh basil leaves 3- | 2 tablespoons grated parmesan cheese Halved cherry tomatoes as required Halved yellow baby tomatoes as required Halved bocconcini roundels as required Fresh basil sprigs for garnish
method: 1. Remove the shells of Select California Roasted and Salted Pistachios. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Transfer in a grinder jar. | 4. Add basil leaves, extra virgin olive oil and grind to coarse paste. | 5. Add parmesan cheese and continue to grind to coarse paste. | 6. Preheat the oven at 180ºC. | 7. Place sourdough bread slices on worktop. | 8. Spread the prepared pesto evenly. | 9. Arrange cherry tomatoes, yellow baby tomatoes and bocconcini halves on top and transfer on a baking tray. | 10. Bake for 6-8 minutes. | 11. Garnish with basil sprigs and serve.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Pizza Roll Ups
description: Pizza roll-ups are a delicious twist on traditional pizza, where pizza dough is rolled up with your favorite toppings and then baked until golden and crispy. They're perfect for a quick snack or...
ingredients: 0 grams fresh yeast | 1 teaspoon sugar | 2 teaspoons salt 1½ cups refined flour + for dusting | 1 tablespoon olive oil + for greasing ¼ cup pizza sauce ½ cup grated mozzarella cheese ¼ cup sweet corn, boiled 4- | 5 button mushrooms, chopped ¼ cup finely chopped mixed capsicums | 1 pickled jalapeno, chopped Dried oregano for sprinkling Red chilli flakes for sprinkling
method: 1. Place the yeast in a big bowl. | 2. Add the sugar, 1 teaspoon salt and 2-3 tablespoons of warm water and set aside for 10-15 minutes. | 3. Add flour, olive oil and remaining salt to the activated yeast and mix well. | 4. Add sufficient water and knead into soft dough. | 5. Cover with a wet muslin cloth and set aside for 15-20 minutes in a warm place. | 6. Preheat the oven to 200°C. | 7. Grease a baking tray with oil. | 8. Dust some flour on the work table, place the dough over it and roll it into a thin rectangular sheet. | 9. Apply pizza sauce and sprinkle mozzarella cheese. | 10. Add corn, mushrooms, mixed capsicums, jalapeno. | 11. Sprinkle dried oregano and chilli flakes. | 12. Roll tightly and cut into thick slices. | 13. Place the slices on a baking tray keeping some distance between each and bake for 10-15 minutes. | 14. Remove and let them cool. | 15. Wrap and pack it in the tiffin.

title: Pizza Samosa
description: Why choose between pizza and samosas when you can have the best of both worlds in one crispy bite?
ingredients: Readymade samosa dough as required | 2 tablespoons oil + to deep fry | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 medium green capsicum, cut into ½ inch cubes ½ cup boiled corn kernels ¼ cup tomato puree | 0 grams mozzarella cheese, cut into ½ inch cubes Parmesan cheese powder to sprinkle Tomato ketchup to serve
method: 1. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add and sauté for a minute, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add capsicum and corn kernels and cook for 1 minute. | 3. Add tomato puree and cook for 1 minute on medium heat. | 4. Add paneer, salt and green peas and mix. | 5. Sauté for 1a minute and switch off heat. | 6. Transfer into a bowl. | 7. Add dried mixed herbs, red chilli flakes and mozzarella cheese and mix well. | 8. Heat sufficient fortune oil a kadai. | 9. Lightly knead the dough, divide into equal medium portions and roll them into thin oval discs. | 10. Halve each disc. | 11. Take water in a bowl. | 12. Apply water on the edges of each halved disc, shape into a cone, fill in the prepared filling, apply water on edges and seal the edges to form a triangle. | 13. Slide in the samosas into hot oil and deep fry till golden and crisp. | 14. Keep rotating the samosas occasionally so that they are evenly cook all around. | 15. Drain on absorbent paper. | 16. Sprinkle parmesan cheese powder, crushed mixed herbs and red chilli flakes on them. | 17. Arrange on a platter and serve hot with tomato ketchup.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Pizza Sauce
description: Originated essentially from Italian cuisine, Pizza has garnered many fans from India. Better than the average store bought Pizza Sauce, this one is much more authentic and flavorful
ingredients: 1 cup fresh tomato puree | 1 tablespoon olive oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 teaspoon paprika | 1 teaspoon red chilli flakes | 1 tablespoon tomato ketchup 3- | 4 fresh basil leaves + to garnish | 2 pizza bases Toppings of your choice Mozzarella cheese as required
method: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan, add and sauté for 2 minutes, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add mix and sauté till golden brown. | 3. Add tomato puree, mix and saute for a minute. | 4. Add salt and peppercorn and mix well. | 5. Add 1 cup water and mix, and cook for a minute. | 6. Add paprika and red chilli flakes and mix well. | 7. Add tomato ketchup, mix and sauté on medium heat for 5-7 minutes. | 8. Tear and add basil leaves and mix well. | 9. Remove the pan from heat and let the sauce cool. | 10. Preheat the oven to 180º C. | 11. Keep the pizza base on a baking tray, spread a ladleful of pizza sauce on it. | 12. Top with topping of your choice and mozzarella cheese. | 13. Bake for 5 minutes. | 14. Make another pizza similarly. | 15. Serve the sauce as a dip with pizza.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Posto Halwa
description: It will take a while to make this khus khus halwa but the end result is well worth the effort
ingredients: 1 cup Poppy seeds (khuskhus / posto), soaked | 2 cups Milk 1/ | 2 cup Ghee | 2 tablespoons Semolina (rawa / suji) 15- | 0 + to garnish Walnuts | 1 1/ | 2 cups Caster sugar | 2 cups Mawa, grated | 1 teaspoon Green cardamom powder a few strands Saffron
method: 1. Spoon the soaked poppy seeds into a blender jar, add ½ cup milk and blend to a fine paste. | 2. Heat a non-stick pan, add ghee, poppy seed paste and semolina, mix and sauté, stirring continuously to avoid the formation of lumps, on medium heat for 15-20 minutes, or till the mixture thickens and becomes golden brown. | 3. Crush and add 15-20 walnuts and mix well. | 4. Add caster sugar, remaining milk and stir continuously to avoid formation of lumps and cook on medium heat till the mixture thickens and colour changes to brown. | 5. Add khoya / mawa and mix well. | 6. Sauté for a minute and add green cardamom powder, mix and sauté for 2-3 minutes. | 7. Switch off heat and add saffron and mix well. | 8. Transfer into a serving bowl and garnish with California walnuts. | 9. Allow to cool down. | 10. Serve cold.

title: Pot Roast Vegetable Stew
description: Medley of vegetables and mushrooms cooked together to make a wonderful stew. Vegetables cooked with whole spices and served with bread.
ingredients: 2 small carrots, peeled | 2 medium potatoes, peeled | 1 medium sweet potato, peeled | 1 tablespoon olive oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 tablespoon butter 6- | 5 baby corns, diagonally sliced 4- | 5 dried shiitake mushrooms, soaked, drained and halved 4- | 5 button mushrooms, halved A few sprigs of fresh thyme | 2 teaspoons refined flour (maida) | 1 1/ | 2 cups vegetable stock | 1 cup mixed capsicums triangles Toasted bread slices to serve
method: 1. Cut carrots into 2 inch pieces, quarter potatoes and . | 2. Cut sweet potato into 2 inch pieces. | 3. Heat olive oil in a non-stick heavy bottom pan, add butter and let it melt, then add a pinch of hing. | 4. Add carrots, sweet potatoes, potatoes, salt, crushed black peppercorns and baby corns, mix and cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes. | 5. Add shitake mushrooms, button mushrooms and thyme and sauté well. | 6. Add refined flour and mix well. | 7. Sauté for 1-2 minutes. | 8. Add stock and mix well. | 9. Add mixed capsicums and mix well. | 10. Cover and cook for 15-20 minutes. | 11. Take it off the heat. | 12. Transfer into a serving dish and serve hot with toasted bread slices.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Power Idli
description: These idlis have neither rice nor rawa but only dals – healthy, tasty and an ideal breakfast snack.
ingredients: 1 cup split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal), soaked | 1 cup split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal), soaked | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds ½ teaspoon asafoetida
method: 1. Drain and put split black gram and split green gram into a blender jar. | 2. Add sufficient water and blend to a fine paste. | 3. Transfer into a large bowl and whisk well. | 4. Cover the bowl and set aside in a warm place to ferment for 6-8 hours. | 5. Add cumin seeds, asafoetida, ginger-green chilli paste, turmeric powder and salt and mix well. | 6. Heat sufficient water in a steamer, pour the batter into greased idli moulds and steam for 12-15 minutes. | 7. Take them off the heat. | 8. Dip spoon in water and scoop out the idlis from the mould and arrange them in a serving platter. | 9. Serve hot with coconut chutney.

title: Protein Bowl
description: Discover our ultimate 'Protein Bowl' recipe that’s both nutritious and flavorful. This bowl features a hearty mix of brown rice, grilled tofu, and colorful veggies. Ideal for a quick, healthy lunch...
ingredients: 0 grams cottage cheese (paneer), cut into | 4 thick slices | 1 teaspoon red chilli flakes | 1 teaspoon dried mixed herbs 1½ teaspoons lemon juice | 2 tablespoons oil Herbed rice | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 cup cooked brown rice | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh mint leaves | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves | 4 teaspoons olive oil | 8 button mushrooms, thickly sliced 12- | 5 medium broccoli florets, blanched and drained ½ cup mixed coloured capsicums triangles | 1 medium carrot, peeled, cut into triangles, boiled and drained | 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil | 2 tablespoons honey | 1 tablespoon lemon juice Salad 8- | 0 cherry tomatoes, halved | 1 medium English cucumber, cut into ½ inch cubes 20- | 5 assorted lettuce leaves | 4 tablespoons assorted micro greens + for garnish
method: 1. Place cottage cheese slices on a plate. | 2. Sprinkle powder, powder, red chilli flakes, dried mixed herbs, lemon juice, olive oil and salt and apply the mixture well on all the slices and set aside to marinate for 10-15 minutes. | 3. To make the herbed rice, heat olive oil in a non-stick pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 4. Add mix and saute for a few seconds. | 5. Add red chilli flakes, mint leaves, coriander leaves, mixed herbs and salt and mix well. | 6. Add brown rice and mix well. | 7. Add ½ cup water, cover and cook for 2-3 minutes. | 8. Take the pan off the heat. | 9. To make the sautéed veggies, heat olive oil in a non-stick pan, add mix and saute till golden brown. | 10. Add mushrooms, mix and cook for 3-4 minutes. | 11. Add broccoli, capsicums, carrots, and and mix well. | 12. Add salt and fresh herbs, mix well and saute for 1-2 minutes. | 13. Take the pan off the heat. | 14. To make the dressing, take extra virgin olive oil in a bowl, add honey, mustard paste, salt and crushed black peppercorns and whisk well. | 15. Add lemon juice and mix well. | 16. Add cherry tomatoes and English cucumber. | 17. Tear the assorted lettuce leaves and add to the bowl along with micro greens and gently toss till well combined. | 18. To grill the cottage cheese, heat oil in a non-stick grill pan. | 19. Place the cottage cheese slices on it and grill on high heat for 1-2 minutes. | 20. Flip and grill the other side for 1-2 minutes as well. | 21. To assemble, place a portion of the herbed rice in a serving bowl. | 22. Add a portion of the veggie mixture on one side, some of the salad on other side and place a cottage cheese slice on top. | 23. Garnish with micro greens and serve.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Protein Rich Poha
description: Elevate your poha with the nutritious power of moth and moong sprouts, adding a protein-rich punch to this beloved breakfast dish.
ingredients: 1½ cups pressed rice ½ cup sprouted moth beans ½ cup sprouted green gram (moong) Salt to taste ½ large | 1 tablespoon oil 10- | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 5 curry leaves ½ teaspoon cumin seeds | 1 inch ginger | 1 green chilli ½ teaspoon turmeric powder ½ lemon Red radish slices for garnish Fresh coriander sprig for garnish
method: 1. Heat a non-stick pan, add moth, green gram, ¼ cup water and salt. | 2. Mix well and cook till the sprouts soften. | 3. Roughly chop . | 4. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add curry leaves and cumin seeds and sauté till the seeds change colour, then add a pinch of hing. | 5. Add sauté well and cook till translucent. | 6. Finely chop ginger, green chilli and add to the pan. | 7. Mix well and cook for 1 minute. | 8. Add cooked sprouts mixture, cover and cook for 2 minutes. | 9. Add turmeric powder and salt and mix well. | 10. Take pressed rice in a large bowl, wash and strain well. | 11. Add pressed rice to the -sprout mixture and mix well. | 12. Cook for 1-2 minutes. | 13. Squeeze the juice of lemon into the pan, mix well and cook for 1-2 minutes. | 14. Transfer the mixture into a serving bowl, garnish with red radish slices and coriander sprig and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Pudla Sandwich
description: A dish that fuses two of our favourites, pudla and sandwich into one is simply brilliant. The remarkable taste of the two dishes blending together beautifully makes it a must try
ingredients: ½ cup gram flour (besan) | 2 green chillies, finely chopped | 1 tablespoon chopped fresh coriander leaves | 1 medium tomato, finely chopped | 3 small mixed bell peppers, roughly chopped ½ teaspoon chaat masala | 1 tablespoon finely chopped purple cabbage | 1 tablespoon finely chopped cabbage Butter for greasing and basting | 2 tablespoons grated processed cheese (optional) + for garnishing Tomato ketchup for serving Green chutney for serving
method: 1. To make pudla batter, mix together gram flour, turmeric powder, chilli powder, salt, green chillies, 1½ teaspoons coriander leaves, and ¼ cup water in a bowl. | 2. Heat a non-stick pan. | 3. Take 2 bread slices, coat in batter and place on the pan. | 4. Add tomatoes, mixed bell peppers, ¼ teaspoon chaat masala, purple cabbage, cabbage, and dab lightly. | 5. Grease the edges with butter lightly. | 6. Turn it over to the other side once the underside turns golden brown. | 7. Apply some butter on the edges and flip once more. | 8. Top with remaining coriander leaves, processed cheese (optional) and remaining chaat masala. | 9. Cut the bread slices from the centre and place one bread slice over the other. | 10. Remove from heat and cut into four parts. | 11. Garnish with processed cheese and serve hot with tomato ketchup and green chutney.

title: Pumpkin Halwa
description: Not many like pumpkin but have you ever tasted a halwa made with pumpkin? Don't judge it before you try it. It's supremely delicious and easy to make too! And since pumpkin is a super food, it would...
ingredients: 4 cups grated pumpkin 1½ tablespoons ghee | 2 cups full fat milk ½ teaspoon green cardamom powder A large pinch of saffron A pinch of nutmeg powder ¾ cup granular sugar ½ cup grated mawa | 2 tablespoons chopped cashewnuts 1½ tablespoons slivered almonds 10- | 2 pistachios, blanched, peeled and slivered + for garnish Silver warq for garnish 5- | 6 cashewnuts, vertically halved and fried
method: 1. Heat ghee in a deep and wide non-stick pan. | 2. Add pumpkin, mix and sauté for 4-5 minutes. | 3. Add milk, green cardamom powder and saffron and mix. | 4. Add nutmeg powder and mix till well combined. | 5. Continue to cook, stirring in between, for 12-15 minutes or till the pumpkin softens and the mixture thickens. | 6. Add sugar, mawa, cashewnuts, almonds and pistachios and mix. | 7. Cook, stirring, for 8-10 minutes or till the mixture thickens again. | 8. Take the pan off the heat and transfer into serving bowls. | 9. Garnish with silver warq, fried cashewnuts and slivered pistachios.

title: Pumpkin Poppers
description: Experience the perfect bite-sized sweet pumpkin mixture cooked in a paniyaram mould, makes a great breakfast snack.
ingredients: 2 cups grated pumpkin (bhopla) 1½ tablespoons ghee + for greasing | 1 cup grated jaggery ½ cup semolina½ cup fine rice flour Oil for deep-frying Tomato ketchup to serve
method: 1. Heat ghee in a non-stick pan. | 2. Add pumpkin and sauté on high heat for 2-3 minutes till moisture evaporates. | 3. Switch off heat. | 4. Add jaggery and mix well. | 5. Transfer in a bowl. | 6. Mix well. | 7. Add semolina, rice flour and mix well. | 8. Divide the dough into equal portions, grease palms with ghee and make small balls of each portion. | 9. Drizzle ghee in a hot paniyaram mould. | 10. Place the balls in the mould. | 11. Cook on low heat till slightly brown. | 12. Flip and cook on the other side as well. | 13. Drain on an absorbent paper. | 14. Serve hot with tomato ketchup.

title: Punjabi Samosa
description: Samosa dough, shaped into a cone, stuffed with spicy potato pea mixture and deep fried. A popular snack from the Indian cuisine".
ingredients: 1½ cups refined flour | 4 tablespoons ghee | 3 tablespoons coriander seeds | 2 tablespoons cumin seeds | 1 tablespoon chopped ginger 2- | 3 green chillies, chopped ½ cup boiled green peas ½ teaspoon garam masala powder | 2 tsps dried and crushed pomegranate seeds | 2 tsps dried mango powder (amchur) | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves Oil for drizzling + for deep frying Green chutney to serve Date and tamarind chutney to serve Fried green chillies to serve
method: 1. Take refined flour in a parat. | 2. Add carom seeds, salt and mix well. | 3. Add 2 tablespoons ghee and mix well. | 4. Add 1 cup water and knead to stiff dough.Cover with a moist muslin cloth and set aside for 10 minutes. | 5. Dry roast coriander seeds on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. | 6. Transfer to a mortar pestle. | 7. Dry roast the cumin seeds in the same pan for 2 minutes on medium heat. | 8. Transfer in the mortar pestle and crush coarsely. | 9. For the stuffing, heat remaining ghee in a kadai. | 10. Add 1½ tablespoons of ground spice mix and sauté for a few seconds. | 11. Add ginger, green chillies and sauté for 30 seconds. | 12. Crush the potatoes and add to the kadai. | 13. Add the green peas and mix well. | 14. Add salt, garam masala powder, dried pomegranate seeds, dried mango powder and mix well. | 15. Add coriander leaves and mix well. | 16. Take the pan off the heat. | 17. Drizzle a little oil on a worktop and spread it evenly. | 18. Divide the dough into equal portions and shape each into a ball. | 19. For making samosa, roll the prepared balls in an oblong and cut in half to make a semi-circle. | 20. Take a semi-circle and apply water on the edges, shape into a cone. | 21. Stuff with a portion of the prepared stuffing and press the edges to seal. | 22. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai, deep fry the prepared samosas on medium heat till golden brown and crisp. | 23. Drain on an absorbent paper. | 24. Serve hot with green chutney, date and tamarind chutney and fried green chillies.

title: Pyaaz ki Kachori
description: Pyaaz ki Kachori is a traditional Rajasthani snack which satisfies your taste buds to the core. Its raw, authentic flavour is bound to make this kachori your favourite snack
ingredients: Refined flour (maida) dough as required | 1 tablespoon oil + for deep-frying | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds (jeera) | 1 inch ginger, finely chopped | 1 teaspoon fennel seeds | 1 tablespoon coriander seeds | 1 tablespoon chopped fresh coriander leaves Date-tamarind chutney for serving
method: 1. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a non-stick pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Add cumin seeds and sauté till it changes colour. | 3. Add ginger, green chilli, fennel seeds and coriander seeds and sauté for 1 minute. | 4. Add and sauté till light golden brown. | 5. Add chilli powder, dried mango powder and salt and sauté for 1 minute. | 6. Add crushed peppercorns and sauté. | 7. Add coriander leaves and mix well. | 8. Transfer in a bowl and cool. | 9. Divide the dough into equal portions and shape into balls. | 10. Make cavity in the center of each ball, put a portion of sautéed mixture, bring the ends together, seal and shape into balls. | 11. Lightly flatten each stuffed portion using your fingers into small thick disc. | 12. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 13. Put the stuffed discs and deep-fry till golden brown and crisp. | 14. Drain on absorbent paper. | 15. Serve hot with date-tamarind chutney.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Quinoa Chole
description: Chole served over a bed of spiced quinoa – a complete meal in itself. Rice is replaced with quinoa in this recipe.
ingredients: 2 cups quinoa, cooked | 1 cup chickpeas (kabuli chane), soaked overnight | 2 1/ | 2 tablespoons oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 1/ | 2 teaspoons cumin seeds 1/ | 2 tablespoon finely chopped ginger | 2 tablespoons chole masala | 2 teaspoons red chilli powder | 2 green chillies Fresh coriander sprigs to garnish Salad to serve
method: 1. Heat 1½ tbsps oil in a pressure cooker, add 1 tsp cumin seeds and saute till they change colour, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Finely chop and add, mix and sauté well. | 3. Add ginger and and mix well. | 4. Add chole masala and mix well. | 5. Add red chilli powder, mix well and cook for 1 minute. | 6. Add tomato puree and mix well. | 7. Drain and add chickpeas, salt and 2 cups water and mix well. | 8. Close the cooker with its lid and cook under pressure till 8-10 whistles are released. | 9. Take it off the heat and allow the pressure to settle completely. | 10. Meanwhile, heat remaining oil in a non-stick pan, add remaining cumin seeds and saute till they change colour. | 11. Add and mix well. | 12. Roughly chop green chillies and add to the mixture, add cooked quinoa and salt and toss till well combined. | 13. Drizzle a little water over the quinoa, cover and cook on low heat for 2-3 minutes. | 14. For serving, place a portion of quinoa on each serving plate, pour a portion of chole over it, garnish with a coriander sprig and serve hot with salad.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Radish Cakes
description: Indulge in the savory delight of deep-fried radish cakes, a delicious Oriental starter paired perfectly with a flavorful dip.
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 hour Serve | 1 small white radish, peeled and grated | 1 medium white radish, peeled and cut into thin strips | 2 teaspoons sugar | 4 dried shitake mushrooms, soaked and chopped
method: 1. Boil sufficient water in a deep non-stick pan, add white radish strips and boil for 4 minutes. | 2. Drain them and transfer into a bowl of ice cold water. | 3. Add carrot strips to the same pan and boil for 4 minutes. | 4. Drain them and transfer into another bowl of ice cold water. | 5. To make batter, take rice flour, refined flour, and wheat starch in a large bowl. | 6. Add grated radish, salt, sugar, powder, powder, and white pepper powder and 3 cups water and mix till well combined. | 7. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a non-stick pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 8. Add mushrooms and sauté on high heat for 2-3 minutes. | 9. Drain white radish strips and carrot strips and add to the pan and sauté well. | 10. Switch off the heat, add the batter and stir immediately to avoid any lump formation. | 11. Transfer the mixture into a 6” x 4” square silicon cake mould and level out the top. | 12. Heat sufficient water in a steamer. | 13. Place the cake mould in the steamer and steam for 1 hour 5 minutes. | 14. Switch the heat off and allow to rest for 5 minutes. | 15. Remove the cake mould from the steamer and allow to cool down to room temperature. | 16. Place it in the refrigerator for 6-8 hours or till completely chilled. | 17. De-mould the cake and cut into 1 inch thick squares. | 18. These are the radish cakes. | 19. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 20. Gently slide in the squares, a few at a time, and deep-fry till golden brown and crisp. | 21. Drain on absorbent paper. | 22. Arrange the radish cakes on a serving plate and garnish with fried and fresh red chillies. | 23. Serve hot with soy-ginger dip.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Ragi Jaggery Cookies
description: A nutritious twist on the classic cookie, blending wholesome ragi flour with the natural sweetness of jaggery for a delicious treat.
ingredients: ¾ cup ragi flour ½ cup jaggery powder ¼ cup cocoa powder ¼ teaspoon baking soda ½ cup butter 2- | 3 tablespoons milk 1- | 2 tablespoon chocolate chips
method: 1. Preheat the oven to 180º C. | 2. Line a baking tray with parchment paper. | 3. Mix together ragi flour, cocoa powder and baking soda in a bowl. | 4. Cream together butter and jaggery powder in another bowl till soft and creamy. | 5. Add the flour mixture and fold in. | 6. Add milk and mix well. | 7. Divide the mixture into small equal portions, shape them into balls and flatten them a little. | 8. Arrange them on the tray keeping them 1 inch apart. | 9. Place the chocolate chips on top. | 10. Put the tray in the preheated oven and bake for 10-15 minutes. | 11. Remove from oven and allow to cool. | 12. Arrange the cookies on a serving plate and serve.

title: Ragi Oats Cookies With Banana Milkshake
description: If you are a health freak, then this combination of ragi cookies and banana milkshake is just right for you
ingredients: ½ cup finger millet (ragi) flour, roasted ¼ cup oats, roasted ½ cup whole wheat flour ½ cup powdered sugar | 1 teaspoon baking powder ½ cup chilled butter Few drops of vanilla essence Milk as required Oil for greasing Banana Milkshake | 1 ripe banana | 2 cups milk | 2 tablespoons peanut butter | 1 tablespoon honey
method: 1. Preheat oven to 180º C. | 2. Sift together roasted millet flour, wheat flour and powdered sugar into a bowl. | 3. Add oats and mix well. | 4. Add baking powder and butter and rub it in with finger tips till you get a breadcrumb like texture. | 5. Add vanilla essence and milk and knead into soft dough. | 6. Grease your palms with a little oil, divide the dough into equal parts and shape them into cookies. | 7. Place the cookies on a baking tray and bake for 15-20 minutes. | 8. For milkshake, peel and chop banana and put it into a smoothie jar. | 9. Add milk, peanut butter and honey and blend till smooth. | 10. Remove the cookies from oven and set aside to cool. | 11. Once cooled, pack them in tiffin. | 12. Pour the milkshake into a mug flask.

title: Ragi Oats Dhokla
description: Savor the wholesome goodness of our Ragi Oats Dhokla, a nutritious twist on a traditional favorite, combining the richness of ragi and oats with the tangy flavors of dhokla.
ingredients: 1 teaspoon ginger-green chilli paste ½ teaspoon baking soda | 1 tablespoon oil + for greasing Green chutney to serveTempering | 2 tablespoons oil | 1 teaspoon mustard seeds | 2 teaspoons white sesame seeds 1- | 2 green chllies, slit 6- | 8 curry leaves | 1 teaspoon sugar Chopped fresh coriander leaves for garnish
method: 1. Combine together finger millet flour, oats powder, split skinless black gram flour, yogurt and 1¼ cups water and whisk till a smooth batter is formed. | 2. Cover and set aside to ferment for 6-8 hours. | 3. Add salt, cumin powder, red chilli powder, ginger-green chilli paste and baking soda and mix well. | 4. Brush a dhokla thali with some oil. | 5. Add 1 tablespoon oil into the batter and mix well. | 6. Pour this into the greased thali. | 7. Heat sufficient water in a steamer. | 8. Place the thali into the steamer basket. | 9. Cover and steam for 12-15 minutes. | 10. Remove the thali and allow to cool slightly. | 11. Scrape the sides of the thali and cut into squares. | 12. To make the tempering, heat oil in a pan. | 13. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter. | 14. Add white sesame seeds, green chillies, curry leaves and sugar and mix well. | 15. Add ¼ cup water and mix well. | 16. Pour this onto the dhokla. | 17. Garnish with coriander leaves. | 18. Serve with green chutney.

title: Rainbow Idli with Beet Ketchup
description: These rainbow idlis made with different coloured vegetables and nuts are served with ruby red ketchup made with beetroots
ingredients: 1 small carrot, peeled and grated ½ cup boiled corn kernels, crushed 10- | 2 blueberries ½ cup chopped nuts ½ cup green peas, lightly crushed | 2 tablespoons boiled beetroot pureeBeet ketchup | 1 cup boiled beetroot puree ¾ cup apple cider vinegar ½ cup brown sugar | 1 teaspoon coriander powder ¼ teaspoon | 5 spice powder ¼ teaspoon clove powder ½ teaspoon crushed black peppercorns
method: 1. Heat a steamer with water, grease the idli moulds with oil. | 2. Add salt to the idli batter and mix well, put a spoonful of idli batter in each mould. | 3. Top the idli batter individually with carrot, sweet corn, blueberry, nuts, green peas and beetroot puree. | 4. Place them in the steamer and steam for 10 minutes. | 5. To make ketchup, heat a non-stick pan, add beetroot puree, apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, coriander powder, 5 spice powder, clove powder, black peppercorns, tomato puree and salt and mix well, cook until it thickens. | 6. Remove the idlis from the steamer, let them cool and then demould. | 7. Wrap them in Freshwrapp and pack in the tiffin with beetroot ketchup.

title: Rainbow Rice Paper Roll
description: Healthy and easy to make – colourful vegetable strips wrapped in rice paper rolls and served with a tasty dipping sauce
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 4 rice paper rolls Dipping sauce ¼ cup sweet chilli sauce | 2 tablespoons lemon juice | 1 teaspoon fish sauce | 2 teaspoons soy sauce | 2 tablespoons rice vinegar | 2 teaspoons castor sugarFilling | 1 cup rice vermicelli, soaked in hot water | 2 tablespoons mint leaves | 1 cucumber, peeled and cut into thin strips | 1 red capsicum, cut into thin strips | 1 yellow capsicum, cut into thin strips | 1 small carrot, peeled and cut into thin strips | 0 Thai basil leaves | 2 tinned pineapple slices, cut into thin strips | 2 tablespoons roasted peanuts, crushed
method: 1. For dipping sauce, take sweet chilli sauce, lemon juice, fish sauce, soy sauce, rice vinegar and, castor sugar in a bowl and mix well. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Take some hot water in a shallow plate. | 4. Cut rice vermicelli into half and drain thoroughly. | 5. Dip the rice paper rolls in hot water for 5 seconds and spread them on the work top. | 6. Arrange some mint leaves at one end. | 7. Put rice vermicelli, cucumber, red capsicum, yellow capsicum, carrot, greems, basil leaves, pineapple and peanuts on top. | 8. Fold the edges of the rice roll from both side and tightly roll it from one end to another. | 9. Pack it in the tiffin box with dipping sauce.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Rajasthani Kaddu ki Sabzi
description: A simple and tasty Rajasthani preparation of red pumpkin or kaddu which is full of spices and flavours. This is
ingredients: 0 grams red pumpkin (kaddu), peeled and cut into | 1 inch cubes | 2 tablespoons oil | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds ½ teaspoon asafoetida (hing) | 2 small potatoes, cut into | 2 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves
method: 1. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add cumin seeds and let them change colour. | 2. Add asafoetida and and sauté for 1-2 minutes. | 3. Add potatoes, mix and sauté for 1 minute. | 4. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, and mix well. | 5. Add coriander powder and salt, mix and sauté for a few seconds. | 6. Add pumpkin, mix well. | 7. Sprinkle ¼ cup water, mix, cover and cook for 8-10 minutes or till the vegetables are ¾ cooked. | 8. Add dry mango powder and garam masala powder, mix and cook for a minute. | 9. Add coriander leaves and take the pan off the heat. | 10. Transfer the sabzi into a serving bowl and serve hot.

title: Rajasthani Raab
description: A lightly-flavoured porridge or a kanji made with maize flour that is a healthy meal in itself. Ideal for winters so do try this. An important tip, it is best to simmer it in earthen cookware for...
ingredients: 2 tablespoons ghee 1½ teaspoons cumin seeds 3- | 4 green chillies, slit | 1 tablespoon chopped fresh coriander leaves Roasted cumin powder for garnish Red chilli powder for garnish
method: 1. To make buttermilk, take yogurt in a large jug. | 2. Add 3 cups water and mix with a help of a wooden churner. | 3. Take maize flour in a large bowl, add 1 cup of the buttermilk and mix till a lump free batter is formed. | 4. Add the remaining buttermilk and mix. | 5. Heat a non-stick deep pan. | 6. Add the mixture and mix well. | 7. Stir occasionally and cook for 20-25 minutes on medium heat. | 8. Add salt and mix well. | 9. Heat ghee in a non-stick pan. | 10. Add cumin seeds and let them change colour. | 11. Add green chillies and mix well. | 12. Pour this into the prepared mixture and mix well. | 13. Add coriander leaves and mix till well combined. | 14. Transfer the raab into serving bowls, garnish with roasted cumin powder and red chilli powder.

title: Rawa Vegetable Pancakes
description: Enjoy a crispy and wholesome twist on pancakes with our Rawa Vegetable Pancakes, bursting with nutritious veggies and savory flavors in every bite.
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | ½ cup semolina (rawa) | 3 tablespoons yogurt ¼ cup grated carrots ¼ cup frozen green peas | 1 small capsicum, chopped
method: 1. In a bowl, mix together semolina, yogurt, and sufficient water. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Add carrots, green peas, capsicum, green chillies, sugar, crushed black peppercorns, red chilli powder, and salt. | 4. Mix well and set aside 10-15 minutes. | 5. Heat a non-stick tawa, pour a ladle of the prepared batter and spread it lightly to make a pancakes. | 6. Drizzle a little oil on the sides and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side. | 7. Serve hot with green chutney.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Reshmi Paneer
description: Paneer pieces cooked in spicy creamy onion tomato masala with capsicum. Savoring the silkiness of Reshmi Paneer – where succulence meets sophistication.
ingredients: 0 gms cottage cheese (paneer) | 3 tbsps oil ½ tsp cumin seeds | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 tsps ginger paste | 1 tsp coriander powder ½ tsp red chilli powder | 2 tbsps cashewnuts paste ¼ cup yogurt | 3 tbsps tomato puree | 1 large green capsicum, cut into thick strips | 1 large yellow capsicum, cut into thick strips | 1 large red capsicum, cut into thick strips | 2 tbsps crumbled mawa / khoya | 2 tbsps cream A few sprigs fresh coriander leaves
method: 1. Heat oil in a non-stick wok, add cumin seeds and and saute, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Cut cottage cheese into thick fingers. | 3. When soften, add ginger paste and paste and saute for 1 minute. | 4. Add turmeric powder, coriander powder and red chilli powder, mix and saute. | 5. Add cashewnuts paste and mix. | 6. Add yogurt and mix. | 7. Add tomato puree and mix. | 8. Add green, yellow and red capsicums, tomatoes and mix. | 9. Saute for1 minute. | 10. Add salt, cottage cheese fingers, garam masala powder, green cardamom powder, crumbled mawa and cream and mix. | 11. Simmer for 2 minutes. | 12. Chop coriander leaves and add. | 13. Transfer into a serving bowl and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Rice Gnocchi Aglio Olio
description: Rice flour gnocchi steamed to perfection and tossed in a herbed garlic-chili butter sauce, finished with parmesan and fresh parsley.
ingredients: Gnocchi | 1 tablespoon oil + for greasing and drizzling A pinch of turmeric powder Sauce | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 1/ | 2 tablespoons olive oil | 2 tablespoon red chilli flakes | 1 tablespoons chopped fresh mixed herbs (thyme, oregano, and rosemary) | 1 teaspoon butter | 1 tablespoon parmesan cheese powder Fresh parsley sprig to garnish Parmesan cheese shavings to garnish
method: 1. To make the gnocchi, Heat 1 cup water, salt, oil and turmeric powder in a deep pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 2. Let the mixture come to boil. | 3. Gradually add the rice flour and mix with a wooden ladle till it starts forming a dough. | 4. Transfer the dough onto the worktop, and knead the dough with a small greased stainless steel bowl. | 5. Once the dough is warm, knead with your hand till a soft dough is formed. | 6. Cover with a damp muslin cloth and let it rest for 15-20 minutes. | 7. To make gnocchi, grease your hands with a mixture of oil and water and divide the dough into small portions, around 5 grams each. | 8. Press each portion on the back of a fork and roll it. | 9. This is a gnocchi. | 10. Heat sufficient water in a steamer, arrange the gnocchi on the steamer basket, cover and steam for 8-10 minutes. | 11. Transfer them into a bowl and drizzle some oil over them. | 12. To make the sauce, heat olive oil in a non-stick pan. | 13. Add and red chilli flakes, mix and sauté for 1-2 minutes. | 14. Add chopped herbs and parsley, mix sauté for a few seconds. | 15. Add the gnocchi and toss them well. | 16. Add salt and crushed black peppercorns and toss again. | 17. Continue to cook for 2-3 minutes. | 18. Add butter and parmesan cheese powder and mix gently till well combined. | 19. Transfer onto a serving plate, garnish with parsley sprig and parmesan cheese shavings. | 20. Serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Rice Kurkure
description: Leftover rice transforms into this light, spicy and chatpata snack that’s popular among kids. You can serve this for teatime too. This is
ingredients: 1 cup leftover cooked rice ¼ cup gram flour (besan) 2½ tablespoons corn flour | 1 teaspoon chaat masala ¼ teaspoon citric acid ¼ teaspoon red chilli powder
method: 1. Put cooked rice in a mixer jar, add ½ cup water, and grind to a fine paste. | 2. Transfer into a large bowl. | 3. Add gram flour, corn flour, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, baking soda, and salt. | 4. Add ¼ cup water and whisk till a smooth batter is formed. | 5. Transfer the mixture into a squeezy bottle. | 6. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 7. Squeeze out small strips of the mixture into the hot oil and deep fry till golden brown and crisp. | 8. Drain on an absorbent paper. | 9. Take chaat masala in a wide shallow bowl. | 10. Add citric acid, red chilli powder and mix well. | 11. Add the fried rice sticks and gently toss.

title: Roasted Pumpkin Soup
description: Red pumpkin is a very healthy vegetable being rich in vitamins, minerals and antioxidants. And all these nutrients are so good for growing children as also adults
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 0 hour Serve | 0 grams red pumpkin, seeded and cut into thick slices | 8 black peppercorns 2- | 3 star anise | 2 inches leek, chopped | 2 inches cinnamon | 2 teaspoons smoked paprika | 2 teaspoons brown sugar | 1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar 4- | 5 sprigs fresh thyme 2- | 3 sprigs fresh rosemary 2- | 3 brown bread slices | 3 cups vegetable stock | 1 tablespoon fresh cream Pumpkin seeds to garnish
method: 1. Preheat oven to 160º C. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Grease a baking tray with 2 tbsps of olive oil, spread the pumpkin slices on it, add sea salt, black peppercorns, star anise, leeks, and cinnamon. | 4. Sprinkle smoked paprika on top, add brown sugar, balsamic vinegar, thyme and rosemary. | 5. Place the tray in the preheated oven and bake for 1 hour. | 6. Trim the edges of the brown bread slices and cut into small cubes. | 7. Spread the bread cubes on another baking tray, drizzle remaining olive oil, toss well and place the tray in the preheated oven and toast for 10 minutes. | 8. Insert a toothpick through the pumpkin slices to check if they are done. | 9. Remove the tray from the oven, peel the pumpkin and chop roughly. | 10. Transfer the roasted pumpkin into a mixer jar, add 1 cup vegetable stock and blend into a smooth puree. | 11. Heat a deep non-stick pan, add the pumpkin puree, remaining stock and mix well. | 12. Bring the mixture to a boil. | 13. Add fresh cream and mix well and remove the pan from heat. | 14. Pour the soup into a jug and then gently into a thermos flask. | 15. Garnish with some pumpkin seeds and cover the flask. | 16. Pack the baked croutons in an airtight container to serve with soup.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Sabudana Papad
description: A crispy and light Indian snack made from tapioca pearls (sabudana), seasoned with spices, and sun-dried to perfection.
ingredients: 1 teaspoon cumin seeds Oil to deep fry
method: 1. Transfer the sago in bowl and wash it thoroughly. | 2. Add 1 cup water and soak for 6-8 hours. | 3. Transfer in a nonstick pan and add salt, cumin seeds and 5 cups water. | 4. Cook on medium heat till sago mixture is transparent and the sago is cooked. | 5. Spread spoonful of mixture on a plastic sheet and spread lightly. | 6. Sundry for 2 days. | 7. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai and deep fry the papads till crisp. | 8. Drain on absorbent paper and serve.

title: Sabudana Vada
description: Crispy, crunchy and 'oh so good' - Sabudana Vadas are an all-time favourite snack for everyone.
ingredients: 1½ sago (sabudana), soaked Chutney ½ cup scrapped fresh coconut ½ cup fresh coriander leaves | 1 green chilli ½ teaspoon cumin seeds | 3 medium potatoes, boiled, peeled and mashed | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds ¾ cup roasted and crushed peanuts ½ lemon Oil for greasing + for deep frying
method: 1. To make the chutney, put coconut in a mixer jar, add coriander leaves, green chilli, cumin seeds, roasted peanuts, salt and ¼ cup water and grind to a fine paste. | 2. To make sweetened yogurt, take yogurt in a bowl. | 3. Add powdered sugar and salt and mix well. | 4. To make the vada mixture, take soaked sago in a large plate. | 5. Add potatoes, ginger-green chilli paste, salt, cumin seeds, peanuts and squeeze the juice of lemon and mix till well combined. | 6. Grease your palms with oil, take a portion of the vada mixture, shape it into a ball and slightly flatten it to shape like a vada. | 7. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 8. Gently slide in the vades into the hot oil and deep fry till golden brown and crisp. | 9. Drain on an absorbent paper. | 10. Serve hot with prepared chutney and sweetened yogurt.

title: Saeb Makhane ki Kheer
description: Makhana sautéed in ghee enhances the taste and falvour of this kheer
ingredients: 2 medium Medium apples (saeb), grated | 1 cup Lotus seeds (makhana) 1/ | 4 cup Ghee 1/ | 4 cup Jaggery, grated 1/ | 4 teaspoon Green cardamom powder 1/ | 4 cup Mixed nuts, chopped | 0 millilitres Milk | 1 cup Khotya (mawa), grated to garnish Apple fans
method: 1. Heat ghee in a non-stick pan, add makhana and sauté till crisp. | 2. Drain and transfer into a bowl and set aside to cool. | 3. Add apples to the ghee remaining in the pan and sauté for 7-8 minutes. | 4. Transfer makhana into a grinder jar and grind to a fine powder. | 5. Add jaggery to the apples and mix till it melts. | 6. Add green cardamom powder and mix well. | 7. Transfer the mixture into another bowl. | 8. Add nuts to the same pan and roast till lightly browned. | 9. Add milk and bring to a boil. | 10. Add makhana powder, mix well and cook till the mixture thickens. | 11. Add khoya and mix till it melts. | 12. Switch off heat, add prepared apple mixture and mix till well combined. | 13. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with apple fans and serve hot.

title: Sambousek
description: A delightful Middle Eastern pastry filled with with spicy chenna and spinach mixture, inviting you to savor its crispy exterior and flavorful fillings in every bite.
ingredients: 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 0 grams readymade whole wheat flour (atta) dough | 1 teaspoon carom seeds (ajwain) | 1 tablespoon finely chopped ginger | 1 cup grated cottage cheese (chhena) | 1 teaspoon garam masala powder ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder
method: 1. Take carom seeds, ginger, cottage cheese, black salt, dried mango powder, garam masala powder, turmeric powder and spinach in a bowl and mix well. | 2. Warm a pinch of hing in a little hot oil before adding the main ingredients. | 3. Add salt and mix well. | 4. Divide the dough into equal portions and roll each portion into a disc. | 5. Spread a spoonful of cottage cheese-spinach mixture on each disc and roll into a cylinder. | 6. Roll into a spiral, dust some flour and roll out into medium size discs. | 7. Heat a non-stick tawa. | 8. Place the discs on it, one at a time, and cook, basting with oil and turning sides, till evenly browned on both sides. | 9. Cut each disc into 4 pieces, arrange them on a serving platter and serve with your choice of pickle.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Samosa Patti
description: Samosa patti are broad strips made of wheat or refined flour which serve as an outer covering for the stuffing of samosas
ingredients: 1 cup refined flour (maida) + for dusting
method: 1. Heat oil in a small pan. | 2. Take refined flour in a large bowl. | 3. Add salt and the hot oil and mix till the mixture comes together. | 4. Add ¼ cup water little by little and knead till a soft dough is formed. | 5. Cover with a damp muslin cloth and set aside to rest for 30 minutes. | 6. Divide the dough into 6 equal portions. | 7. Shape each portion into a ball. | 8. Place a dough ball on a worktop and dust with some refined flour. | 9. Roll into a small disc. | 10. Apply some oil and generously dust some refined flour over it. | 11. Place this on a plate. | 12. Dust the worktop with some refined flour again and place another dough ball on it. | 13. Roll it into a small disc. | 14. Repeat this process one more time with another dough ball. | 15. Place the first disc over the second one with the flour side facing each other. | 16. Similarly, place the third disc with the flour side facing down over the sandwiched discs. | 17. Dust some flour on top and roll it into a large thin disc. | 18. Heat a non-stick tawa. | 19. Place the rolled out discs and cook on low heat for a few minutes while rotating the tawa to ensure the discs are uniformly semi-cooked. | 20. Flip and cook the other side for a few minutes as well. | 21. Carefully separate the layers and place a worktop. | 22. Trim the sides and cut into 24cm x 8cm sized thick strips. | 23. Carefully place them in a zip lock bag and keep them in the freezer. | 24. Use as required.

title: Sangam Vada
description: The popular vada pav made differently – potato mixture sandwiched in pavs, dipped in batter and deep-fried
ingredients: 3 medium potatoes, boiled, peeled and mashed | 4 pavs | 3 green chillies, stemmed and broken | 2 tbsps oil + to deep fry | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 8 curry leaves, shredded | 2 tbsps chopped fresh coriander leaves + to sprinkle 1¼ cups gram flour ¼ cup rice flour
method: 1. Transfer green chillies, and ginger in a mixer jar and grind coarsely without adding any water. | 2. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, then add a pinch of hing. | 3. Add curry leaves and sauté for a few seconds. | 4. Add the prepared paste and sauté for 2 minutes on medium heat. | 5. Add ½ tsp turmeric powder, salt and mix well. | 6. Cook for 30 seconds. | 7. Add the boiled potatoes and mix well. | 8. Sprinkle 2 tbsps water and cook for 2-3 minutes or till the mixture is heated thoroughly. | 9. Add coriander leaves and mix well. | 10. Transfer in a bowl and set aside to cool down. | 11. For the batter, take gram flour in a bowl. | 12. Add rice flour, red chilli powder, remaining turmeric powder, salt, baking soda and mix well. | 13. Add 1¼ cups water and mix well to form a smooth batter. | 14. Slit the pavs without cutting through. | 15. Apply a generous portion of the potato mixture on lower half and close it. | 16. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 17. Dip the pav pieces in the gram flour batter and slide into the hot oil. | 18. Deep fry till golden brown and crisp. | 19. Drain on absorbent paper. | 20. Cut the fried pav sandwich into 4 pieces and transfer on a serving plate. | 21. Drizzle date and tamarind chutney and green chutney on top. | 22. Sprinkle sev and coriander leaves. | 23. Serve immediately.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Sattu Baingan Masala
description: Brinjals stuffed with spicy sattu mixture, tossed with Indian spices and cooked to perfection.
ingredients: 1 1/ | 2 cups sattu | 8 medium brinjals | 1 teaspoon turmeric powder | 2 green chillies, finely chopped | 1 tablespoon ginger, finely chopped | 3 teaspoons red chilli powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon asafoetida | 2 teaspoons fennel (saunf) powder | 2 1/ | 2 teaspoons dried mango powder (amchur) | 2 teaspoons coriander powder | 3 teaspoons mustard oil Fresh coriander sprigs to garnish
method: 1. Take sufficient water in a bowl, add ½ tsp turmeric powder and mix well. | 2. Slit brinjals without cutting through and immerse in turmeric water. | 3. To prepare the stuffing, take sattu in another bowl, add green chillies, ginger, 2 tsps red chilli powder, remaining turmeric powder, ¼ tsp asafoetida, fennel powder, 1½ tsps dried mango powder, coriander powder, cumin seeds, salt and 6 tsps mustard oil and mix well. | 4. Heat 5 tsps mustard oil in a stainless steel pan till it begins to smoke. | 5. Stuff the brinjals with the sattu mixture, place them in the hot pan, cover and shallow fry on low heat, stirring occasionally, till golden brown on all sides. | 6. Heat another non-stick pan, add remaining mixture of sattu and dry roast till colour darkens. | 7. Spread this on a serving plate. | 8. Heat remaining mustard oil in the same pan, add remaining asafoetida, brinjals, remaining red chilli powder, remaining dried mango powder and mix lightly. | 9. Cook for 1-2 minutes. | 10. Place the brinjals over the sattu mixture spread on the serving plate. | 11. Garnish with coriander sprigs and serve hot.

title: Sattu Pancakes
description: Enjoy a hearty breakfast with sattu pancakes, combining the wholesome goodness of sattu and whole wheat flour for a delightful morning treat.
ingredients: 1 cup sattu ½ cup yogurt ½ teaspoon baking soda | 4 tablespoons butter + as required ½ cup jaggery powder ½ cup whole wheat flour | 1 teaspoon baking powder A pinch of salt ¼ teaspoon green cardamom powder ½ teaspoon vanilla essence | 1 cup milk Banana slices as required Chopped almonds as required Chopped pistachios as required Maple syrup to serve
method: 1. Take yogurt in a bowl. | 2. Add baking soda and mix well. | 3. Set aside. | 4. Heat butter in a deep nonstick pan. | 5. Add jaggery powder and cook till butter melts. | 6. Allow to cool slightly. | 7. Sift sattu, whole wheat flour, baking powder and salt in a bowl. | 8. Make a well in the centre, add yogurt mixture, butter -jaggery mixture, green cardamom powder, vanilla essence, milk and whisk well. | 9. Heat a nonstick tawa. | 10. Grease with some butter, spread a ladleful of batter. | 11. Cover and cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. | 12. Flip and cook on other side for 2 minutes. | 13. Pile up the pancakes on serving plate. | 14. Arrange banana slices on top. | 15. Sprinkle almonds, pistachios on top. | 16. Place some butter cubes in the centre. | 17. Serve with maple syrup.

title: Shakkarkand Tuk Tuk
description: Tuk is a popular Sindhi side dish that is generally served with Sindhi kadhi and chawal. Here sweet potatoes replace the usual potatoes
ingredients: 1 teaspoon red chili powder | 2 teaspoons coriander powder ½ teaspoon dried mango (amchur) powder | 1 tablespoon chopped fresh coriander leaves Tomato ketchup to serve Green chutney to serve
method: 1. Heat sufficient oil in a deep pan. | 2. Cut the sweet potato into thick slices. | 3. Flatten the slices between your palms by pressing a little and deep-fry in hot oil till golden brown and crisp. | 4. Drain on absorbent paper. | 5. To prepare the spice mix, mix together salt, red chilli powder, coriander powder, amchur powder and coriander leaves in a bowl. | 6. While tuk is still hot, sprinkle spice mix over them and toss well. | 7. Insert a toothpick in each tuk and arrange them on a serving plate. | 8. Serve hot with tomato ketchup and green chutney.

title: Shakkarkandi Matar Masala
description: Super food sweet potatoes mixed with green peas and some masalas to make a tasty dish. Serve this with choice of your bread.
ingredients: 4 medium sweet potatoes (shakkerkand), scrubbed clean and cut into cubes 1/ | 4 cup green peas (matar) | 4 tablespoons sunflower oil | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds | 1 tablespoon ginger, finely chopped 2- | 3 green chillies, finely chopped | 1 1/ | 2 tablespoons coriander powder | 1 teaspoon cumin powder | 1 teaspoon red chilli powder 1/ | 2 teaspoon turmeric powder | 2 teaspoons dried mango powder (amchur) | 2 Fresh coriander sprigs + to garnish Fresh red chilli to garnish
method: 1. Heat sunflower oil in a non-stick pan, add cumin seeds, ginger and green chillies, stir and sauté till fragrant. | 2. Add sweet potatoes, toss and cook for 2-3 minutes. | 3. Add coriander powder, cumin powder, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, dried mango powder, garam masala powder and salt, mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes. | 4. Add green peas and mix. | 5. Add water as required, cover and cook for 6-8 minutes. | 6. Finely chop coriander leaves, add to the pan and mix. | 7. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprigs and red chilli and serve hot.

title: Sheer Payara
description: Simple fudge made with milk powder and some dried fruits
ingredients: 1 cup Sugar | 1 1/ | 2 cups Milk powder to grease Butter | 1 tablespoon Rose water | 1 teaspoon Green cardamom powder 1/ | 4 cup Walnuts and almonds, chopped to garnish Pistachios, chopped | 1 tablespoon Sultanas, chopped
method: 1. Grease a burfi tray with ghee, line with butter paper. | 2. Heat a deep-pan, add sugar and water and bring the mixture to a boil. | 3. Simmer, stirring continuously, till the syrup reaches one string consistency. | 4. Remove the pan from heat and add rose water, green cardamom powder, walnuts, almonds and milk powder and mix well. | 5. Pour the mixture into the greased tray and spread evenly. | 6. Sprinkle pistachios and sultanas and set aside to cool for about an hour. | 7. When the fudge cools, cut into pieces of your preferred shape, remove from tray and serve.

title: Shegaon Kachori
description: A popular street food, these kachoris were first made in Shegaon and therefore the name. Eat one and you will want more and more
ingredients: 1½ cups refined flour (maida) Salt to taste | 3 tablespoons oil + for greasing + for deep frying Fried green chillies to serve Masala stuffing 1½ tablespoons fennel seeds (saunf) | 1 tablespoon coriander seeds | 1 teaspoon cumin seeds | 1 cup gram flour (besan) | 2 tablespoons oil 1½ ginger-green chilli paste
method: 1. Take refined flour in a large bowl. | 2. Add salt and oil and mix till the mixture resembles bread crumb consistency. | 3. Add ½ cup water and knead to a stiff dough. | 4. Cover with a damp muslin cloth and set aside for 10-15 minutes. | 5. To make the masala stuffing, dry roast fennel seeds, coriander seeds and cumin seeds till fragrant. | 6. Transfer into a mortar and coarsely crush it with a pestle. | 7. Heat oil in a non-stick pan. | 8. Add ginger-green chilli paste and mix well. | 9. Sauté for 1-2 minutes. | 10. Add red chilli powder, garam masala powder, turmeric powder and salt and sauté for a few seconds. | 11. Add gram flour and roast on low heat for 4-5 minutes. | 12. Add the crushed mixture and mix well. | 13. Set aside to cool completely. | 14. Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. | 15. To make the kachoris, grease the worktop with some oil. | 16. Take a medium sized portion of the dough and shape it into a ball. | 17. Place it on the greased worktop and roll it into a small disc. | 18. Add a portion of the masala stuffing and bring the edges to the centre and press to seal. | 19. Gently press and lightly roll into a small disc. | 20. Gently slide in the kachoris a few at a time and deep fry on medium heat till they turn golden, crisp and lightly puffed. | 21. Serve with fried green chillies.

title: Sitaphal Basundi
description: Sitaphal adds its unique flavour and texture to this basundi
ingredients: 2 medium Custard apples (sitaphal) | 1 litres Milk 3/ | 4 cup Condensed milk | 2 tablespoons Cashewnuts, chopped | 2 tablespoons Almonds, chopped 1/ | 2 teaspoon Green cardamom powder a few strands Saffron water, soaked in water to brush Honey 1/ | 2 cup Pistachios, crushed
method: 1. Boil milk in a deep non-stick pan, stirring frequently, till it is reduced to half. | 2. Keep scraping the sides of the pan and mixing with milk. | 3. Meanwhile break open the custard apples and scoop the seeds and mash through a strainer, to separate the flesh from the seeds, into a bowl. | 4. Some pieces may not get separated from the seeds so collect these pieces in another bowl and remove their seeds. | 5. Add condensed milk, cashewnuts, almonds and green cardamom powder to the reduced milk and mix well. | 6. Cook on low heat for 5-6 minutes or till it thickens a little more. | 7. Scrape the sides occasionally. | 8. Put a ladle of the milk mixture into a bowl, add saffron along with water and mix well. | 9. Refrigerate for 40-50 minutes. | 10. Add the remaining milk mixture to the custard apple pulp. | 11. Add 2 tbsps of chunky pieces. | 12. Mix well. | 13. Refrigerate it for 40-50 minutes. | 14. Brush the rims of serving glasses with honey. | 15. Take crushed pistachios in a plate and roll the rims of the and roll over it. | 16. Fill each serving glass till ¾ with plain sitafal basundi and add 2 spoons of saffron basundi on top. | 17. Serve chilled.

title: Sofiyani Sherbat
description: Sofiyani Sherbat is best enjoyed cold, making it a perfect beverage to serve during festive gatherings or as a refreshing drink on a warm day.
ingredients: 2 tbsps fennel seeds (saunf) | 4 tsps cumin seeds | 4 tbsps coriander seeds | 2 cups sugar Ice cubes as required | 6 ½ tsps black salt | 6 ½ lemons juice Mint sprigs for garnish
method: 1. Heat a non-stick deep pan, add saunf, cumin seeds and coriander seeds. | 2. Dry roast on low heat for 2-3 minutes till saunf is fragrant. | 3. Transfer roasted mixture in a blender. | 4. Add sugar and blend to a fine powder. | 5. Transfer the blended mixture in a bowl. | 6. Add 1 cup water, ice cubes as required, black salt and lemon juice. | 7. Mix well. | 8. Remove some ice cubes and add in a serving glass. | 9. Strain the sherbet in another bowl and add ice cubes. | 10. Transfer mixture in the serving glass and garnish with mint sprigs. | 11. Serve cold.

title: Soya Aloo Tahiri Pulao
description: This is recipe is so simple yet so very tasty and nutritious too
ingredients: 2 cup Soya chunks, soaked and boiled | 1 large Potato (aloo), peeled | 1 1/ | 2 cups Rice, soaked for | 0 minutes and drained | 2 tablespoons Oil 10- | 2 Black peppercorns | 1 Bay leaf | 2 medium Tomatoes to garnish Fresh coriander sprig
method: 1. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add peppercorns and bay leaf and sauté till fragrant. | 2. Cut potatoes into cubes, add and sauté till they turn brown. | 3. Roughly chop tomatoes. | 4. Add rice to the pan, mix and cook for 4-5 minutes. | 5. Add soya chunks and 3 cups water and stir. | 6. Add tomatoes and mix. | 7. Cover and cook for 10-15 minutes or till the rice is fully done, stirring occasionally. | 8. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot.

title: Soya Keema Sheperd's Pie
description: Soya granules contain immunity building nutrients, protein, iron and zinc besides being rich in dietary fiber. They are an excellent substitute for mutton keema
ingredients: ½ cup soya granules (keema), soaked and drained | 1 medium tomato, chopped 4- | 1 inch ginger, chopped ½ cup chopped fresh coriander leaves | 2 green chillies | 2 tablespoons oil | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 1 teaspoon fennel seeds | 1 inch cinnamon stick | 1 bay leaf 3- | 4 black peppercorns 3- | 4 cloves ½ teaspoon turmeric powder | 2 teaspoons red chilli powder | 2 large potatoes, boiled | 2 tablespoons butter | 2 tablespoons fresh cream ½ teaspoon chaat masala | 4 tablespoons fresh breadcrumbs
method: 1. Put tomato, ginger, fresh coriander, green chillies and ¼ cup water and blend into a coarse paste. | 2. Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add fennel seeds, cinnamon, bay leaf, black peppercorn and cloves and sauté until fragrant, then add a pinch of hing. | 3. Add half the prepared paste and sauté well. | 4. Add soya granules and mix well. | 5. Add ½ cup of water and combine to mix, add ¼ teaspoon turmeric powder, red chilli powder, garam masala powder and mix well. | 6. Add green peas, salt and mix well, cook for 2 minutes and remove the pan from heat. | 7. Preheat the oven to 200º C. | 8.
