description: An extremely popular dish, Vegetable Makhani is made with the classic rich and creamy gravy. Served with any Indian bread, this dish is finger-licking good
ingredients: 0 grams baby potatoes, boiled and peeled | 0 grams cottage cheese (paneer) | 0 fresh button mushrooms, halved 15- | 0 small Broccoli florets, blanced | 2 inches ginger, roughly chopped 10- | 5 green cardamoms A few maceblades | 2 teaspoons red chilli powder (deghi mirch powder) 1/ | 2 teaspoon crushed red chillies A few fresh coriander stems | 4 tablespoons oil 1/ | 1 pinch hing (asafoetida) | 2 teaspoon cumin seeds 1/ | 2 teaspoon garam masala powder | 1 teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi), roasted and powdered | 2 tablespoons honey | 1 tablespoon butter | 2 tablespoons fresh cream Ginger, cut into thin strips for garnish
method: 1. Heat a deep non-stick pan, add tomatoes, salt, ginger, green cardamoms, mace blades, red chilli powder, crushed red chillies and 3 cups water and mix. | 2. Add fresh coriander stems, cover and cook. | 3. Heat 2 tbsps oil in a non-stick wok, add baby potatoes and salt and let them cook till light golden, then add a pinch of hing. | 4. Cut cottage cheese into medium cubes and put them in a bowl. | 5. Add the baby potatoes. | 6. Add button mushrooms to the same wok and toss till they soften. | 7. Transfer them into same bowl. | 8. The tomatoes are fully done. | 9. Strain the liquid into a bowl. | 10. Transfer the tomatoes into a mixer jar, cool and grind into a smooth puree. | 11. Heat 2 tbsps oil in the same wok, add cumin seeds and let them change colour. | 12. Add the strained liquid and cook till it is reduced. | 13. Strained the tomato puree into the wok, pressing the residue in the strainer to extract all the flavours. | 14. Mix well. | 15. Add the vegetables and cottage cheese and mix. | 16. Add garam masala powder and dried fenugreek leaf powder and mix well. | 17. Cook till the gravy thickens. | 18. Add honey and mix well. | 19. Take the broccoli florets in a serving and heat in the microwave. | 20. Add butter and fresh cream to the wok and mix well. | 21. Pour this over the broccoli. | 22. Garnish with a few more broccoli florets and ginger strips and serve hot.
adaptations: Removed onion/garlic (and related alliums). Added hing (asafoetida). Egg-free.

title: Walnut Phirni in Chocolate Cups
description: Chocolate and walnuts make a great combination, and when walnut phirni is served in chocolate cups it is divine
ingredients: 2 cup Walnuts | 2 cups Dark chocolate, melted | 1 litres Full fat milk | 3 tablespoons Rice, soaked for | 0 minutes and drained 3/ | 4 cup Sugar 1/ | 4 teaspoon Green cardamom powder a few strands Saffron to garnish Chocolate cigars to garnish Fresh mint sprigs
method: 1. To prepare chocolate cups, fill silicon moulds till ¾ full with melted dark chocolate. | 2. Swirl to coat all sides and drain extra chocolate. | 3. Keep them in the refrigerator for 5-10 minutes or till set. | 4. Put walnuts in a grinder jar and grind coarsely. | 5. Boil milk in a deep non-stick pan. | 6. Transfer walnuts into a bowl. | 7. Put drained rice in the same grind jar and grind coarsely. | 8. Add both rice and walnuts to the boiling milk and cook, stirring continuously till the rice gets cooked and the mixture thickens. | 9. Scrape the sides occasionally and mix well. | 10. Stir continuously so that no lumps are formed. | 11. Add sugar, green cardamom powder and saffron strands, mix and cook for 2-3 minutes on low heat. | 12. Remove from heat and let it cool down to room temperature. | 13. Demould chocolate cups from the silicon moulds and place on a serving platter. | 14. Fill up each chocolate cups with phirni. | 15. Garnish with chocolate cigars and mint sprig. | 16. Serve immediately.

title: Zarda Boondi Biryani
description: Sweet biryani made with sweet boondi, basmati rice and plenty of nuts
ingredients: 2 grams saffron strands | 1 cup Sweet boondi | 3 cups Basmati rice, cooked | 2 tablespoons Ghee | 2 tablespoons Chironjee / charoli 2- | 3 tablespoons Cashewnuts, chopped | 2 tablespoons Raisins 1/ | 4 Dried coconut, sliced | 1 1/ | 2 cup Milk 1/ | 4 cup Castor sugar 1/ | 4 teaspoon Cinnamon powder 15- | 0 Walnuts 1/ | 2 cup Khoya | 1 + to garnish Fresh mint sprig 1/ | 2 Lemon to garnish Orange to garnish Rabdi
method: 1. Heat ghee in a deep stainless steel handi, add chironji, cashewnuts, raisins and dried coconut and sauté on medium heat for 1-2 minutes. | 2. Add milk, boondi, castor sugar, saffron and ½ tsp cinnamon powder, mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally. | 3. After the mixture comes to a boil, add walnuts and mix well. | 4. Cook for 1-2 minutes, add ¼ cup khoya, cooked basmati rice, mint leaves and remaining khoya. | 5. Squeeze lemon juice into the handi and add remaining cinnamon powder, mix, cover and cook for 4-5 minutes on low heat. | 6. Cut orange into round slices and halve them. | 7. Transfer the biryani onto a serving platter, garnish with rabdi, orange slices and a mint sprig and serve.
